Job Description: Front-line customer service professional responsible for processing daily financial transactions and addressing basic customer inquiries.
Key Responsibilities:
• Process deposits, withdrawals, and loan payments.
• Balance a cash drawer daily.
• Answer account inquiries and identify referral opportunities.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma.
• Strong cash handling, accuracy, and customer service skills.

Job Description: A senior teller who processes transactions while also providing oversight, coaching, and vault management support to the teller line.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform all teller duties.
• Manage and balance the bank vault.
• Coach junior tellers and handle escalated customer issues.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 2+ years of teller experience.
• Leadership potential, advanced problem-solving, and cash vault management skills.

Job Description: A sales and service professional in a branch responsible for opening new accounts and selling bank products like loans and credit cards.
Key Responsibilities:
• Open new checking/savings accounts and CDs.
• Consult with customers to identify needs.
• Process consumer loan and credit card applications.
• Meet established sales and referral goals.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree preferred.
• Strong sales, communication, and relationship-building skills.

Job Description: A more advanced Personal Banker role focused on developing deeper, long-term relationships with a portfolio of branch clients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Proactively manage and grow a portfolio of consumer clients.
• Provide holistic financial advice.
• Cross-sell a full range of bank products (loans, investments, etc.).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Business.
• Proven sales record, financial acumen, and CRM skills.

Job Description: A specialized banker, often located within a branch, who focuses exclusively on the needs of small business clients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Open new business checking accounts.
• Originate small business loans (e.g., SBA loans, lines of credit).
• Sell treasury/cash management services to small businesses.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Business/Finance.
• B2B sales skills and a strong understanding of business financial statements.

Job Description: Guides clients through the mortgage application process, from pre-approval to closing, helping them secure financing for a home purchase.
Key Responsibilities:
• Advise clients on various mortgage products.
• Collect and verify all required financial documents.
• Manage a pipeline of loan applications.
• Liaise with underwriters and processors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Must hold an NMLS license.
• Strong sales, organization, and communication skills.

Job Description: Supports the Branch Manager in all operational and sales functions, acting as the manager-on-duty in their absence.
Key Responsibilities:
• Supervise the teller line and personal banking team.
• Handle complex operational duties and audit procedures.
• Coach staff to meet sales goals.
• Resolve escalated customer complaints.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 3-5+ years of banking experience.
• Proven leadership, sales coaching, and operational skills.

Job Description: Leads a single bank branch, accountable for its overall profitability, operational integrity, sales performance, and team development.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage all branch staff (hiring, training, scheduling).
• Develop and execute sales strategies to meet branch goals.
• Ensure compliance with all banking regulations and security protocols.
• Represent the bank in the local community.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• 5+ years of banking management.
• Strong leadership, sales management, and financial acumen.

Job Description: A senior leader responsible for the performance and operations of an entire geographic region, managing multiple Branch Managers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the P&L for a defined region.
• Set regional sales and performance targets.
• Coach and develop a team of Branch Managers.
• Ensure operational consistency and compliance across all branches.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• 7-10+ years of senior retail bank management experience.

Job Description: The primary administrative support role for a team of Financial Advisors, handling all client service and operational tasks.
Key Responsibilities:
• Handle client requests (e.g., money movement, paperwork).
• Open new investment accounts and prepare client review materials.
• Assist Financial Advisors with scheduling and administration.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Exceptional organization and client service skills.
• (Often requires Series 7/66 licenses).

Job Description: A junior, often salaried or trainee-level advisor who supports a senior advisory team by conducting research and assisting with financial plans.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist in building financial models and plans for clients.
• Conduct investment research and portfolio analysis.
• Sit in on client meetings and handle follow-up tasks.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance.
• Must pass SIE, Series 7, and Series 66.
• Strong analytical and Excel skills.

Job Description: A licensed professional who builds a book of clients, providing holistic financial planning and investment management services.
Key Responsibilities:
• Prospect for and acquire new high-net-worth (HNW) clients.
• Create comprehensive financial plans (retirement, estate, insurance).
• Build and manage client investment portfolios.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• CFP certification highly preferred.
• Strong sales, networking, and portfolio management skills.

Job Description: Services the needs of ultra-high-net-worth (UHNW) clients, providing complex credit solutions (e.g., asset-backed loans) in addition to investments.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the single point of contact for UHNW clients.
• Provide customized credit and lending solutions.
• Coordinate with trust, investment, and estate planning specialists.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• Series 7/66.
• Expertise in complex credit products and HNW client management.

Job Description: An investment specialist responsible for making the final buy/sell decisions for client portfolios, either on a discretionary basis or for a bank's model portfolio.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct macroeconomic and security-specific research.
• Construct and rebalance investment portfolios to match risk tolerance.
• Monitor market activity and execute trades.
• Communicate investment strategy to clients and advisors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Master's in Finance or MBA.
• CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) designation is the standard.

Job Description: A fiduciary who administers trusts and estates according to the grantor's wishes, managing assets on behalf of beneficiaries.
Key Responsibilities:
• Ensure compliance with all legal terms of a trust document.
• Manage trust assets, investments, and real estate.
• Handle distributions to beneficiaries and all tax filings for the trust.
• Communicate regularly with beneficiaries and their legal counsel.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • Bachelor's degree; JD (law degree) or CTFA (Certified Trust and Fiduciary Advisor) cert is standard.

Job Description: A specialist who creates comprehensive, long-term financial plans for clients, often without directly managing the investments.
Key Responsibilities:
• Gather and analyze all client financial data (income, assets, insurance).
• Model scenarios for retirement, college savings, and estate planning.
• Prepare and present a formal financial plan with actionable recommendations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• CFP (Certified Financial Planner) certification is required.

Job Description: An analyst who supports Relationship Managers by performing the financial "underwriting" on loan requests from business clients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze company financial statements (balance sheet, P&L, cash flow).
• Perform ratio analysis, sensitivity analysis, and cash flow modeling.
• Prepare a detailed credit memorandum (write-up) recommending approval or denial.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Accounting or Finance.
• Strong analytical and Excel skills; formal credit training.

Job Description: A risk-assessment professional (often a senior Credit Analyst) who has the authority to approve or deny a business loan application.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform final due diligence on loan applications.
• Assess all forms of risk (credit, collateral, market).
• Approve, deny, or restructure loan terms and covenants.
• Hold the final "credit authority" for the bank.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Accounting.
• 5+ years of credit analysis experience and delegated lending authority.

Job Description: The primary sales and client-facing role in commercial banking. Manages a portfolio of business clients and is responsible for lending and cross-selling all bank services.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the primary advisor and contact for a portfolio of business clients.
• Originate new commercial loans (C&I loans, CRE loans).
• Cross-sell other bank products (e.g., Treasury Management, Wealth Management).
• Negotiate loan terms, pricing, and covenants.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA in Finance.
• Formal credit training and strong B2B sales skills.

Job Description: A specialist who sells "cash management" products to business clients, helping them manage their operating cash flow.
Key Responsibilities:
• Sell products like ACH/wire services, remote deposit capture, and fraud protection (positive pay).
• Analyze a company's cash conversion cycle to propose solutions.
• Conduct product demos and manage implementation.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Business.
• CTP (Certified Treasury Professional) cert is a plus.
• Strong technical sales skills.

Job Description: An operations and analytical role that supports the Treasury Sales Officers by implementing products and analyzing client service usage.
Key Responsibilities:
• Implement new treasury management services for clients.
• Provide technical support and troubleshooting for cash management products.
• Analyze product pricing and client profitability.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance.
• Technical aptitude, project management, and analytical skills.

Job Description: A lending expert who specializes in originating and processing loans that are backed by the Small Business Administration (SBA).
Key Responsibilities:
• Structure loans according to complex SBA guidelines.
• Collect and package all required SBA documentation.
• Work with underwriters to ensure full compliance with federal programs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Deep expertise in all SBA lending programs (e.g., 7(a), 504).

Job Description: A Relationship Manager who specializes exclusively in lending for commercial property (e.g., office buildings, apartments, retail centers).
Key Responsibilities:
• Originate loans for property acquisition, development, or construction.
• Analyze property cash flows (Net Operating Income), rent rolls, and appraisals.
• Manage a portfolio of CRE loans and developer relationships.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance or Real Estate.
• Expertise in property valuation and construction finance.

Job Description: An internal, non-sales role that partners with Relationship Managers to manage the risk and administration of an existing loan portfolio.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor the financial health of existing borrowers.
• Ensure covenant compliance and collect required financial statements.
• Manage loan renewals, extensions, and modifications.
• Process loan advances and paydowns.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Accounting.
• Strong credit analysis and administrative skills.

Job Description: The entry-level "grunt" role. Responsible for building the financial models, doing research, and creating the pitch books used to win deals.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build complex financial models (DCF, LBO, merger models).
• Perform valuation analysis (comps, precedents).
• Create pitch books and presentations.
• Work 80-100+ hours per week.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's from a top university.
• Expert-level Excel, finance, and valuation skills; intense work ethic.

Job Description: A post-MBA role (or a direct promotion) that manages the Analysts and serves as the bridge between junior and senior bankers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage and review the analytical work of Analysts.
• Draft offering memorandums and management presentations.
• Begin to interact more with clients and coordinate deal processes.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA from a top university or 3+ years as an Analyst.
• Strong project management and financial skills.

Job Description: A mid-level senior banker responsible for project management and client relationships. Manages the entire deal team and execution process.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the primary project manager on live deals (M&A, IPOs).
• Manage associates and analysts to execute the deal.
• Lead key diligence sessions and client communications.
• Begin to develop their own client network.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 3-4+ years as an Associate.
• Strong leadership, project management, and client relationship skills.

Job Description: A senior banker focused almost entirely on client relationship management and winning new business ("origination").
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage senior-level client relationships.
• Identify and pitch new deal ideas to prospective clients.
• Lead negotiations and provide strategic advice to CEOs/CFOs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Proven track record as a VP.
• Expert negotiation, strategic, and "rainmaking" (sales) skills.

Job Description: The highest rank. A senior partner responsible for generating millions in revenue by originating deals, managing key client relationships, and running the business group.
Key Responsibilities:
• Serve as the lead relationship manager for the firm's biggest clients.
• Generate significant revenue by winning major M&A or capital-raising deals.
• Manage the P&L for their industry or product group.
• Set strategy and recruit senior talent.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Elite track record as a Director.
• World-class reputation, network, and deal-generation skills.

Job Description: An investment banker who works in the Mergers & Acquisitions "product group," specializing only in advising companies on buying or selling.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build complex merger models (accretion/dilution analysis).
• Manage the M&A process data room and diligence requests.
• Analyze strategic alternatives for clients.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • All the skills of an IB Analyst, plus specialized M&A modeling expertise.

Job Description: A "product group" specialist who advises companies on raising money by selling stock (e.g., an IPO, Follow-On Offering).
Key Responsibilities:
• Advise clients on the optimal timing and structure of equity offerings.
• Manage the IPO execution process (drafting prospectus, roadshow).
• Act as the liaison between the client and the bank's sales & trading desk.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • Expertise in equity valuation, market dynamics, and SEC regulations for IPOs.

Job Description: A "product group" specialist who advises companies and governments on raising money by issuing bonds (debt).
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze a company's debt profile and credit rating.
• Advise on the optimal timing and structure of a bond issuance.
• Manage the execution, pricing, and sale of bonds to the market.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • Deep knowledge of fixed-income markets, credit ratings, and debt structuring.

Job Description: A high-yield specialist who arranges debt (junk bonds, leveraged loans) for companies with low credit ratings, often to fund LBOs by Private Equity firms.
Key Responsibilities:
• Model complex debt scenarios for LBOs.
• Structure and market high-yield debt to institutional investors.
• Work very closely with Private Equity clients and credit funds.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • Expert-level credit analysis, LBO modeling, and debt market knowledge.

Job Description: Supports a Senior Analyst in covering a "universe" of public stocks in a specific industry (e.g., software). They write research reports and build models.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build and maintain financial models for a list of covered stocks.
• Write research reports with "Buy/Sell/Hold" recommendations.
• Listen to earnings calls and gather industry data.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Accounting.
• CFA (or progress) is required.
• Strong modeling and writing skills.

Job Description: The senior, client-facing analyst who publishes research under their own name and serves as an expert for institutional investors (e.g., mutual funds).
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead an industry coverage team (associates).
• Formulate investment theses and make official stock recommendations.
• Build relationships with institutional clients and company management teams.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA or CFA.
• 7-10+ years of research experience and a stellar reputation/track record.

Job Description: A junior role on the "sell side" sales desk. Supports senior salespeople who call on institutional clients (hedge funds, mutual funds) to pitch ideas and take orders.
Key Responsibilities:
• Support senior sales staff with research, data, and client requests.
• Prepare morning update summaries for clients.
• Learn market dynamics and build client relationships.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Must pass SIE, Series 7, and 63.
• Strong communication and sales skills.

Job Description: A junior role on a trading desk (e.g., equities, bonds, derivatives) who assists senior traders in executing trades and managing the desk's "book" (risk).
Key Responsibilities:
• Execute client trades under the supervision of a senior trader.
• Analyze market data and P&L for the trading desk.
• Develop pricing models for specific financial products.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's (often in Math, Econ, or Engineering).
• Must pass SIE, Series 7, and 63.
• Highly quantitative, analytical, and risk-tolerant.

Job Description: An internal risk role that analyzes the bank's overall lending portfolio (not just one loan) to measure risk and ensure diversification.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze the overall credit risk of the bank's entire loan portfolio.
• Perform stress tests on the portfolio (e.g., "what if a recession hits?").
• Set industry concentration limits and risk appetite for the bank.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Stats.
• Strong quantitative modeling and data analysis skills.

Job Description: Monitors the bank's risk exposure from its own trading desks (S&T), measuring potential losses from moves in interest rates, stock prices, or currencies.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor and calculate the bank's risk metrics (e.g., Value-at-Risk or "VaR").
• Ensure trading desks stay within their approved risk limits.
• Report risk exposures directly to senior management and regulators.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Master's in Financial Engineering or Quantitative Finance.
• Highly advanced math, stats, and programming skills (Python/R).

Job Description: Identifies and mitigates risks related to internal failures—bad processes, human error, failed systems, or external events (e.g., fraud, natural disasters).
Key Responsibilities:
• Map internal business processes to identify potential failure points.
• Develop and implement internal controls to prevent fraud or errors.
• Manage business continuity and disaster recovery plans.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Business/Finance.
• Strong analytical, process-mapping, and auditing skills.

Job Description: Manages the risk that the bank will not have enough cash on hand to meet its short-term obligations (e.g., a "run on the bank").
Key Responsibilities:
• Model the bank's daily, weekly, and monthly cash flow needs.
• Conduct stress tests on funding sources.
• Manage the bank's portfolio of highly liquid assets.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/Master's in Finance.
• Strong cash flow modeling and treasury management skills.

Job Description: An investigative analyst who monitors customer transactions for suspicious activity to prevent money laundering and terrorist financing.
Key Responsibilities:
• Investigate automated alerts for potentially suspicious transactions.
• Conduct deep due diligence on high-risk accounts.
• Write and file Suspicious Activity Reports (SARs) with the government (FinCEN).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree (Criminal Justice or Finance).
• Strong investigative, analytical, and writing skills.
• CAMS certification is a plus.

Job Description: An onboarding compliance role responsible for performing due diligence on new clients before they open an account to verify their identity and assess their risk.
Key Responsibilities:
• Verify the identity of new individual and corporate clients.
• Perform Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) on high-risk clients.
• Ensure all new client documentation meets legal requirements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• High attention to detail, research skills, and knowledge of the Bank Secrecy Act (BSA).

Job Description: A mid-to-senior level professional who advises the business on adhering to all banking laws and regulations, interprets new rules, and manages regulatory exams.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the subject matter expert on banking regulations (e.g., Reg W, Reg O).
• Conduct compliance testing and monitoring of business activities.
• Manage formal examinations by regulators (Fed, OCC, FDIC).
• Develop and deliver compliance training to employees.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/JD.
• CRCM (Certified Regulatory Compliance Manager) certification is standard.

Job Description: An internal employee who independently tests the bank's own internal controls, processes, and risk management functions to ensure they are working properly.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform audits of various bank departments (e.g., lending, operations).
• Test financial, operational, and compliance controls.
• Write audit reports identifying control weaknesses and recommendations.
• Report findings directly to the Board's Audit Committee.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Accounting/Finance.
• CPA, CIA (Certified Internal Auditor), or CFSA (Certified Financial Services Auditor) required.

Job Description: A specialized internal auditor who focuses only on testing the bank's technology systems, cybersecurity defenses, and IT infrastructure.
Key Responsibilities:
• Audit IT general controls (e.g., user access, change management).
• Perform cybersecurity and vulnerability assessments.
• Ensure IT systems are compliant with regulations (e.g., data privacy).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Info Systems.
• CISA (Certified Information Systems Auditor) certification is required.

Job Description: An administrative role that supports loan officers by gathering, verifying, and assembling all the documents needed to submit a loan file to underwriting.
Key Responsibilities:
• Collect all required documentation from borrowers.
• Verify income, employment, and assets.
• Order appraisals, title reports, and flood certifications.
• Package the complete loan file for the underwriter.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma.
• Exceptional organization, detail, and communication skills.

Job Description: A "post-closing" operations role that manages a loan after it has closed, handling payments, taxes, and customer service inquiries.
Key Responsibilities:
• Process customer loan payments and manage escrow accounts (for taxes/insurance).
• Handle payoffs, subordinations, and lien releases.
• Answer customer inquiries about their loan balance or payments.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma.
• Strong bookkeeping, data entry, and customer service skills.

Job Description: A back-office investigator who monitors customer accounts and transactions in real-time to identify and stop fraudulent activity (e.g., stolen debit cards, check fraud).
Key Responsibilities:
• Review real-time transaction alerts for potential fraud.
• Contact customers to verify suspicious activity.
• Place holds on accounts or cards to prevent losses.
• Investigate and document fraud cases for law enforcement.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Associate's/Bachelor's degree.
• Strong investigative, analytical, and quick decision-making skills.

Job Description: A high-risk operational role responsible for accurately and securely processing high-value domestic and international wire transfers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Process incoming and outgoing wire transfer requests.
• Verify client identity and perform security callbacks.
• Ensure compliance with AML and OFAC (sanctions) screening.
• Manually key and verify transfer details (routing, SWIFT codes).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma.
• Extreme attention to detail and ability to work under pressure.

Job Description: Manages all "back office" departments that support the bank, such as loan servicing, deposit operations (ACH/Wire), and fraud prevention.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead and manage multiple operational teams.
• Develop and optimize operational workflows for efficiency.
• Ensure operational compliance with all banking regulations.
• Manage vendor relationships (e.g., core processing software).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• 7-10+ years in bank operations.
• AAP (Accredited ACH Professional) cert is a plus.

Job Description: A software engineer who builds and maintains the bank's technology, from customer-facing mobile apps to internal trading or risk platforms.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design, code, and test new applications (e.g., mobile banking features).
• Maintain and integrate legacy banking systems.
• Work with core processing systems and APIs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Computer Science.
• Proficiency in Java, Python, C#, and modern web/mobile frameworks.

Job Description: The bridge between the Finance/Accounting department and the IT department. Manages the bank's core financial software (e.g., the general ledger, risk systems).
Key Responsibilities:
• Configure and maintain financial reporting and risk software.
• Write custom reports for finance and accounting users.
• Troubleshoot system errors and manage software upgrades.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance or Info Systems.
• Strong understanding of both accounting principles and IT systems.

Job Description: A technical expert on the bank's "core" system (e.g., Fiserv, FIS, Jack Henry)—the central computer system that holds all customer accounts and ledgers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the daily processing and nightly batch jobs for the core system.
• Troubleshoot core system errors and outages.
• Manage updates, new feature implementation, and system parameters.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Associate's/Bachelor's in IT.
• Specific certification/training on a core banking platform.

Job Description: An IT professional focused exclusively on defending the bank's data and systems from cyber threats.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor the bank's network for intrusions using SIEM tools.
• Conduct vulnerability assessments and penetration testing.
• Respond to and investigate security incidents.
• Implement security protocols (firewalls, data encryption).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Cybersecurity.
• CompTIA Security+, CISSP certifications.
• Deep knowledge of network security.

Job Description: A specialized accountant (often at a "custodian bank" like State Street or BNY Mellon) who calculates the daily Net Asset Value (NAV) of a mutual fund.
Key Responsibilities:
• Process all fund portfolio transactions (buys, sells, dividends).
• Reconcile cash and asset positions with the broker.
• Calculate the daily NAV (price per share) for the fund.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Accounting/Finance.
• Strong detail-orientation and ability to work on a strict daily deadline.

Job Description: An investment professional (at a bank's Asset Management division) who manages a mutual fund or ETF, making all buy/sell decisions for the fund's investors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct macroeconomic and company research.
• Make all final investment decisions for the fund's portfolio.
• Market the fund to investors and consultants.
• Adhere to the fund's stated investment prospectus.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA and/or CFA designation.
• A proven, long-term track record of investment performance.

Job Description: A Personal Banker or Manager who holds a state commission as a Notary Public to witness and certify signatures on legal documents for bank customers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Witness the signing of legal documents (e.g., deeds, power of attorney).
• Verify the identity of all signers.
• Administer oaths and affirmations.
• Record all notarial acts in a logbook as required by law.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • Must meet state requirements and pass an exam to receive a Notary commission.

Job Description: A marketing professional who develops campaigns and materials (digital and print) to promote the bank's brand and specific products (e.g., new checking accounts).
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop marketing campaigns for loans, deposits, and wealth services.
• Manage the bank's social media, website, and digital ad spending.
• Ensure all marketing materials are compliant with bank regulations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Marketing.
• Strong knowledge of digital marketing and financial services compliance.

Job Description: Specializes in Asset-Based Lending (ABL), where loans are secured by a company's accounts receivable or inventory, requiring daily monitoring.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor daily "borrowing base" certificates from clients.
• Analyze A/R aging reports and inventory listings.
• Perform field exams (on-site audits) of client collateral.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Accounting/Finance.
• High attention to detail; specific training in secured lending.

Job Description: A highly quantitative role responsible for validating the mathematical models the bank uses for things like credit risk, market risk, and pricing derivatives.
Key Responsibilities:
• Independently test and validate the bank's financial models.
• Assess model accuracy, assumptions, and limitations.
• Report findings and necessary model corrections to senior management.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• PhD or Master's in a quantitative field (Math, Stats, Physics).
• Elite programming (Python/R) and mathematical modeling skills.

Job Description: A specialized commercial lender who works exclusively with farmers and agricultural businesses.
Key Responsibilities:
• Originate loans for farmland, livestock, equipment, and crop operations.
• Analyze farm-specific cash flows, commodity prices, and government subsidies.
• Understand the unique risks and cycles of the agriculture industry.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Agri-Business or Finance.
• Strong background in or knowledge of the agriculture industry.

Job Description: The senior executive responsible for the bank's entire lending portfolio (both commercial and retail), lending strategy, and credit risk appetite.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage all senior lending officers (commercial, retail, mortgage).
• Set the bank's overall credit policy and risk strategy.
• Chair the bank's loan committee for approving the largest loans.
• Report on portfolio health to the CEO and Board.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA in Finance.
• 15-20+ years of senior credit and lending experience.

Job Description: The executive responsible for identifying and managing all major risks across the entire enterprise (credit, market, operational, liquidity, and compliance).
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead all risk management departments.
• Develop the bank's enterprise risk management (ERM) framework.
• Report directly to the Board of Directors on all major risk exposures.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Advanced degree (MBA/Finance/Quant).
• Extensive experience across multiple risk disciplines.

Job Description: The highest-ranking executive, responsible for the bank's overall strategy, profitability, and vision. Reports directly to the Board of Directors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Set the overall strategic direction and vision for the bank.
• Manage the entire senior executive team (CFO, CLO, CRO, COO).
• Act as the primary public face of the bank to investors, regulators, and the community.
• Drive shareholder value and long-term profitability.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Advanced degree (MBA).
• Decades of progressive leadership experience in banking.

Job Description: An operations expert on the Automated Clearing House (ACH) network, processing direct deposits, payroll files, and pre-authorized debits.
Key Responsibilities:
• Process daily incoming and outgoing ACH batch files.
• Handle ACH returns, disputes, and exceptions.
• Ensure strict compliance with all Nacha operating rules.
• Originate ACH files for commercial clients.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma.
• AAP (Accredited ACH Professional) certification is highly valued.

Job Description: An execution-only trader working within a private bank or wealth division to execute buy/sell orders (stocks, bonds) placed by Financial Advisors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Execute large block trades for multiple client accounts simultaneously.
• Find the best market price ("best execution") for client orders.
• Monitor market activity and communicate with advisors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Must hold Series 7 and 63 licenses.
• Ability to work calmly in fast-paced market conditions.

Job Description: A specialist investment banker (often called "RX") who advises companies that are in deep financial distress or bankruptcy.
Key Responsibilities:
• Advise distressed companies or their creditors on chapter 11 bankruptcy.
• Model complex "waterfall" scenarios and recovery analysis.
• Negotiate with creditors to restructure the company's debt.
• Arrange DIP (Debtor-in-Possession) financing.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• All IB Analyst/Associate skills.
• Expertise in bankruptcy law and complex credit analysis.

Job Description: The individual formally designated and responsible for the bank's entire anti-money laundering (AML) and BSA compliance program.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and manage the bank's overall BSA/AML policies.
• Oversee the AML/KYC analyst teams.
• Ensure all regulatory reporting (SARs, CTRs) is filed correctly.
• Act as the main contact for regulators on all BSA matters.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/Master's/JD.
• CAMS certification required.
• 7-10+ years in AML/BSA compliance.

Job Description: Facilitates international trade for commercial clients by issuing letters of credit, handling documentary collections, and managing trade loans.
Key Responsibilities:
• Issue and advise on Letters of Credit (L/Cs) for importers/exporters.
• Examine trade documents to ensure strict compliance with L/C terms.
• Manage import/export financing and open account transactions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• CDCS (Certified Documentary Credit Specialist) cert. is a plus.
• Deep knowledge of UCP 600 rules.

Job Description: Designs and implements complex mathematical models that are used to price securities, assess risk, or execute algorithmic trading strategies.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop mathematical models for pricing complex derivatives.
• Create algorithms for high-frequency trading.
• Use statistical analysis to back-test trading strategies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• PhD or Master's in a highly quantitative field (Math, Physics, Stats).
• Elite programming (C++, Python) and mathematical skills.

Job Description: Manages relationships with very large corporations (Fortune 500), coordinating all bank products including loans, capital markets, and treasury services.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the senior relationship manager for large corporate clients.
• Coordinate with product partners (IB, Treasury) to deliver bank solutions.
• Originate massive, complex syndicated loans.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA in Finance.
• 10+ years experience.
• Strong credit skills and ability to manage complex relationships.

Job Description: A compliance specialist who analyzes bank lending data to ensure the bank is not discriminating against applicants based on race, gender, or other protected classes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Use statistical analysis to review loan application data (approvals/denials).
• Test for disparities in underwriting and pricing.
• Ensure compliance with the Fair Housing Act and Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Stats.
• Strong data analysis skills and knowledge of fair lending laws.

Job Description: Manages the bank's compliance with the Community Reinvestment Act, ensuring the bank meets the credit needs of its entire community, including low-income areas.
Key Responsibilities:
• Track and document all lending, investment, and services in LMI census tracts.
• Manage community development donations and initiatives.
• Prepare for and manage the bank's CRA regulatory exam.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Strong community relations and data analysis skills.

Job Description: A senior executive, often reporting to the CRO or CLO, who sets the bank's credit policies and manages the entire credit underwriting and approval function.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and maintain the bank's formal credit policy.
• Manage all underwriting teams and Credit Analysts.
• Chair loan committees and hold the highest level of credit approval authority.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• 20+ years of expert-level credit and underwriting experience.

Job Description: A hybrid branch role that combines the duties of both a Teller (transactions) and a Personal Banker (sales and account opening).
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform all teller duties (processing transactions).
• Perform all personal banker duties (opening accounts, taking loan apps).
• Handle all customer needs from start to finish.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma/Bachelor's.
• Strong skills in both customer service, cash handling, and sales.

Job Description: Works in a specialized group that provides a bundle of services to hedge fund clients (e.g., lending them cash/securities, trade execution, and custody).
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the relationship manager for hedge fund clients.
• Facilitate leverage (margin lending) for the fund.
• Coordinate trade settlement and cash management for the fund.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• Deep knowledge of hedge fund operations, risk, and derivatives.

Job Description: A licensed expert within a wealth management division who advises clients on life, disability, and long-term care insurance as part of their overall financial plan.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze a client's existing insurance policies and risk exposure.
• Recommend and sell insurance products (life, disability, LTC).
• Integrate insurance planning into the overall estate plan.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• Must hold State Life, Health & Accident insurance licenses.
• CFP or CLU designation.

Job Description: Services the most elite clients (Single Family Offices), coordinating all bank services for families typically with $100M+ in assets.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the "quarterback" for all bank services for a single family.
• Coordinate private banking, custom credit, and institutional-level investing.
• Manage generational wealth transfer and philanthropic planning.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA/CFA/JD.
• Decades of experience serving UHNW individuals.

Job Description: A banker who manages the portfolio of non-performing commercial loans. Their job is to get the bank's money back from companies that are defaulting.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage a portfolio of defaulted and non-accrual loans.
• Negotiate workout plans, forbearance agreements, or settlements.
• Manage the foreclosure and liquidation process on collateral.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance.
• Expert credit, negotiation, and bankruptcy law skills.

Job Description: An expert on branch procedures, compliance, and systems. They often "float" between branches to train staff and conduct self-audits.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct operational self-audits of branch cash controls and compliance.
• Train new and existing branch staff on policies and procedures.
• Troubleshoot complex operational errors.
• Fill in for Assistant Managers during vacations/absences.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 5+ years of retail banking experience.
• Deep, expert-level knowledge of branch operations and compliance.

Job Description: A high-level investigator (often ex-law enforcement) who handles complex fraud rings, internal investigations, and elder financial abuse cases.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct deep investigations into complex external fraud rings.
• Investigate internal employee fraud and ethical violations.
• Work directly with federal and local law enforcement.
• Handle sensitive cases like elder financial abuse.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree (Criminal Justice).
• CFE (Certified Fraud Examiner) cert.
• 10+ years of investigative experience.

Job Description: A senior market expert who develops the high-level investment "house view" (e.g., asset allocation, economic forecast) for the entire wealth division.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct global macroeconomic research.
• Develop the firm's official investment outlook and asset allocation models.
• Publish white papers and market commentary for advisors and clients.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA, CFA designation required.
• 10+ years as a senior economist or portfolio manager.

Job Description: Manages the team and equipment responsible for processing all paper checks, including scanning (Check 21), processing exceptions, and handling return items.
Key Responsibilities:
• Supervise the daily operations of the check processing department.
• Manage high-speed image sorting and capture equipment.
• Handle large-dollar exception items and NSF (non-sufficient funds) returns.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Associate's degree.
• Strong operational and item-processing knowledge.

Job Description: An Investment Banker (Analyst to MD) who works in an "Industry Group," focusing only on clients in one sector (e.g., Technology, Media, & Telecom).
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide all IB services (M&A, IPO) specifically for clients in one industry.
• Develop deep industry expertise and relationships.
• Create industry-specific valuation models and trend reports.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • All standard IB qualifications, plus a deep passion/knowledge for a specific sector.

Job Description: Manages a bank operation where commercial clients have their customer payments (checks) mailed directly to a bank P.O. box for faster processing and deposit.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage a team responsible for opening mail and scanning checks.
• Oversee data entry and validation of payments against client files.
• Ensure same-day deposit and reporting for corporate clients.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Associate's degree.
• Strong people management and high-volume operations skills.

Job Description: An HR/Finance role responsible for accurately calculating and processing complex, variable commission payments for MLOs, bankers, and advisors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Gather sales performance data from multiple systems.
• Apply complex commission plan rules to calculate payouts.
• Prepare payroll reports and handle commission disputes from employees.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/HR.
• Advanced Excel skills and extreme attention to detail.

Job Description: Manages the lifecycle of a single bank product (e.g., the mobile app, a credit card). They blend tech, marketing, and business strategy.
Key Responsibilities:
• Define the strategy and feature roadmap for a specific bank product.
• Write user stories and work with developers to build new features.
• Analyze product usage data and profitability.
• Gather user feedback to guide product improvements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• Experience in product management, finance, and Agile development.

Job Description: A compliance/operations role that reviews all new accounts after they are opened by the branch to ensure all documentation and KYC data is correct and compliant.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform a quality control review of new account documentation.
• Check for missing signatures, IDs, or incorrect information.
• Ensure all KYC/AML data was collected correctly per the Bank Secrecy Act.
• Send correction notices back to the branch.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 2-3+ years of branch or compliance experience.
• Meticulous attention to detail.

Job Description: An independent "auditor of the auditors." This role independently re-underwrites loans after they have closed to test the quality of the bank's underwriting.
Key Responsibilities:
• Select a sample of recently closed loans.
• Re-underwrite the loan from scratch to validate the original credit decision.
• Assign an independent risk rating to the loan.
• Report findings on underwriting quality to the Board/Regulators.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Accounting/Finance.
• 10+ years as a senior underwriter or credit officer.

Job Description: A tech role that builds "bots" to automate repetitive, manual tasks within the bank's operations (e.g., data entry, reconciling reports).
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze operational tasks to identify automation opportunities.
• Design, build, and test software bots using RPA tools (e.g., UiPath, Blue Prism).
• Maintain and update the bot workforce.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CompSci or IT.
• Certification in RPA software; strong process-mapping skills.

Job Description: An operations specialist who works with the MLO and Underwriter. They are the primary file manager, collecting all documents and verifying them.
Key Responsibilities:
• Gather all loan documentation from the borrower (paystubs, bank statements).
• Verify income/employment and order appraisal/title.
• Assemble the complete, verified file for underwriting.
• Act as the main communication link between the borrower and underwriter.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High school diploma.
• Extreme organization, multitasking, and communication skills.

Job Description: The risk professional who makes the final decision on a mortgage. They analyze the borrower's file to ensure it meets all bank and regulatory (Fannie/Freddie) guidelines.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze the borrower's "Three C's": Credit, Capacity (income), and Collateral (appraisal).
• Ensure the loan file is 100% compliant with guidelines.
• Issue the final credit decision: approval, denial, or suspense (need more info).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• DE (Direct Endorsement) certification for FHA loans is common.
• Strong analytical and guideline-interpretation skills.

Job Description: The final step in the loan process. This person prepares the final legal documents, coordinates the signing, and wires the money ("funds the loan").
Key Responsibilities:
• Prepare the final Closing Disclosure (CD) and closing document package.
• Review all signed documents for accuracy.
• Balance the funding worksheet and wire the loan proceeds to the title company.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 2+ years of mortgage operations experience.
• Extreme attention to detail and ability to work under a strict deadline.

Job Description: A back-office role for an asset manager. Responsible for trade settlement, portfolio reconciliation, and managing corporate actions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Settle trades executed by portfolio managers.
• Reconcile cash and asset positions between the firm, custodians, and brokers.
• Process mandatory corporate actions (e.g., mergers, stock splits).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance.
• Strong operations, reconciliation, and settlement process knowledge.

Job Description: A specialized lender who focuses only on providing loans and leases for companies to acquire specific "hard assets" like manufacturing machinery or transportation fleets.
Key Responsibilities:
• Originate loans/leases for equipment purchases.
• Analyze the value and depreciation schedule of specific collateral.
• Structure lease agreements (e.g., operating vs. capital lease).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance.
• Strong sales skills and deep knowledge of equipment valuation.

Job Description: A corporate recruiter who specializes in finding and hiring talent specifically for the banking industry, from tellers to specialized risk roles.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the full-cycle recruiting process for open roles.
• Source candidates (passive and active) for specialized positions (e.g., underwriters).
• Partner with hiring managers to define roles and negotiate offers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• 3-5+ years of recruiting experience, preferably within financial services.

Job Description: A role within Prime Brokerage that acts as a "matchmaker," introducing hedge fund clients to potential investors (e.g., pensions, endowments).
Key Responsibilities:
• Research and profile potential institutional investors.
• Understand a hedge fund's strategy in detail.
• Facilitate introductions and host capital introduction events.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Finance/Marketing.
• Strong networking, sales, and communication skills.

Job Description: Manages the risk associated with hiring third-party vendors (e.g., software providers, call centers).
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform due diligence on new vendors (checking their finances, security, etc.).
• Review vendor contracts for risk and compliance clauses.
• Conduct ongoing monitoring of critical vendors to ensure they meet performance and security standards.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's in Business/Finance.
• Strong contract review, risk assessment, and analytical skills.

Job Description: An Operational Risk specialist who creates and maintains the bank's Business Continuity Plan (BCP) to ensure the bank can function during a disaster.
Key Responsibilities:
• Identify critical business processes and systems.
• Develop and document plans to recover operations after a disruption (e.g., fire, cyberattack).
• Conduct regular disaster recovery tests and exercises.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• CBCP (Certified Business Continuity Professional) certification.
• Strong project management skills.

Job Description: Manages the operations for the bank's debit and credit card portfolio, including disputes, fraud, and network processing.
Key Responsibilities:
• Process customer disputes for card transactions (Reg E).
• Work with card fraud monitoring systems.
• Reconcile card network settlement reports (e.g., from Visa/Mastercard).
• Order and maintain debit card inventory.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Associate's degree.
• Strong regulatory knowledge (Reg E) and problem-solving skills.

Job Description: An entry-level AML role that focuses on one specific part of the process, such as reviewing Currency Transaction Reports (CTRs) or clearing low-level alerts.
Key Responsibilities:
• Review daily reports for cash transactions exceeding $10,000.
• Prepare and file Currency Transaction Reports (CTRs) with FinCEN.
• Clear or escalate low-level automated transaction monitoring alerts.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree.
• High attention to detail and data entry skills.

Job Description: A senior executive (often reporting to the CFO) who manages the bank's own balance sheet, including liquidity, investments, and interest rate risk.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the bank's investment portfolio (bonds, etc.).
• Model and manage the bank's Interest Rate Risk (IRR) exposure.
• Oversee the bank's liquidity and funding sources (deposits, FHLB borrowing).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA, CFA designation.
• 15+ years experience in treasury, capital markets, or asset-liability management.

Job Description: Supports the Treasurer by running complex models that measure the bank's sensitivity to changes in interest rates.
Key Responsibilities:
• Run complex financial models (simulation, gap analysis) to measure Interest Rate Risk.
• Analyze how rate changes will impact the bank's Net Interest Margin (NIM).
• Prepare ALCO (Asset-Liability Committee) reports.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/Master's in Finance or Econ.
• Very strong quantitative, modeling, and data analysis skills.

Job Description: A Product Manager who specifically manages the bank's digital investment platform (robo-advisor).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the user experience and investment algorithms for the digital platform.
• Work with developers to build and test the product.
• Analyze user data and market trends to drive strategy.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's/MBA.
• Strong background in both FinTech/product management and investment principles.

Job Description: The senior executive responsible for managing all non-financial, non-sales functions of the bank (the "back office"), including all operations and technology.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead all bank operations (deposit, loan, card) and IT departments.
• Drive process improvement and operational efficiency.
• Execute the bank's strategic plan and manage large-scale tech projects.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MBA or advanced degree.
• 15-20+ years of senior leadership in bank operations and technology.

Job Description: The executive formally responsible for the bank's entire compliance program (excluding BSA/AML, which may be separate). Reports directly to the Board.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and manage the bank's Compliance Management System (CMS).
• Act as the primary liaison with all regulators (FDIC, OCC, Fed, CFPB).
• Oversee all compliance areas (Fair Lending, CRA, consumer protection regs).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• JD (Law Degree) or CRCM certification.
• 10-15+ years of expert-level bank compliance experience.

Job Description: An entry-to-mid-level role focusing on designing, analyzing, and testing mechanical components and systems under the guidance of senior engineers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create 3D models and 2D drawings using CAD software.
• Perform basic stress analysis (FEA).
• Build and test prototypes.
• Support manufacturing and assembly teams.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Mechanical Engineering.
• Proficiency in CAD (SolidWorks, AutoCAD, or CATIA).
• Strong grasp of physics, mechanics, and thermodynamics.

Job Description: A technical leader who manages complex design projects, performs advanced analysis, and mentors junior engineers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead the design lifecycle of major mechanical systems.
• Run complex simulations (FEA, CFD).
• Select materials and manufacturing processes.
• Interface with vendors and clients.
• Review and approve designs from junior staff.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Mechanical Engineering.
• 5-8+ years of experience.
• Expert in CAD/CAE tools.
• Professional Engineer (PE) license often preferred.

Job Description: Specializes in designing Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning systems for residential, commercial, and industrial buildings.
Key Responsibilities:
• Calculate heating and cooling loads for buildings.
• Design ductwork, piping, and control systems.
• Select equipment (boilers, chillers, AHUs).
• Ensure designs meet building codes and energy standards.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Mechanical Engineering.
• Knowledge of ASHRAE standards.
• Proficiency in HVAC design software (e.g., Revit MEP, Trane TRACE).

Job Description: A specialist focusing on thermal management, heat transfer, and energy conversion in systems like engines, power plants, and electronics.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze and model heat transfer within complex systems.
• Design thermal management solutions (e.g., heat sinks, cooling systems).
• Optimize systems for thermal and energy efficiency.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MS or PhD in Mechanical Engineering preferred.
• Expertise in thermodynamics, fluid dynamics, and heat transfer.
• Skills in CFD and thermal modeling software.

Job Description: A multidisciplinary role (ME/EE/CS) that designs, builds, and programs robotic systems and automation solutions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design robotic actuators, grippers, and chassis (mechanical aspect).
• Integrate sensors, motors, and controllers (electrical aspect).
• Program robot movements and logic (software aspect).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in ME, EE, or Robotics.
• Skills in C++, Python, ROS (Robot Operating System).
• Knowledge of kinematics, dynamics, and control systems.

Job Description: Stands for Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing. This engineer designs and coordinates these three critical building systems, typically for a construction/architecture firm.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and integrate HVAC, power distribution, and plumbing systems.
• Ensure systems do not clash (using BIM/Revit).
• Coordinate utility hookups and building-wide system controls.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering.
• High proficiency in Revit and AutoCAD.
• Strong knowledge of building codes.

Job Description: A role focused purely on the design and modeling aspect of engineering, translating concepts and requirements into detailed manufacturing blueprints.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create detailed 3D models and assemblies.
• Produce 2D manufacturing drawings with full GD&T (Geometric Dimensioning and Tolerancing).
• Iterate designs based on feedback from analysis and manufacturing teams.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Mechanical Engineering.
• Expert-level CAD skills.
• Deep knowledge of manufacturing processes (machining, injection molding) and GD&T.

Job Description: A specialist (often in Oil & Gas or Chem) who designs the complex piping systems used to transport fluids and gases within an industrial plant.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design pipe layouts, routes, and support structures.
• Perform pipe stress analysis to handle pressure and temperature.
• Select appropriate materials, valves, and fittings.
• Create P&ID (Piping & Instrumentation Diagrams).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Mechanical or Chemical Engineering.
• Proficiency in piping design software (e.g., AutoCAD Plant 3D, Caesar II).

Job Description: A top-tier technical expert (often non-managerial) who solves the most complex technical problems and sets the technical strategy for the department.
Key Responsibilities:
• Serve as the lead technical consultant for the organization.
• Solve novel engineering problems with no existing precedent.
• Mentor entire teams of senior engineers.
• Drive innovation and new technology adoption.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MS or PhD with 15+ years of experience.
• Recognized expert in a specific domain.
• Often holds multiple patents.

Job Description: An ME specializing in the design, development, and testing of vehicles or their subsystems (e.g., powertrain, chassis, suspension).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and test vehicle components for performance and durability.
• Analyze vehicle dynamics and powertrain efficiency.
• Integrate various subsystems (engine, transmission, electronics).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Mechanical or Automotive Engineering.
• Passion for vehicles.
• Skills in CAD and vehicle simulation tools.

Job Description: Designs, develops, and tests electrical systems and components, focusing on power generation, distribution, and electronic device applications.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design electrical schematics and circuit layouts.
• Select electrical components (resistors, capacitors, ICs).
• Build and test prototypes of electrical circuits.
• Assist with compliance and safety testing.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Electrical Engineering (BSEE).
• Skills in circuit design software (e.g., Altium, Eagle) and simulation (SPICE).

Job Description: Leads complex electrical design projects, manages power systems architecture, and oversees compliance for entire products.
Key Responsibilities:
• Architect complex electrical systems from concept to production.
• Solve advanced issues in power, analog, or digital design.
• Mentor junior engineers and review their schematics.
• Manage relationships with component suppliers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or MSEE.
• 7+ years of experience.
• Expert in schematic capture and PCB layout.
• PE license often required for power roles.

Job Description: A specialized EE focusing on the generation, transmission, and distribution of high-voltage electric power (the "power grid").
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and analyze power grids, substations, and transformers.
• Perform load flow, short circuit, and stability studies.
• Develop protective relaying schemes to protect the grid.
• Ensure grid reliability and efficiency.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE (MSEE often preferred).
• Expertise in power systems analysis software (ETAP, PSS/E).
• PE license is critical.

Job Description: Designs and programs the automated control logic (like PLCs) that run industrial machinery, manufacturing lines, and process plants.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design automated control strategies using feedback loops.
• Program PLCs (Programmable Logic Controllers) and HMIs (Human-Machine Interfaces).
• Tune PID loops for optimal system performance.
• Integrate sensors, motors, and actuators.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or BS in ME/ChemE.
• Expertise in PLC programming (e.g., Rockwell/Allen-Bradley, Siemens).
• Knowledge of industrial networks.

Job Description: A highly specialized EE who designs and tests systems that transmit or receive radio waves, such as Wi-Fi, 5G, Bluetooth, radar, and satellite communications.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design RF circuits, antennas, amplifiers, and filters.
• Test and optimize RF signal propagation and quality.
• Use spectrum analyzers and network analyzers.
• Ensure products meet FCC and wireless standards.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MSEE or PhD usually required.
• Deep knowledge of electromagnetics, high-frequency signals, and Smith Charts.

Job Description: A Digital Signal Processing engineer. Uses advanced math and algorithms to manipulate digital signals (like audio, video, or sensor data).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and implement algorithms to filter noise, compress data, or identify patterns.
• Program DSP chips or FPGAs to execute algorithms in real-time.
• Work heavily in audio/video processing, telecom, and radar.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MSEE or PhD required.
• Expert in advanced mathematics (Fourier transforms) and MATLAB/C++.

Job Description: A broad title often synonymous with an EE focused on low-power components, printed circuit boards (PCBs), and consumer electronic devices.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design schematics for PCB assemblies.
• Oversee the PCB layout, fabrication, and assembly process.
• Test and debug prototype boards.
• Manage product lifecycle from concept to end-of-life.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE.
• Skills in PCB design tools (Altium, KiCad).
• Knowledge of microcontrollers.

Job Description: An EE who specializes in testing, validating, and debugging electronic hardware rather than designing it from scratch.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop test plans and procedures for new PCBs.
• Build automated test fixtures (using tools like LabVIEW).
• Identify, document, and debug hardware failures.
• Certify that hardware meets all design specifications.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or BS in Computer Engineering.
• Strong debugging and troubleshooting skills.
• Experience with oscilloscopes, logic analyzers.

Job Description: Designs, selects, and installs the instruments (sensors, transmitters, valves) used to measure and control variables in an industrial process plant.
Key Responsibilities:
• Select the correct instrument (e.g., for pressure, temperature, flow).
• Create instrumentation loop diagrams and hookup drawings.
• Calibrate and commission instruments in the field.
• Integrate sensor data into the main control system (DCS/PLC).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or BS in ChemE.
• Knowledge of industrial instrumentation and process control.

Job Description: A role (often in construction/utilities) focused on designing electrical power systems for buildings, campuses, or subdivisions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design power distribution layouts, cable sizing, and panel schedules.
• Plan lighting systems, fire alarms, and emergency power.
• Ensure all designs comply with the NEC (National Electrical Code).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE.
• PE license is critical.
• Proficiency in AutoCAD and electrical design software.

Job Description: An entry-level role supporting projects related to public works, including roads, bridges, and water systems. Works under a PE.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform basic design and drafting tasks using AutoCAD Civil 3D.
• Assist with site surveying and data collection.
• Conduct material testing and site inspections.
• Prepare quantity calculations and cost estimates.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Civil Engineering.
• Must have passed the FE (Fundamentals of Engineering) exam to be on the PE track (EIT status).

Job Description: A licensed Professional Engineer who has the legal authority to sign and seal (approve) engineering plans for public projects.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage entire design projects for land development or public works.
• Perform and stamp complex engineering calculations.
• Serve as the Engineer of Record, assuming legal responsibility for the design.
• Testify at public meetings and zoning boards.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Civil Engineering.
• Must hold an active PE license (requires 4+ years of experience and passing the PE exam).

Job Description: A specialized civil engineer who analyzes and designs the "bones" of structures (buildings, bridges, towers) to ensure they can withstand loads and resist forces.
Key Responsibilities:
• Calculate all potential loads (gravity, wind, seismic).
• Design structural systems (beams, columns, foundations).
• Analyze the structural integrity of existing buildings.
• Produce detailed structural drawings.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Civil Engineering (Structural emphasis).
• PE or SE (Structural Engineer) license is required.
• Expertise in analysis software (RISA, STAAD.Pro).

Job Description: A civil engineer focused on the planning, design, and operation of transportation systems, including roads, highways, and public transit.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design highway geometry, intersections, and signage.
• Conduct traffic impact studies for new developments.
• Analyze traffic flow and design signal timing.
• Plan pedestrian and bicycle pathways.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Civil Engineering.
• Knowledge of transportation modeling software (e.g., Vissim, Synchro).
• PE or TE (Traffic Engineer) license.

Job Description: A civil engineer who manages water systems, focusing on hydrology (water flow), hydraulics (water pressure/movement), and infrastructure.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design stormwater management and drainage systems (culverts, ponds).
• Model floodplains and analyze flood risk (using HEC-RAS).
• Design water distribution (potable) and wastewater collection systems.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Civil or Environmental Engineering.
• Skills in hydrology/hydraulic modeling software.
• PE license.

Job Description: A civil engineer who analyzes the properties of soil and rock to determine their suitability for supporting structures.
Key Responsibilities:
• Plan and coordinate soil boring and testing.
• Analyze soil mechanics, stability, and load-bearing capacity.
• Design foundations (deep piles, shallow footings) for buildings/bridges.
• Design retaining walls and analyze slope stability.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MS in Civil Engineering (Geotechnical emphasis) is standard.
• PE license required.

Job Description: A civil engineer who works on-site at a construction project, applying engineering principles to construction methods, scheduling, and quality.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the technical liaison between the design team and on-site contractors.
• Manage project scheduling, cost control, and procurement.
• Ensure construction adheres to the engineering drawings and specs.
• Oversee on-site safety and quality control (QC).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Civil Engineering or Construction Management.
• Strong field and communication skills.

Job Description: A civil engineer employed directly by a city or county government to manage all public works and infrastructure.
Key Responsibilities:
• Oversee the design and maintenance of public roads, sewers, and parks.
• Review engineering plans submitted by private developers for code compliance.
• Manage public works budgets and capital improvement plans.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Civil Engineering.
• Must hold a PE license.
• Strong understanding of public policy and regulations.

Job Description: Manages teams of land surveyors who make precise measurements to determine property boundaries and map land features.
Key Responsibilities:
• Coordinate field survey crews (using GPS, total stations, drones).
• Perform complex boundary calculations and resolve deed disputes.
• Produce and stamp official plats, topo maps, and ALTA surveys.
• Work closely with title companies, architects, and civil engineers.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • Must be a Professional Land Surveyor (PLS), which requires state licensure.

Job Description: A technical specialist (not always an engineer) who translates the designs and calculations of engineers into formal construction drawings using AutoCAD Civil 3D.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create detailed site plans, grading plans, and road profiles.
• Manage drawing sets, layers, and standards.
• Incorporate survey data and redlines from engineers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Associate's degree or certificate in CAD drafting.
• Expert proficiency in AutoCAD Civil 3D is the key skill.

Job Description: A broad role involving the design, development, testing, and maintenance of software applications using programming languages and engineering principles.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code.
• Collaborate with teams (PMs, designers) to define and build features.
• Debug and resolve bugs in existing codebases.
• Write unit tests and participate in code reviews.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Computer Science (CS) or related field.
• Proficiency in at least one language (e.g., Java, Python, C++, C#).

Job Description: A software engineer specializing in the "client-side"—the visual elements of a website or application that the user directly interacts with in their browser.
Key Responsibilities:
• Translate UI/UX designs into functional, responsive web pages using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
• Build and maintain user interfaces using frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.
• Optimize applications for speed and cross-browser compatibility.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS.
• Expertise in JavaScript, HTML5, CSS3, and modern JS frameworks.

Job Description: A software engineer specializing in the "server-side"—the database, servers, and APIs that power the application behind the scenes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build and maintain server-side applications and business logic.
• Design and manage databases (SQL or NoSQL).
• Create and maintain scalable APIs for the frontend to consume.
• Manage application security and data protection.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS.
• Expertise in backend languages (e.g., Java, Python, Node.js, Go) and database design.

Job Description: An engineer who is comfortable working on both the frontend (client-side) and backend (server-side) parts of an application.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and build complete web applications from end to end.
• Manage database, server, API, and client-side UI.
• Work across the entire software stack to solve problems.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS.
• Proficiency in both frontend (JS framework) and backend (e.g., Node.js, Python) technologies.

Job Description: An engineering role that bridges software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops), focusing on automating the software build, test, and deployment pipeline.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build and manage CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines.
• Manage cloud infrastructure as code (IaC) using tools like Terraform.
• Implement application monitoring and alerting systems.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS or IT.
• Skills in scripting (Bash, Python), cloud platforms (AWS, Azure), and CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitLab).

Job Description: A specialized DevOps role (originating at Google) that treats operations problems as software problems. Focuses on reliability, scalability, and performance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Define and measure Service Level Objectives (SLOs) and Error Budgets.
• Automate incident response and operations tasks.
• Conduct "blameless postmortems" to analyze failures.
• Focus on making systems more scalable and resilient.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS.
• Strong software engineering and systems administration skills.

Job Description: A software engineer who writes low-level code that runs on hardware directly, rather than on a traditional OS. This is code for "embedded" systems like microwaves, cars, or IoT devices.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write and debug code in C or C++ for microcontrollers.
• Work directly with hardware, reading datasheets and schematics.
• Optimize code for speed, memory, and power constraints.
• Also known as "Firmware Engineer."
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Computer Engineering, EE, or CS.
• Strong C/C++ skills and understanding of computer architecture.

Job Description: A software engineer who specializes in building applications specifically for mobile operating systems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop native applications for either iOS (using Swift/Objective-C) or Android (using Kotlin/Java).
• Integrate with mobile-specific features (camera, GPS, notifications).
• Optimize apps for mobile performance and battery life.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS.
• Expertise in the specific mobile platform's SDK and development tools (Xcode, Android Studio).

Job Description: A very senior engineer who makes high-level design choices for the entire software system, defining standards, platforms, and technology stacks for other teams to follow.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the overall system architecture (e.g., microservices vs. monolith).
• Select the technology stack (languages, databases, cloud services).
• Define technical standards for coding, APIs, and security.
• Ensure the system is scalable, reliable, and maintainable.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS/MS in CS.
• 10-15+ years of experience as a senior software engineer.

Job Description: An IT/engineering role focused on designing, deploying, and managing applications and infrastructure on cloud computing platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design scalable and secure cloud infrastructure.
• Migrate on-premise applications to the cloud.
• Manage cloud services (VMs, databases, storage).
• Monitor cloud spending and optimize costs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS or IT.
• Cloud certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect) are critical.

Job Description: A Quality Assurance (QA) engineer who writes code specifically to automate the testing process, rather than testing manually.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write automated test scripts using frameworks (e.g., Selenium, Cypress, Playwright).
• Integrate automated tests into the CI/CD pipeline.
• Identify, log, and track software bugs.
• Develop and maintain the test automation framework.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS or related.
• Strong programming/scripting skills (Python, JavaScript).
• Detail-oriented and analytical mindset.

Job Description: An engineering role focused on designing and building systems that are resilient to cyber attacks. This is a builder role, distinct from a security analyst (a defender role).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design secure network architectures (firewalls, VPNs, zero-trust).
• Implement identity and access management (IAM) solutions.
• Write and review code for security vulnerabilities (secure coding).
• Build systems for encryption, intrusion detection, and data protection.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in CS or Cybersecurity.
• Deep knowledge of networks, protocols, and encryption.
• Security certs (CISSP, CEH) common.

Job Description: Researches, designs, develops, and tests computer systems and components such as processors, circuit boards, memory devices, and routers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design computer hardware components (e.g., motherboards, processors).
• Test prototypes and validate hardware performance.
• Integrate components into computer systems.
• Stay current with new hardware technologies (e.g., next-gen memory).
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Computer Engineering or BSEE.

Job Description: Sits between hardware and software. Writes the permanent software (firmware) that is programmed onto a hardware device's ROM (e.g., the code that "boots" a computer).
Key Responsibilities:
• Write low-level C or Assembly code for embedded systems.
• Develop the code that initializes hardware on power-up (BIOS/UEFI).
• Debug issues at the hardware/software boundary.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Computer Engineering (CompE) or BSEE.

Job Description: Designs Application-Specific Integrated Circuits (ASICs)—custom chips designed for one specific purpose (e.g., a Bitcoin miner chip, a custom video codec chip).
Key Responsibilities:
• Write hardware description language (HDL) code (Verilog or VHDL) to define chip logic.
• Simulate and verify the chip design before "tape-out" (manufacturing).
