• Run logic synthesis and timing analysis.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MSEE or MS in CompE required.
• Expertise in HDL (Verilog/VHDL) and design tools (e.g., Synopsys, Cadence).

Job Description: Designs logic for FPGAs (Field-Programmable Gate Arrays)—chips that can be re-programmed after manufacturing, ideal for prototyping ASICs or for low-volume tasks.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write Verilog or VHDL code to define logic circuits.
• Implement and test designs on FPGA development boards.
• Perform timing analysis and resource optimization for the FPGA.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or BS in CompE.
• Expertise in HDL and FPGA vendor tools (e.g., Xilinx Vivado, Intel Quartus).

Job Description: Very Large-Scale Integration. A broad term for engineers who design and manufacture extremely complex integrated circuits (microchips) with billions of transistors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Can be a design role (writing Verilog) or a physical layout role (designing the transistor layout on the silicon wafer).
• Focuses on chip fabrication processes.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MSEE or PhD required.
• Deep knowledge of semiconductor physics and microelectronics.

Job Description: A very senior hardware engineer who defines the high-level architecture of a complete hardware system (e.g., a new server, a smartphone).
Key Responsibilities:
• Define the key components (processor, memory, I/O) and how they interconnect.
• Make critical trade-offs between performance, cost, and power.
• Create the system-level technical specification.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • MS or PhD in CompE/EE with 15+ years experience.

Job Description: A post-design role. Takes the physical hardware prototypes (new chips or boards) and runs rigorous tests to find bugs (escapes) from the design phase.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop test plans for new hardware.
• Write test scripts (often in Python or C) to stress the hardware.
• Debug failures using complex lab equipment (oscilloscopes, logic analyzers).
• Verify the hardware works across all temperatures and voltages.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or BS in CompE.
• Strong scripting (Python) and lab debugging skills.

Job Description: An engineer who plans, designs, and implements the physical computer networks (routers, switches, firewalls) that allow computers to communicate.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design logical and physical network layouts.
• Configure and install routers, switches, and firewalls.
• Monitor network performance and troubleshoot connectivity issues.
• Implement network security policies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in IT or CompE.
• Cisco certifications (CCNA, CCNP) are standard.

Job Description: Uses principles of chemistry, physics, and math to design, operate, and optimize large-scale industrial processes that turn raw materials into valuable products.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design chemical plant equipment (reactors, distillation columns).
• Develop and optimize large-scale manufacturing processes.
• Monitor process variables (temperature, pressure, flow) to ensure safety and quality.
• Also known as a "Process Engineer."
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Chemical Engineering (BSChE).

Job Description: Leads process design projects, manages plant optimization, ensures process safety, and mentors junior engineers in a manufacturing or energy plant.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead the design of new chemical production units.
• Perform advanced process modeling and simulation (e.g., Aspen HYSYS).
• Manage process safety (PSM) and conduct risk assessments (HAZOP).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSChE or MSChE.
• 5-10+ years experience.
• PE license often required.

Job Description: A specialized chemical engineer who focuses 100% on preventing chemical releases, fires, and explosions in industrial plants.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead Process Hazard Analyses (PHA) and HAZOP studies.
• Ensure compliance with OSHA Process Safety Management (PSM) regulations.
• Design safety systems (e.g., relief valves, shutdown systems).
• Investigate industrial accidents and incidents.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSChE.
• Expertise in PSM, HAZOP, and safety regulations.

Job Description: A specialist in chemical or materials engineering who focuses on the design, development, and manufacturing of polymer-based products (plastics, rubbers, fibers).
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop new polymer formulations with specific properties.
• Design manufacturing processes for polymers (e.g., injection molding, extrusion).
• Test the mechanical and chemical properties of plastics.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in ChemE, Materials Science, or Polymer Science.

Job Description: A research-focused role. Studies the properties and structures of materials at the atomic level to understand why they behave as they do.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct lab experiments on materials (metals, ceramics, polymers).
• Analyze material structures using microscopy (SEM, TEM).
• Research and develop novel materials with new properties.
• Focuses on the "why" (science) behind a material.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • MS or PhD in Materials Science required.

Job Description: An applied science role. Selects the optimal existing material for a specific application or develops new materials to meet engineering specifications.
Key Responsibilities:
• Select the best material (metal, composite, ceramic) for a product (e.g., a jet engine blade).
• Analyze material failure (failure analysis).
• Develop processes to manufacture materials with desired properties.
• Focuses on the "how" (application) of a material.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Materials Science & Engineering (MSE).

Job Description: A specialized materials engineer who focuses only on metals—their extraction from ore, processing (alloying, heat treating), and properties.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop new metal alloys.
• Design processes to extract metals from ore (extractive metallurgy).
• Analyze metal failure (corrosion, fatigue) and microstructure.
• Oversee heat treatment and finishing processes.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Metallurgical Engineering or MSE.

Job Description: A chemical or materials engineer who works in a "fab" (semiconductor factory), developing and controlling the complex chemical processes used to manufacture microchips.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage chemical processes like photolithography, etching, and chemical vapor deposition (CVD).
• Monitor process control data to maximize chip yield.
• Troubleshoot fabrication and contamination issues.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in ChemE, MSE, or Electrical Engineering.

Job Description: A ChemE who works in manufacturing to ensure that the final product (e.g., chemicals, pharmaceuticals, plastics) meets all quality standards and specifications.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and implement quality control test procedures.
• Use statistical process control (SPC) to monitor production.
• Investigate product failures and manage the CAPA (Corrective Action) process.
• Host regulatory and customer audits.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Chemical Engineering.
• Knowledge of Six Sigma, ISO 9001, and quality systems.

Job Description: A specialist (common in Pharma/BME) who formally proves and documents that equipment, processes, and systems work exactly as intended and meet all regulatory requirements.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write and execute validation protocols (IQ/OQ/PQ - Installation/Operational/Performance Qualification).
• Test manufacturing equipment and software to ensure they meet GMP/FDA rules.
• Create final validation summary reports for regulatory auditors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Engineering (ChemE, BME, ME).
• Extreme attention to detail and knowledge of FDA/GMP regulations.

Job Description: Designs, develops, and tests aircraft, spacecraft, satellites, and missiles. This field combines mechanical, electrical, and software principles for flight systems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design airframes, wings, and control surfaces.
• Perform aerodynamic analysis and structural testing.
• Integrate all subsystems (propulsion, avionics, navigation) into a working vehicle.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering.

Job Description: A specialist aerospace engineer who focuses on how air (or other fluids) flows over a vehicle, and the forces (lift, drag) it creates.
Key Responsibilities:
• Run complex Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) simulations.
• Conduct wind tunnel testing on scale models.
• Optimize the shape of a vehicle (plane, car, missile) to reduce drag and maximize lift.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MS or PhD in Aerospace Engineering required.
• Expertise in CFD software (e.g., ANSYS Fluent).

Job Description: A specialist aerospace engineer who designs and tests the engines that create thrust, such as jet engines, rocket engines, or ion thrusters.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design engine components (turbines, nozzles, combustion chambers).
• Analyze engine performance, fuel efficiency, and thermodynamics.
• Conduct hot-fire tests of rocket engines or jet engine ground tests.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Aerospace or Mechanical Engineering.
• Expertise in thermodynamics and propulsion systems.

Job Description: An electrical engineer who specializes in the electronic systems used on aircraft and spacecraft (aviation electronics = avionics).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and integrate communication, navigation, and flight control systems.
• Manage the complex wiring harnesses and data buses of an aircraft.
• Test and validate all electronic systems for reliability.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BSEE or BS in Aerospace Engineering.

Job Description: An engineer who plans, manages, and executes the testing of new aircraft to verify they are safe and meet all design specifications. Often flies on the test aircraft.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create detailed flight test plans and data requirements.
• Coordinate the test flight with pilots and ground control.
• Analyze telemetry data collected during the flight.
• Write the final test report certifying performance.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Aerospace Engineering.
• Must pass a flight medical exam.
• Requires strong nerves and communication skills.

Job Description: A "big picture" engineer who ensures all the different subsystems of a satellite or spacecraft (propulsion, thermal, power, comms) work together as a whole system.
Key Responsibilities:
• Define subsystem requirements and interfaces.
• Manage the spacecraft's "budgets" (mass, power, data).
• Oversee the full integration and testing (I&T) of the spacecraft.
• Plan mission operations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Aerospace or Systems Engineering.
• Broad knowledge of multiple engineering disciplines.

Job Description: A specialized ME or Aero engineer who uses Finite Element Analysis (FEA) to ensure the airframe of a plane or rocket is strong and light enough.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build complex FEA models of aerospace components.
• Analyze structures for stress, strain, fatigue, and vibration.
• Optimize designs to reduce weight while maintaining safety factors.
• Correlate analysis models with physical test data.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MS in ME or Aerospace preferred.
• Expertise in FEA software (e.g., Nastran, Abaqus).

Job Description: A Guidance, Navigation, and Control engineer. Designs the "brain" of an autonomous vehicle (satellite, missile, drone) that allows it to steer itself.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design control loop algorithms (e.g., for an autopilot).
• Model the vehicle's dynamics and simulate its flight behavior.
• Implement navigation systems using GPS, INS, and star trackers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MSEE, MSME, or MSAE required.
• Expertise in control theory, dynamics, and MATLAB/Simulink.

Job Description: Applies engineering principles to biology and medicine to design solutions for healthcare, such as medical devices, diagnostics, and therapeutics.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and test medical devices (e.g., glucose monitors, surgical tools).
• Research new biological materials or diagnostic methods.
• Ensure all products comply with FDA regulations.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Biomedical Engineering (BME).

Job Description: A BME or ME who focuses specifically on the design and development of mechanical or electromechanical medical devices, like pacemakers, infusion pumps, or prosthetic limbs.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design components for medical devices using CAD.
• Select biocompatible materials suitable for use in the human body.
• Manage the product development process through FDA approval stages.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in BME or ME.
• Knowledge of FDA regulations (Design Controls) and ISO 13485.

Job Description: A BME who works inside a hospital. They advise on the selection, manage the maintenance, and ensure the safe operation of all medical technology within the hospital.
Key Responsibilities:
• Evaluate and recommend new medical equipment purchases (e.g., MRI machines).
• Manage the hospital's medical device inventory and maintenance program.
• Train clinical staff (doctors, nurses) on the safe use of equipment.
• Ensure all devices comply with healthcare safety standards.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in BME.
• Often holds a CCE (Certified Clinical Engineer) certification.

Job Description: A specialist who applies classical mechanics to biological systems. They study the motion and structure of human bodies, organs, and cells.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and test prosthetic limbs and artificial joints (orthopedics).
• Analyze human movement (gait analysis) for sports or rehabilitation.
• Model the mechanics of human tissue (e.g., arteries, bone).
Required Skills & Qualifications: • MS or PhD in BME or ME (Biomechanics focus) required.

Job Description: A research-focused role at the intersection of BME, biology, and materials science. Designs "scaffolds" and processes to grow or repair human tissues.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop biocompatible scaffolds that cells can grow on.
• Design bioreactors to culture artificial tissues or organs.
• Research and apply stem cell therapies for regeneration.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • PhD in BME or Chemical Engineering required. (Highly advanced research field).

Job Description: A BME who ensures that medical devices are designed and manufactured in full compliance with FDA regulations and quality standards (ISO 13485).
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the Design History File (DHF) required by the FDA.
• Conduct risk analysis (FMEA) on device designs.
• Handle the CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) process.
• Oversee all testing and validation to prove the device is safe and effective.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in BME or related engineering field.
• Expertise in FDA 21 CFR 820 and ISO 13485 regulations.

Job Description: An engineering-adjacent role (often held by engineers) responsible for managing the entire regulatory submission process to health agencies like the FDA.
Key Responsibilities:
• Prepare and submit all paperwork for FDA approval (e.g., 510(k), PMA submissions).
• Act as the primary contact with regulatory agencies (FDA).
• Interpret complex regulatory guidelines for the engineering team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in BME/Science.
• Extreme attention to detail.
• RAC (Regulatory Affairs Certification) is highly valued.

Job Description: An engineer focused on optimizing complex systems and processes to eliminate waste. They make processes faster, safer, more efficient, and more cost-effective.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze manufacturing or service processes to find bottlenecks.
• Use statistical analysis and modeling to improve efficiency.
• Design factory floor layouts for optimal workflow.
• Implement Lean Manufacturing and Six Sigma principles.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Industrial & Systems Engineering (ISE).

Job Description: A hands-on engineer who develops and improves the physical manufacturing processes. They are the expert on the machinery and tools used to make a product.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and implement assembly lines and work cells.
• Select and program manufacturing equipment (e.g., CNC machines, robots).
• Troubleshoot daily production issues on the factory floor.
• Write work instructions for assembly operators.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Mechanical or Industrial Engineering.

Job Description: A general title (used by ChemE, IE, or ME) for an engineer who owns and optimizes a specific manufacturing process from start to finish.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor process variables (inputs, outputs, yield, quality).
• Use statistical process control (SPC) to keep the process stable.
• Drive continuous improvement projects to reduce cost or improve quality.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Engineering (ChemE, ME, IE).
• Strong statistical and problem-solving skills.

Job Description: An engineer who designs and manages the quality systems for a manufacturing plant, focusing on preventing defects rather than just finding them.
Key Responsibilities:
• Implement and manage the Quality Management System (QMS), like ISO 9001.
• Use statistical tools (SPC, Gauge R&R) to analyze product/process quality.
• Lead root cause analysis (RCA) and implement corrective actions (CAPA).
• Manage supplier quality and audit suppliers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Engineering (IE or ME).
• Six Sigma (Green/Black Belt) certification is common.

Job Description: Manages the entire Quality department (including QEs and QC Inspectors). Responsible for overall product quality and regulatory compliance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead the QA/QC team and department budget.
• Serve as the primary host for all customer and regulatory audits.
• Maintain the company's QMS (Quality Management System).
• Report key quality metrics (defect rates, scrap) to senior leadership.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Engineering.
• 5+ years in a Quality role.
• Strong leadership and knowledge of quality standards.

Job Description: An IE who applies engineering and data principles to optimize a company's entire supply chain, from sourcing raw materials to final delivery.
Key Responsibilities:
• Use data modeling to optimize inventory levels and warehouse locations.
• Design packaging to reduce cost and damage.
• Analyze transportation networks and logistics data.
• Improve demand forecasting models.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Industrial & Systems Engineering.

Job Description: A high-level, interdisciplinary engineer responsible for the integration and life-cycle management of large, complex systems (e.g., a defense system, an airplane, a software suite).
Key Responsibilities:
• Define the requirements for the entire system.
• Manage the interfaces between different engineering sub-teams.
• Oversee the full integration and verification & validation (V&V) process.
• Manage system requirements throughout the project lifecycle.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Engineering (IE, EE, Aero are common).

Job Description: An IE who designs and analyzes the physical systems for moving and storing goods, including warehouse layouts, material handling equipment, and transportation networks.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and optimize warehouse layouts and picking processes.
• Select material handling equipment (conveyors, AGVs, racking).
• Analyze freight costs and optimize shipping routes.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Industrial or Supply Chain Engineering.

Job Description: Also known as an Ergonomics Engineer. Applies knowledge of human psychology and physiology to the design of products and systems to make them safer, more comfortable, and easier to use.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design user interfaces (physical or digital) to be intuitive.
• Design workstations to reduce repetitive stress injuries.
• Conduct usability testing with real users.
• Ensure products are safe and effective for human use.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Industrial Engineering or Cognitive Psychology.

Job Description: The senior leader responsible for all operations within a single manufacturing plant. Often an engineer by training.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage all aspects of plant operations: production, quality, maintenance, and logistics.
• Responsible for the plant's P&L, budget, and operational KPIs.
• Ensure a safe (OSHA compliance) and efficient work environment.
• Lead continuous improvement and capital expenditure projects.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Engineering (ME or IE) + MBA preferred.
• 10+ years of progressive manufacturing leadership.

Job Description: Uses principles of biology, chemistry, and engineering to solve environmental problems related to water, air, and land pollution.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design water and wastewater treatment systems.
• Develop systems for solid waste management and disposal.
• Monitor air and water quality to ensure regulatory compliance (EPA).
• Design pollution control systems (e.g., smokestack scrubbers).
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Environmental, Civil, or Chemical Engineering.

Job Description: A specialized environmental engineer who designs systems to clean up (remediate) contaminated soil and groundwater sites.
Key Responsibilities:
• Investigate the extent of site contamination (soil/water sampling).
• Design cleanup strategies (e.g., soil vapor extraction, chemical oxidation).
• Oversee the implementation of cleanup systems in the field.
• Prepare reports for environmental regulatory agencies (EPA).
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Environmental or Chemical Engineering.

Job Description: An engineer who designs and develops methods for extracting oil and gas from deposits below the Earth's surface.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and implement drilling operations.
• Select and manage drilling equipment and processes.
• Oversee the "completion" of wells to make them ready for production.
• Analyze geological data to find oil and gas reservoirs.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Petroleum Engineering.

Job Description: A specialized petroleum engineer who estimates the size of an oil/gas reservoir and determines the optimal strategy to extract the maximum amount of product.
Key Responsibilities:
• Use computer models to simulate reservoir fluid flow and pressure.
• Forecast future production rates and total recoverable reserves.
• Plan well placement and optimal extraction methods (e.g., water flooding).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Petroleum or Chemical Engineering.
• Strong simulation and data analysis skills.

Job Description: The primary project manager for drilling a well. They design the well path and manage the complex, high-cost drilling operation safely.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the well casing and select the drilling fluid (mud).
• Manage the on-site drilling rig operations and budget.
• Solve real-time technical drilling problems (e.g., high pressure, equipment failure).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Petroleum or Mechanical Engineering.
• Often works in remote, on-site rotational shifts.

Job Description: Researches and develops the processes, instruments, and systems used to derive benefits from nuclear energy and radiation.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and operate nuclear reactor cores and power plant systems.
• Develop protocols for the safe handling and disposal of nuclear waste.
• Ensure compliance with NRC (Nuclear Regulatory Commission) safety rules.
• Research nuclear fusion or medical applications of radiation.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS or MS in Nuclear Engineering.
• Must be able to pass extensive background checks and security clearances.

Job Description: Designs and oversees the installation of commercial-scale or utility-scale solar photovoltaic (PV) power systems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design solar array layouts and electrical configurations (inverters, wiring).
• Perform production modeling (using PVSyst) to forecast energy output.
• Select components and ensure all designs meet electrical code.
• Coordinate utility interconnection.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BSEE or BS in Renewable Energy Engineering.
• Proficiency in PVSyst and AutoCAD.

Job Description: A specialized ME or EE who designs wind turbine components (blades, gearbox) or manages the operation and maintenance of wind farms.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and analyze turbine blades, generators, and control systems.
• Analyze wind data to select optimal turbine sites.
• Manage the on-site operations and maintenance of a wind farm.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in ME or EE.
• Strong skills in mechanics, power systems, and data analysis.

Job Description: Applies engineering principles (mechanical, civil, chemical) to the challenges of agriculture and food production.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design agricultural machinery (tractors, harvesters).
• Design irrigation, drainage, and erosion control systems.
• Design food processing plants and storage structures (silos, barns).
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS in Agricultural Engineering or Mechanical Engineering.

Job Description: An engineer responsible for the design, operation, and maintenance of the mechanical systems on a ship, such as propulsion, power, and HVAC.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and manage a ship's engines, generators, and HVAC systems.
• Work aboard ships managing the engine room (Chief Engineer).
• Oversee maintenance and repair of all onboard machinery.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Marine Engineering.
• Often requires a US Coast Guard license (Merchant Mariner Credential).

Job Description: The engineer who designs the actual ship—its hull, form, and structure. They focus on hydrodynamics, stability, and structural integrity.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the hull shape to optimize speed and stability.
• Analyze the ship's structural strength against wave forces.
• Ensure the ship meets all maritime safety regulations.
• Focuses on the ship's design, while a Marine Engineer focuses on its internal machinery.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Naval Architecture.

Job Description: A physics/engineering specialist who designs optical components and systems, such as lenses, lasers, telescopes, fiber optics, and camera systems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design lens assemblies and optical instruments using software (e.g., ZEMAX).
• Develop and test lasers and fiber optic communication systems.
• Design illumination and imaging systems for machine vision.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • MS or PhD in Optical Engineering or Physics required.

Job Description: An engineer (often ME) who specializes in the science of sound and vibration. They work to control noise, improve sound quality, or use sound for analysis.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design building interiors (theaters, concert halls) for optimal sound.
• Design solutions to reduce noise from machinery, highways, or aircraft.
• Work on audio products (speakers, microphones).
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in ME or Physics (Acoustics focus).

Job Description: Manages the schedule, budget, and scope of a specific engineering project, coordinating between different engineering teams (ME, EE, SW) and the client.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and maintain the project schedule, budget, and resource plan.
• Act as the primary point of communication for the project.
• Identify and mitigate project risks.
• Does not typically manage the engineers directly (that is the functional manager).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• BS in Engineering.
• PMP (Project Management Professional) certification is highly valued.

Job Description: A senior executive who manages all engineering departments or functions. Responsible for technical strategy, team leadership, budgeting, and execution.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the entire engineering department, including all functional managers.
• Develop the long-term technical roadmap for the company.
• Hire, mentor, and lead the entire engineering team.
• Responsible for the departmental budget and successful product delivery.
Required Skills & Qualifications: • BS or MS in Engineering + MBA (often).

Job Description: A primary care physician who provides comprehensive, continuous healthcare for individuals and families, serving as the first point of contact for a wide variety of medical issues.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and treat acute illnesses (e.g., infections, injuries).
• Manage chronic conditions (e.g., diabetes, hypertension).
• Provide preventative care, health screenings, and immunizations.
• Refer patients to medical specialists for further treatment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Broad medical diagnostic skills, communication, empathy, problem-solving.
• Qualifications: Medical Degree (M.D. or D.O.), completion of a Family Medicine Residency, and state medical license.

Job Description: A physician who performs a wide range of surgical operations, often focusing on the abdominal area, to treat diseases, injuries, and deformities.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose conditions that require surgical intervention.
• Perform operations such as appendectomies, hernia repairs, and cholecystectomies.
• Provide pre-operative and post-operative patient care.
• Lead the surgical team in the operating room.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Exceptional manual dexterity, stamina, leadership, decision-making under pressure.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., 5-7 year General Surgery Residency, state license, and board certification.

Job Description: A highly specialized physician who diagnoses and surgically treats disorders of the central and peripheral nervous system, including the brain, spinal cord, and nerves.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform complex operations on the brain and spinal cord (e.g., tumor removal, aneurysm clipping, spinal fusion).
• Diagnose neurological conditions using MRI, CT scans, and other tests.
• Manage critical pre- and post-operative neurological care.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Elite precision and dexterity, spatial reasoning, intense focus, problem-solving, resilience.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., 7-8 year Neurosurgery Residency, state license, board certification.

Job Description: A physician responsible for administering anesthesia to patients before, during, and after surgical or medical procedures, while monitoring vital signs and managing patient pain.
Key Responsibilities:
• Evaluate patients pre-operatively to create an anesthetic plan.
• Administer general, regional, or local anesthesia.
• Monitor patient vital signs (heart rate, breathing, blood pressure) intra-operatively.
• Manage patient pain and recovery immediately post-surgery (in the PACU).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of pharmacology, vigilance, critical thinking, calmness under pressure.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Anesthesiology Residency, state license, board certification.

Job Description: A physician who diagnoses and treats diseases of the heart and cardiovascular system using non-surgical methods, such as medication, diet, and lifestyle changes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose heart conditions using stress tests, echocardiograms (ECGs), and monitors.
• Prescribe medications to treat conditions like high blood pressure and high cholesterol.
• Manage patients with chronic heart disease and heart failure.
• Counsel patients on preventative heart health.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced diagnostic interpretation, pharmacology, patient education, analytical skills.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Internal Medicine Residency followed by a Cardiology Fellowship, board certification.

Job Description: A cardiologist with additional training who performs catheter-based, minimally invasive procedures to treat structural heart diseases, such as blocked arteries.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform angioplasty and stent placements to open blocked coronary arteries.
• Use catheters to repair heart valves (e.g., TAVR).
• Diagnose blockages using cardiac catheterization (angiograms).
• Manage patient care before and after invasive procedures.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: High-level dexterity, problem-solving, spatial reasoning, steady hands under pressure.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Cardiology Fellowship followed by an Interventional Cardiology Fellowship, board certification.

Job Description: A physician who specializes in diagnosing and treating diseases and injuries using medical imaging techniques, such as X-rays, CT scans, MRIs, and ultrasound.
Key Responsibilities:
• Interpret and read diagnostic images from X-rays, MRIs, CT scans, etc.
• Write detailed reports of findings to provide diagnoses to other physicians.
• Perform image-guided procedures (e.g., biopsies, draining fluids).
• Consult with other physicians on appropriate imaging tests.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Exceptional visual detail, analytical reasoning, pattern recognition, medical knowledge.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Radiology Residency (often followed by a specialization fellowship), state license.

Job Description: A physician who diagnoses and treats cancer using chemotherapy, hormone therapy, targeted therapy, and immunotherapy. They are the main care coordinator for cancer patients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose cancer and determine its stage.
• Create comprehensive cancer treatment plans.
• Administer and manage chemotherapy and immunotherapy infusions.
• Coordinate care with surgical oncologists and radiation oncologists.
• Manage patient symptoms and treatment side effects.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of pharmacology, empathy, communication, long-term patient management.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Internal Medicine Residency followed by an Oncology Fellowship, board certification.

Job Description: A physician specializing in the medical care of infants, children, and adolescents, providing preventative care and treating common illnesses and injuries.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct "well-child" check-ups, monitoring growth and development.
• Administer all scheduled immunizations.
• Diagnose and treat common childhood illnesses (colds, infections, rashes).
• Advise parents on child nutrition, safety, and behavior.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Strong rapport with children, communication with parents, patience, broad diagnostic skills.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Pediatrics Residency, state license, board certification.

Job Description: A physician specializing in two areas: obstetrics (pregnancy, childbirth, and postpartum care) and gynecology (female reproductive health).
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide prenatal care to pregnant women and deliver babies (vaginally or via C-section).
• Perform routine gynecological exams (e.g., Pap smears, pelvic exams).
• Diagnose and treat conditions of the female reproductive system.
• Perform surgeries such as hysterectomies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Stamina, surgical skills, communication, empathy, ability to work long/unpredictable hours.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., OB/GYN Residency, state license, board certification.

Job Description: A physician who diagnoses and treats conditions related to the skin, hair, and nails, from acne and eczema to skin cancer.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and treat skin conditions (e.g., acne, psoriasis, dermatitis).
• Perform full-body skin cancer screenings.
• Surgically remove skin cancers, cysts, and moles.
• Perform cosmetic procedures (e.g., Botox, fillers, laser therapy).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Strong visual diagnosis, knowledge of pathology, precision surgical skills.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Dermatology Residency, state license, board certification.

Job Description: A physician (typically trained in internal medicine) whose primary professional focus is the general medical care of hospitalized patients. They work exclusively inside the hospital.
Key Responsibilities:
• Admit patients from the emergency department.
• Manage and coordinate the entire course of a patient's hospital stay.
• Collaborate with specialists and surgeons involved in the patient's care.
• Discharge patients, coordinating follow-up with their primary care doctor.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Broad diagnostic skills, care coordination, multitasking, communication, efficiency.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Internal Medicine or Family Medicine Residency, board certification.

Job Description: A physician who examines tissues, organs, bodily fluids, and autopsies to identify and diagnose diseases. They are the "doctor's doctor" who provides the definitive diagnosis.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose diseases by examining tissue samples (biopsies) under a microscope.
• Oversee clinical laboratories (blood banks, microbiology, chemistry labs).
• Analyze lab test results and ensure quality control.
• Perform autopsies to determine the cause of death.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expert diagnostic and analytical skills, pattern recognition, attention to detail, laboratory management.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Pathology Residency, state license, board certification.

Job Description: A physician specializing in diseases of the kidney. They manage patients with chronic kidney disease, acute kidney failure, and those requiring dialysis.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and manage acute and chronic kidney disease.
• Manage patient care during dialysis treatments.
• Treat kidney stones, hypertension related to kidney disease, and electrolyte imbalances.
• Oversee patients post-kidney transplant.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of physiology and internal medicine, long-term patient management.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Internal Medicine Residency followed by a Nephrology Fellowship, board certification.

Job Description: A physician specializing in the endocrine system—the glands and hormones of the body. They primarily treat metabolic disorders like diabetes, thyroid disease, and osteoporosis.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and manage Type 1 and Type 2 diabetes (medication, insulin pumps).
• Treat thyroid disorders (hyper/hypothyroidism, thyroid cancer).
• Manage metabolic and bone disorders (e.g., osteoporosis, pituitary disorders).
• Treat hormonal imbalances and infertility issues.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Complex problem-solving, deep knowledge of physiology and biochemistry, patient education.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Internal Medicine Residency followed by an Endocrinology Fellowship, board certification.

Job Description: An advanced practice registered nurse (APRN) with a master's or doctoral degree. NPs are trained in a patient-centered (holistic) model and can diagnose illnesses, manage treatment plans, and prescribe medication.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform comprehensive physical examinations and take patient histories.
• Order, perform, and interpret diagnostic tests.
• Diagnose acute and chronic illnesses and prescribe medications.
• Manage a patient's overall care (often as a Primary Care Provider).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced diagnostics, pharmacology, patient counseling, holistic care approach.
• Qualifications: MSN or DNP degree, active RN license, and national NP certification in a specialty (e.g., FNP, AGNP). Scope of practice varies by state.

Job Description: A licensed medical professional with an advanced degree who works collaboratively with physicians. PAs are trained in a medical (disease-focused) model to diagnose, treat, and prescribe.
Key Responsibilities:
• Take detailed patient histories and perform physical exams.
• Diagnose and treat illnesses; develop treatment plans.
• Prescribe medications.
• Assist in surgeries (a common specialty for PAs).
• Must work under the supervision or in collaboration with a physician (MD/DO).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Broad medical knowledge, diagnostic skills, adaptability, collaboration, procedural skills.
• Qualifications: Master's degree from an accredited PA program; must pass the PANCE certifying exam and hold a state license.

Job Description: An APRN with specialized training in anesthesia. CRNAs administer anesthesia and related care before, during, and after procedures, with a similar scope to an anesthesiologist.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform pre-anesthesia patient assessments.
• Administer all forms of anesthesia (general, regional, spinal).
• Monitor patient vital signs and manage airways during surgery.
• Provide post-anesthesia care and pain management.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep pharmacology knowledge, critical care expertise, vigilance, crisis management.
• Qualifications: MSN or DNP, prior critical care (ICU) nursing experience, CRNA program graduation, and national certification.

Job Description: An APRN specializing in women's reproductive health and childbirth. They provide comprehensive care to women before, during, and after pregnancy, focusing on low-intervention and holistic approaches.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide gynecological exams and family planning services.
• Manage prenatal care throughout pregnancy.
• Manage the labor and delivery process (for low-risk pregnancies).
• Provide postpartum and newborn care.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expertise in childbirth, patient coaching, empathy, clinical assessment.
• Qualifications: MSN or DNP, active RN license, graduation from a certified midwifery program, and national certification (AMCB).

Job Description: An APRN focused on improving patient outcomes and nursing practice within a specific specialty (e.g., oncology, pediatrics). They are expert consultants, educators, and researchers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide expert clinical care to complex patients within their specialty.
• Educate and mentor other nurses and nursing staff on best practices.
• Lead evidence-based practice initiatives and quality improvement projects.
• Conduct nursing research and develop patient care policies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Clinical expertise, leadership, mentoring, research, systems-thinking.
• Qualifications: MSN or DNP, active RN license, and national CNS certification.

Job Description: The foundational RN role, providing direct care to a diverse range of adult patients who are acutely ill, recovering from surgery, or managing chronic conditions in a hospital setting.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess patient conditions and monitor vital signs.
• Administer medications and IV fluids.
• Perform wound care and manage post-operative drains.
• Educate patients and families on disease management and discharge instructions.
• Coordinate care with physicians, therapists, and other staff.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Clinical assessment, time management, multitasking, patient education, teamwork.
• Qualifications: Associate's Degree in Nursing (ADN) or Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN); must pass NCLEX-RN exam and hold a state license.

Job Description: A critical care nurse who manages patients with life-threatening conditions. They work in a high-intensity environment requiring advanced monitoring and rapid intervention.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform continuous, advanced monitoring of patient vital signs and hemodynamic status.
• Manage complex life-support equipment (ventilators, CRRT, ECMO).
• Titrate (adjust) continuous IV medications (vasopressors, sedation).
• Intervene rapidly in life-threatening emergencies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced clinical assessment, critical thinking, calmness under pressure, technical proficiency, emotional resilience.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); requires extensive training and certifications like BLS, ACLS. CCRN certification is the specialty standard.

Job Description: A specialized nurse who treats patients experiencing trauma or acute illness in a fast-paced emergency department. They must rapidly triage patients and stabilize their conditions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform rapid triage to prioritize patients by severity of condition.
• Stabilize patients experiencing trauma, cardiac arrest, or stroke.
• Administer emergency medications and assist with urgent procedures.
• Manage a wide variety of medical conditions simultaneously.
• Handle high-stress situations and patient/family anxiety.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Rapid assessment, multitasking, adaptability, decisiveness, crisis management.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); BLS, ACLS, PALS certifications. CEN (Certified Emergency Nurse) is the specialty cert.

Job Description: A perioperative nurse who cares for patients before, during, and after surgery. They ensure the operating room is sterile and safe and advocate for the patient while they are under anesthesia.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as a "Scrub Nurse" (handling sterile instruments) or "Circulating Nurse" (managing the OR environment, documentation, and patient safety).
• Prepare the OR, ensuring all equipment is sterile and functional.
• Advocate for the patient's safety and needs during the procedure.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Meticulous attention to detail, organization, teamwork, adherence to sterile technique.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred). Requires specialized on-the-job training. CNOR certification is the standard.

Job Description: Provides care for women during labor, childbirth, and the immediate postpartum period. They monitor both the mother and fetus, coach the mother, and assist the physician or midwife.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor the mother's vital signs and the fetal heart rate during labor.
• Administer medications (e.g., Pitocin, epidurals) as ordered.
• Coach the mother through the pushing and delivery stages.
• Assist the provider with the delivery and provide immediate care to the newborn.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Patient coaching, fetal monitoring interpretation, empathy, ability to act quickly in emergencies (e.g., C-section).
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); BLS, ACLS, and Neonatal Resuscitation Program (NRP) certifications.

Job Description: A nurse specializing in the care of patients diagnosed with cancer. They administer treatments, manage complex side effects, and provide long-term emotional support.
Key Responsibilities:
• Administer chemotherapy, immunotherapy, and other treatments (requires special certification).
• Manage the complex side effects of cancer and its treatment (pain, nausea, fatigue).
• Educate patients and families on their diagnosis and treatment plan.
• Provide psychosocial and end-of-life care and support.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: IV proficiency, pharmacology knowledge, patient education, deep empathy, emotional resilience.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); specialized chemotherapy/biotherapy certification required to administer drugs. OCN® cert is the standard.

Job Description: A nurse specializing in the care of infants, children, and adolescents. They adapt nursing care to the unique physical, developmental, and emotional needs of young patients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform age-appropriate assessments and monitor development.
• Calculate and administer medications based on pediatric dosing.
• Provide care for common childhood illnesses and post-surgical recovery.
• Communicate with and educate parents/guardians.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Family-centered communication, patience, knowledge of child development, patient education.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); BLS and PALS (Pediatric Advanced Life Support) certifications. CPN cert is the standard.

Job Description: A critical care nurse who cares for newborn infants born prematurely or with life-threatening conditions like birth defects, infections, or breathing problems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide highly specialized critical care to premature and sick newborns.
• Manage incubators, ventilators, and complex feeding (IV nutrition, gavage feeding).
• Monitor the subtle changes in a neonate's condition.
• Support and educate anxious parents on their infant's care.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Extreme attention to detail, critical care skills, gentle handling, parent communication, emotional resilience.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); BLS and NRP (Neonatal Resuscitation) certifications.

Job Description: A critical care nurse who cares for patients in the immediate period after surgery as they recover from anesthesia. They are the first stop before a patient goes to a med-surg room.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor patients waking up from anesthesia, focusing on airway, breathing, and circulation.
• Manage post-operative pain, nausea, and vital signs.
• Stabilize the patient until they meet criteria for discharge to an inpatient unit or home.
• Assess the surgical site for immediate complications (e.g., bleeding).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Rapid assessment, airway management, pain management, critical thinking.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN preferred); typically requires prior ICU or ER experience. BLS, ACLS, PALS certifications.

Job Description: A nurse who provides care to patients (often elderly or post-operative) in their own homes. This role requires a high degree of autonomy and patient education.
Key Responsibilities:
• Travel to patients' homes to perform skilled nursing care (wound care, IV infusions).
• Assess the patient's condition and the home environment for safety.
• Educate patients and family members on how to manage their condition at home.
• Coordinate care with physicians, physical therapists, and social workers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: High autonomy, strong assessment and clinical skills, patient education, time management, driving.
• Qualifications: RN license; typically requires 1-2 years of hospital experience first.

Job Description: A highly experienced critical care nurse (ICU or ER) who provides care to critically ill patients during transport on a helicopter or fixed-wing aircraft.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide advanced critical care (managing vents, drips, monitors) in a cramped, mobile environment.
• Work with a flight paramedic to stabilize and transport trauma or critically ill patients.
• Manage all care autonomously, reporting to a medical director via radio.
• Perform advanced procedures as part of the transport team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expert-level critical care, adaptability, crisis management, communication, working in austere environments.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN); 3-5+ years of ICU/ER experience; multiple certifications (BLS, ACLS, PALS, NRP, trauma certs like CFRN).

Job Description: A leadership role responsible for managing a specific nursing unit (e.g., Med/Surg, ICU). They handle staffing, budgeting, and daily operations for their unit.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the hiring, scheduling, and performance evaluations of nursing staff.
• Develop and manage the unit's operational and capital budget.
• Ensure the unit is compliant with all hospital policies and regulatory standards (e.g., Joint Commission).
• Handle patient complaints and service recovery.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Leadership, budgeting, communication, conflict resolution, operational management.
• Qualifications: RN license (BSN required, MSN preferred); several years of clinical and charge nurse (leadership) experience.

Job Description: A senior clinical nurse on a specific shift who is responsible for the overall operations of the unit during that shift, acting as the team leader and resource person.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assign patient loads to the oncoming shift of nurses.
• Manage patient flow (admissions, discharges, transfers) on the unit.
• Act as the primary resource person for junior nurses with clinical questions.
• Handle immediate problems, emergencies, and physician concerns.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Clinical expertise, leadership, multitasking, delegation, problem-solving.
• Qualifications: Experienced RN with proven leadership skills; not a formal management role but the first step up.

Job Description: A nurse (often with an advanced degree) who designs, implements, and evaluates educational programs for nursing staff, ensuring clinical competency and adherence to standards.
Key Responsibilities:
• Onboard and train all new nursing hires.
• Develop and teach continuing education classes (e.g., new equipment, new policies).
• Manage annual competency validation for all nursing staff.
• Lead evidence-based practice initiatives.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Teaching and presentation, curriculum design, clinical expertise, leadership.
• Qualifications: RN license (MSN in Nursing Education highly preferred); clinical specialty certification.

Job Description: A licensed nurse who provides basic, supervised nursing care under the direction of an RN or physician. Commonly found in long-term care, clinics, and home health.
Key Responsibilities:
• Administer medications (oral, injection) and monitor for side effects.
• Perform routine nursing tasks (check vital signs, perform wound care, insert catheters).
• Collect patient data and report changes to the supervising RN.
• Assist patients with activities of daily living (ADLs).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Clinical skills, patient monitoring, reliability, following directions, time management.
• Qualifications: Completion of a 1-2 year LPN/LVN certificate program; must pass the NCLEX-PN exam and hold a state license.

Job Description: A healthcare provider who works under the supervision of an RN or LPN to provide direct, hands-on care to patients, focusing on activities of daily living (ADLs).
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist patients with ADLs: bathing, dressing, feeding, and toileting.
• Turn and reposition bedridden patients to prevent bedsores.
• Take and record vital signs (temperature, pulse, blood pressure, respiration).
• Report any observed changes in a patient's condition to the nurse.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Patience, physical stamina, empathy, communication, reliability.
• Qualifications: Completion of a state-approved training program (often a few weeks) and passing a state competency exam.

Job Description: A role similar to a CNA but often with additional technical skills training. PCTs are common in hospitals and may also perform phlebotomy and EKG tests.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform all CNA duties (ADLs, vital signs, patient transport).
• Perform phlebotomy (draw blood samples).
• Conduct 12-lead EKG tests.
• Assist with inserting or removing catheters and other minor procedures.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: All CNA skills + technical skills (phlebotomy, EKG), versatility.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; often requires CNA certification plus on-the-job training or a specialized PCT certificate.

Job Description: Provides care for patients (typically elderly or disabled) in their private homes, focusing on daily living activities and companionship.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist clients with ADLs (bathing, dressing, grooming).
• Perform light housekeeping, meal preparation, and grocery shopping.
• Provide companionship and assistance with ambulation (walking).
• Remind clients to take medications (but does not administer them).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Compassion, reliability, housekeeping, communication, patience.
• Qualifications: Completion of a state-approved training program and competency exam.

Job Description: The administrative coordinator for a hospital nursing unit. They manage the unit's front desk, communications, and non-clinical patient documentation.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the unit's phone systems, answering calls from patients, families, and doctors.
• Transcribe physician orders into the electronic health record (EHR).
• Organize and maintain patient charts.
• Coordinate patient appointments and transportation for tests.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Organization, multitasking, medical terminology, computer proficiency, communication.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; knowledge of medical terminology is critical (often learned via certificate or on the job).

Job Description: A licensed healthcare professional who helps patients recover from injuries or illnesses that limit their mobility. They diagnose movement dysfunction and use exercise and manual therapy to restore function.
Key Responsibilities:
• Evaluate a patient's mobility, strength, and range of motion to diagnose issues.
• Develop an individualized treatment plan of therapeutic exercises and stretches.
• Perform manual therapy (joint mobilization, massage) to relieve pain.
• Educate patients on injury prevention and at-home exercise routines.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of anatomy and kinesiology, manual therapy, patient education, motivation.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) degree; must pass a national (NPTE) and state licensing exam.

Job Description: A licensed therapist who helps patients with injuries, illnesses, or disabilities participate in the things they want and need to do. They focus on fine motor skills and adapting the environment to the patient.
Key Responsibilities:
• Evaluate a patient's ability to perform activities of daily living (ADLs) like dressing, cooking, or writing.
• Design treatment plans to improve fine motor skills and hand-eye coordination.
• Recommend and train patients on adaptive equipment (e.g., walkers, splints, modified utensils).
• Adapt the patient's home or work environment for better function.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Creativity, problem-solving, patient education, fine motor skill analysis.
• Qualifications: Master's or Doctoral degree in Occupational Therapy (MOT/OTD); must pass the NBCOT exam and be state-licensed.

Job Description: Assesses, diagnoses, and treats speech, language, social communication, cognitive-communication, and swallowing disorders in children and adults.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and treat speech disorders (articulation, fluency/stuttering, voice).
• Treat language disorders (aphasia in stroke patients) and cognitive-communication issues.
• Perform swallow studies (often with radiology) and provide swallowing therapy (dysphagia).
• Work with children on developmental communication delays.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Knowledge of linguistics and anatomy, patience, communication, diagnostic skills.
• Qualifications: Master's degree in Speech-Language Pathology; must hold the Certificate of Clinical Competence (CCC-SLP) and a state license.

Job Description: A specialized health professional who assesses and treats patients with breathing and cardiopulmonary disorders. They are experts in managing ventilators and airway emergencies.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage and monitor life-support ventilators for critically ill patients.
• Administer breathing treatments (e.g., nebulizers, inhaled medications).
• Respond to "Code Blue" (cardiac arrest) calls as part of the resuscitation team.
• Perform pulmonary function tests (PFTs) to diagnose lung disease.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Critical care, ventilator management, airway management, pharmacology, critical thinking.
• Qualifications: Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Respiratory Therapy; must pass NBRC exams to earn CRT or RRT credential and hold a state license.

Job Description: A licensed professional who works under the supervision of a Physical Therapist (PT) to help patients carry out their treatment plans and regain mobility and function.
Key Responsibilities:
• Implement the patient treatment plan created by the PT.
• Guide patients through therapeutic exercises and stretches.
• Observe patient progress and report findings back to the supervising PT.
• Administer modalities like heat, ice, or ultrasound.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Strong understanding of exercises, coaching/motivation, observation skills, physical stamina.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree from an accredited PTA program; must pass a national (NPTAE) and state licensing exam.

Job Description: A licensed professional working under an Occupational Therapist (OT) to help patients carry out plans that improve their ability to perform daily living and work activities.
Key Responsibilities:
• Implement treatment plans, guiding patients in exercises to improve fine motor skills.
• Teach patients how to use adaptive equipment (e.g., dressing aids).
• Monitor patient progress and communicate outcomes to the supervising OT.
• Document patient care.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Creativity, patient coaching, empathy, communication, observation.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree from an accredited OTA program; must pass the NBCOT exam to earn the COTA credential.

Job Description: Plans and coordinates recreation-based treatment programs for people with disabilities, injuries, or illnesses to improve their physical, social, and emotional well-being.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess patients' needs using interviews and observations.
• Design therapeutic recreation programs (e.g., adaptive sports, arts and crafts, community outings).
• Help patients reduce depression, stress, and anxiety through recreation.
• Improve patients' motor skills and social functioning.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Creativity, leadership, adaptability, knowledge of adaptive sports/arts.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Recreational Therapy; must pass exam to become a Certified Therapeutic Recreation Specialist (CTRS).

Job Description: Healthcare professionals who diagnose, manage, and treat hearing, balance, and other auditory disorders in patients of all ages.
Key Responsibilities:
• Administer and interpret hearing tests (audiograms) to diagnose hearing loss.
• Fit and dispense hearing aids and other assistive listening devices.
• Diagnose and manage balance disorders (vestibular issues).
• Provide auditory rehabilitation and counseling.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Diagnostic testing, knowledge of auditory technology, patient counseling.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Audiology (Au.D.) degree; state licensure is required.

Job Description: A professional who advises individuals and families affected by or at risk of genetic disorders. They interpret genetic test results and counsel patients on risks and options.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze family medical history to assess the risk of inherited conditions.
• Explain complex genetic testing options and results to patients.
• Counsel individuals and families on managing genetic conditions or risks.
• Provide supportive counseling to help patients make informed decisions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of genetics, empathy, complex communication, counseling skills.
• Qualifications: Master's degree in Genetic Counseling; must be certified by the American Board of Genetic Counseling (ABGC).

Job Description: Operates diagnostic imaging equipment, primarily X-ray machines, to create images of the body's structures. They position patients correctly and safely to capture clear diagnostic images.
Key Responsibilities:
• Position patients and equipment correctly to capture the specific images ordered by a physician.
• Operate X-ray, and sometimes CT or mammography, machines.
• Strictly adhere to radiation safety protocols (ALARA) to protect patients and self.
• Ensure the technical quality of all images captured.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Patient positioning, knowledge of anatomy, radiation safety protocols, equipment operation.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree in Radiologic Technology; must be certified by ARRT (American Registry of Radiologic Technologists) and licensed.

Job Description: A specialized Rad Tech who operates Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scanners to create detailed, cross-sectional images of the body using magnetic fields and radio waves (no radiation).
Key Responsibilities:
• Operate complex MRI scanners.
• Position patients correctly and screen them for any metal implants (critical for safety).
• Administer IV contrast dye (gadolinium) for certain procedures.
• Ensure image quality for the radiologist to interpret.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: MRI physics, patient screening (safety), anatomy, operation of complex software.
• Qualifications: ARRT (R) certification, plus advanced certification in MRI (ARRT-MR). Associate's or Bachelor's degree.

Job Description: A specialized Rad Tech who operates Computed Tomography (CT) scanners to create detailed, 3D cross-sectional images (slices) of the body using X-rays.
Key Responsibilities:
• Operate CT scanner and related equipment.
• Position patients for scans and administer oral or IV contrast agents.
• Reconstruct 3D images from the scan data for the radiologist.
• Adhere to radiation safety protocols.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Cross-sectional anatomy, CT physics, IV insertion, patient care.
• Qualifications: ARRT (R) certification, plus advanced certification in CT (ARRT-CT). Associate's or Bachelor's degree.

Job Description: Also known as an Ultrasound Tech. Uses high-frequency sound waves (ultrasound) to create dynamic images (sonograms) of the body's organs and tissues (e.g., abdominal, OB/GYN, cardiac).
Key Responsibilities:
• Operate ultrasound equipment and select the appropriate transducer (probe).
• Move the transducer over the patient's body to capture real-time images of organs or blood flow.
• Analyze the images for abnormalities and create a summary report for the radiologist.
• Specialize in areas like OB/GYN (fetal), cardiac (Echocardiographer), or vascular.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Exceptional hand-eye coordination, pattern recognition, anatomy, physics, visual detail.
• Qualifications: Associate's or Bachelor's degree from an accredited sonography program. Must pass registry exams (e.g., ARDMS).

Job Description: A specialized cardiac sonographer who uses ultrasound technology exclusively to create images of the heart's chambers, valves, and blood vessels to assess cardiac function.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform complex ultrasound scans of the heart (transthoracic and transesophageal echoes).
• Assess heart valve function, blood flow, and overall heart muscle movement.
• Calculate key data points (e.g., ejection fraction) for the cardiologist.
• Assist in stress echo procedures.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of cardiac anatomy/physiology, ultrasound physics, pattern recognition.
• Qualifications: Accredited sonography program; must be registered (e.g., RDCS - Registered Diagnostic Cardiac Sonographer).

Job Description: A specialized sonographer who uses ultrasound to scan the body's vascular system (arteries and veins) to check for blockages, clots, or aneurysms.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform non-invasive ultrasound scans of arteries (carotid, peripheral) and veins (DVT studies).
• Measure blood flow and pressure using Doppler ultrasound.
• Create a technical report of findings for the physician to diagnose vascular disease.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Knowledge of hemodynamics, ultrasound physics, anatomy, attention to detail.
• Qualifications: Accredited vascular technology program; must be registered (e.g., RVT - Registered Vascular Technologist).

Job Description: Prepares and administers radioactive drugs (radiopharmaceuticals) to patients for the purpose of imaging specific body parts or treating diseases (like thyroid cancer).
Key Responsibilities:
• Prepare and handle radioactive materials safely.
• Administer radiopharmaceuticals (by injection, orally, or inhalation).
• Operate specialized cameras (Gamma cameras, PET scanners) that detect the radiation emitted by the drug to create an image.
• Adhere to strict radiation safety protocols.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Handling of radioactive materials, radiation physics, IV insertion, patient safety.
• Qualifications: Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Nuclear Medicine Technology; certification from ARRT(N) or NMTCB.

Job Description: An entry-level technician who operates electrocardiogram (EKG/ECG) machines to record the electrical activity of the heart, often as a pre-operative or diagnostic test.
Key Responsibilities:
• Place electrodes (leads) correctly on the patient's chest, arms, and legs.
• Operate the EKG machine to run a 12-lead tracing.
• Monitor the patient for any immediate, critical abnormalities during the test.
• Prepare the final EKG strip for the physician to interpret.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Patient positioning, knowledge of lead placement, basic rhythm recognition, attention to detail.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; certificate program or on-the-job training.

Job Description: Operates electroencephalograph (EEG) equipment to record the electrical activity of the brain. Used to diagnose conditions like epilepsy, sleep disorders, and brain death.
Key Responsibilities:
• Measure and mark the patient's scalp and apply dozens of electrodes.
• Operate the EEG machine to record brain wave patterns.
• Monitor the patient for seizure activity during the test.
• Prepare the lengthy recording for a neurologist to interpret.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Precise measurement, pattern recognition, patient care, technical proficiency.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree or certificate program; certification (R. EEG T.) from ABRET is the standard.

Job Description: Monitors and records patients during overnight sleep studies. They collect data on brain waves, breathing, oxygen levels, and heart rhythm to diagnose sleep disorders like sleep apnea.
Key Responsibilities:
• Prepare the patient for the sleep study, applying numerous sensors (EEG, EKG, airflow, O2).
• Monitor the patient from a control room throughout the night.
• Document sleep patterns, apneas (pauses in breathing), and other abnormalities.
• "Score" the sleep study data for the physician to review.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Patient monitoring, technology operation, pattern recognition, attention to detail (overnight).
• Qualifications: Certificate or Associate's degree; must pass the BRPT exam to become a Registered Polysomnographic Technologist (RPSGT).

Job Description: A trained clinical professional who collects blood samples from patients for laboratory testing, transfusions, research, or donations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Correctly identify the patient and verify test orders.
• Select the appropriate vein (typically in the arm) for venipuncture.
• Draw blood using sterile technique (needles, butterfly, vacutainer).
• Prepare and label all blood samples correctly for transport to the lab.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Venipuncture technique, patient communication (calming anxious patients), sterile technique, high attention to detail.
• Qualifications: Completion of a phlebotomy certificate program; national certification (e.g., PBT-ASCP) is standard.

Job Description: A laboratory professional who works under the supervision of a Medical Laboratory Scientist to perform routine diagnostic tests on patient samples (blood, urine, tissue).
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform routine lab tests in areas like hematology, chemistry, and microbiology.
• Operate and maintain automated laboratory analyzers.
• Prepare specimens and reagents for testing.
• Report test results in the patient's record.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Lab procedures, equipment operation, attention to detail, adherence to protocols.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree in Medical Laboratory Technology; MLT (ASCP) certification is the standard.

Job Description: A senior laboratory professional (also called Medical Technologist) who performs complex diagnostic tests, validates test results, and solves problems with analyzers or samples.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform complex, high-level manual testing and analysis.
• Validate results from automated analyzers and investigate abnormal findings.
• Perform quality control and maintain lab instrumentation.
• Supervise and train MLTs and lab assistants.
• Analyze samples in specialized areas like blood banking or microbiology.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced analytical skills, troubleshooting, quality control, laboratory science, leadership.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Medical Laboratory Science or a related science; MLS (ASCP) certification is the standard.

Job Description: A key member of the surgical team who works in the sterile field with the surgeon. They prepare the operating room, pass instruments, and maintain a sterile environment.
Key Responsibilities:
• Set up the sterile operating room, arranging all necessary instruments and supplies ("setting up the back table").
• "Scrub in" and pass surgical instruments, sutures, and supplies to the surgeon during the procedure.
• Perform surgical counts (sponges, needles, instruments) before, during, and after surgery to ensure nothing is retained in the patient.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of surgical instruments, sterile technique (asepsis), anticipation (knowing what the surgeon needs next), focus.
• Qualifications: Completion of an accredited surgical technology certificate or Associate's degree program. CST certification is the standard.

Job Description: A critical support role responsible for the cleaning, decontamination, sterilization, and distribution of all surgical instruments and medical equipment used in the hospital.
Key Responsibilities:
• Decontaminate and clean all used surgical instruments.
• Inspect instruments for damage and assemble them into procedural trays.
• Operate sterilization equipment (autoclaves, gas plasma sterilizers).
• Manage and distribute sterile instrument sets to the operating rooms.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Extreme attention to detail, knowledge of decontamination and sterilization protocols, organization, operating complex machinery.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; specialized certification (e.g., CBSPD or IAHCSMM) is required.

Job Description: An advanced surgical role that assists the surgeon directly during the operation with tasks like tissue retraction, controlling bleeding, and suturing (closing) the incision.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist the surgeon with visualization by holding retractors.
• Control bleeding (hemostasis) using suction, sponges, and cautery devices.
• Assist with closing the surgical incision (suturing, stapling).
• Position the patient and handle surgical equipment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced surgical skills (suturing), anatomy knowledge, anticipation, stamina.
• Qualifications: This is an advanced role; certification (CFA/CSA) requires being an experienced Surgical Tech, PA, or RN (RNFA) with specialized training.

Job Description: A licensed medication expert who works within a hospital to ensure the safe and effective use of drugs. They compound medications, advise physicians, and monitor patient drug therapies.
Key Responsibilities:
• Verify physician medication orders for accuracy and appropriateness (dosage, drug interactions).
• Compound and dispense sterile medications (IV bags).
• Advise physicians and nurses on drug selection, dosing, and side effects.
• Participate in patient care rounds (as a clinical pharmacist) to optimize drug therapy.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep pharmacology knowledge, accuracy, sterile compounding technique, analytical skills.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Pharmacy (Pharm.D.) degree; state licensure. A post-doctoral residency (PGY1) is often required for clinical roles.

Job Description: A licensed pharmacist in a community setting (e.g., CVS, Walgreens) responsible for dispensing prescription medications, counseling patients, and administering immunizations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Dispense prescription medications and verify their accuracy.
• Counsel patients on the correct use of their medication, side effects, and potential interactions.
• Administer immunizations (flu shots, COVID vaccines, etc.).
• Communicate with physicians to clarify prescriptions.
• Manage pharmacy technicians.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Pharmacology, patient communication and counseling, attention to detail, management.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Pharmacy (Pharm.D.) degree; must pass NAPLEX and MPJE exams for state licensure.

Job Description: Works under the direct supervision of a licensed pharmacist to assist with the technical aspects of dispensing medication.
Key Responsibilities:
• Receive and enter prescription information into the computer system.
• Count, package, and label prescription medications.
• Manage pharmacy inventory and stock supplies.
• Handle customer transactions and insurance billing issues.
• (In hospitals) May prepare IV medication mixtures (compounding).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Accuracy, data entry, math, customer service, adherence to protocol.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; state licensure and national certification (e.g., CPhT) are typically required.

Job Description: A doctor who diagnoses and treats diseases, injuries, and malformations of the teeth, gums, and related oral structures.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose oral health issues using exams and X-rays.
• Perform restorative procedures (e.g., filling cavities, root canals, placing crowns).
• Perform surgical procedures like tooth extractions.
• Create treatment plans to maintain or restore patient oral health.
• Supervise dental hygienists and assistants.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Medical/dental knowledge, manual dexterity, precision, spatial awareness, patient care.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS) or Doctor of Dental Medicine (DMD) degree; state licensure.

Job Description: A licensed oral healthcare professional who provides preventative dental care. They clean teeth, examine patients for oral diseases, and educate on oral hygiene.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform dental cleanings by removing tartar, plaque, and stains (prophylaxis).
• Conduct initial oral exams and screen for oral cancer and gum disease (gingivitis).
• Take and develop dental X-rays.
• Apply preventative treatments like sealants and fluoride.
• Educate patients on brushing, flossing, and nutrition.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Manual dexterity, attention to detail, patient education, operation of dental tools.
• Qualifications: Associate's Degree in Dental Hygiene (required); must pass national board and state clinical licensing exams.

Job Description: Works chairside with the dentist, providing support during procedures. They prepare the treatment room, sterilize instruments, and perform patient-facing tasks.
Key Responsibilities:
