• Prepare patients for treatment and set up instrument trays.
• Pass instruments to the dentist during procedures.
• Operate suction hoses and other chairside equipment.
• Sterilize all dental instruments.
• Take X-rays and handle administrative tasks.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Multitasking, chairside assistance, organization, sterilization protocols, patient comfort.
• Qualifications: Certificate or diploma from a dental assisting program; some states require licensure/registration.

Job Description: A dental specialist who diagnoses, prevents, and treats facial and dental irregularities ("malocclusions"). They are experts in straightening teeth and aligning jaws using braces and retainers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and treat misaligned teeth and jaws (malocclusions).
• Design and apply orthodontic appliances (braces, clear aligners, retainers).
• Monitor the progress of treatment over months or years.
• Interpret X-rays and 3D dental models to create treatment plans.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of orthodontics, spatial reasoning, precision, long-term planning.
• Qualifications: DDS/DMD degree, followed by a 2-3 year specialized Orthodontics Residency Program.

Job Description: A medical doctor (M.D. or D.O.) who specializes in mental health. As an MD, they can diagnose mental illnesses, provide psychotherapy, and are uniquely able to prescribe medication.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose the full spectrum of mental, emotional, and behavioral disorders.
• Prescribe, manage, and monitor psychiatric medications.
• Provide psychotherapy (talk therapy) or coordinate with therapists.
• Manage the treatment of complex conditions (e.g., schizophrenia, bipolar disorder).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Medical diagnostics, pharmacology (psychotropics), counseling, active listening.
• Qualifications: M.D. or D.O., Psychiatry Residency, state medical license, board certification.

Job Description: A doctoral-level professional (Ph.D. or Psy.D.) who specializes in diagnosing and treating mental and emotional disorders primarily through psychotherapy (talk therapy) and testing.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose mental health disorders using interviews and observation.
• Conduct psychological testing (cognitive, personality) to aid diagnosis.
• Provide various forms of psychotherapy (e.g., CBT, DBT, Psychodynamic).
• (Cannot prescribe medication, except in a few states with additional training).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Psychotherapy techniques, active listening, psychological assessment, diagnostics, empathy.
• Qualifications: Doctoral Degree (Ph.D. or Psy.D.) in Clinical Psychology; completion of internship/post-doc hours and passing state licensing exams.

Job Description: A master's-level professional who provides mental health services (therapy) and case management. LCSWs focus on a "person-in-environment" model, addressing social and external factors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide individual, group, and family psychotherapy.
• Conduct psychosocial assessments to understand a client's challenges.
• Provide case management: connecting clients to resources (housing, food, healthcare).
• Act as a patient advocate and crisis intervention specialist.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Psychotherapy, crisis intervention, case management, empathy, knowledge of community resources.
• Qualifications: Master of Social Work (MSW) degree; completion of several thousand hours of post-master's supervised clinical experience; state licensure (LCSW).

Job Description: A licensed therapist who diagnoses and treats mental and emotional disorders within the specific context of family systems, couples, and relationships.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide psychotherapy to couples, families, and individuals.
• Focus on relationship dynamics and how family systems impact individual mental health.
• Diagnose and treat disorders within the family context.
• Mediate family conflicts and teach communication skills.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Systems theory, psychotherapy, conflict mediation, communication, active listening.
• Qualifications: Master's degree in Marriage and Family Therapy; completion of extensive supervised clinical hours and state licensure (LMFT).

Job Description: A counselor who works with individuals struggling with chemical dependency (alcohol or drugs) and other addictions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct intake assessments to evaluate the extent of a client's addiction.
• Lead individual and group therapy sessions.
• Develop treatment and relapse prevention plans.
• Provide case management and referrals to support services (e.g., 12-step programs).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Knowledge of addiction models, group facilitation, crisis intervention, empathy, firm boundaries.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree; state licensure/certification (e.g., CADC, LADC) is required.

Job Description: An entry-level role in mental health (often in residential or inpatient facilities) who provides direct support and supervision to patients, monitoring their safety and behavior.
Key Responsibilities:
• Monitor patient behavior, safety, and activities in an inpatient or residential setting.
• Assist patients with activities of daily living.
• Co-facilitate group sessions (under the supervision of a licensed therapist).
• De-escalate agitated patients and maintain a safe, therapeutic environment.
• Document patient behavior and interactions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Patience, de-escalation techniques, observation, strong interpersonal boundaries, communication.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; certificate or Associate's degree in behavioral health is common. On-the-job training.

Job Description: A C-suite executive responsible for the entire strategic, financial, and operational management of a hospital or healthcare system. A non-clinical, business leadership role.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop the hospital's long-term strategic plan and financial goals.
• Manage the hospital's budget, profitability, and resource allocation.
• Oversee all department heads (clinical and non-clinical).
• Ensure compliance with all healthcare laws and regulatory bodies.
• Represent the hospital to the board of directors and the community.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Executive leadership, financial acumen, strategic planning, regulatory knowledge, operations management.
• Qualifications: Master's degree in Health Administration (MHA) or Business (MBA) is required. Extensive senior leadership experience.

Job Description: The administrator responsible for all business operations of an outpatient medical practice or clinic (e.g., a doctor's office or specialty clinic).
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage all non-clinical office staff (front desk, billers, medical assistants).
• Manage patient scheduling, billing, and revenue cycle.
• Oversee the office budget, payroll, and supply ordering.
• Ensure the clinic is compliant with HIPAA and OSHA regulations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Operations management, leadership, medical billing, budgeting, scheduling, compliance.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Business or Healthcare Admin preferred. Experience in a medical office is essential.

Job Description: A senior management role (often an experienced RN) who oversees multiple nursing units or an entire clinical service line (e.g., Director of Surgical Services, Director of Women's Health).
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide strategic and operational leadership for a clinical division.
• Manage all Nurse Managers within that division.
• Develop the division's budget, staffing models, and quality improvement goals.
• Ensure clinical practice standards are met across all units.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Senior leadership, clinical expertise, financial acumen, strategic planning, operations.
• Qualifications: Active RN License; BSN required, MSN or MHA/MBA strongly preferred. Extensive nurse management experience.

Job Description: A licensed executive who manages the complete operation of a long-term care facility (nursing home), including patient care, administration, and finances.
Key Responsibilities:
• Oversee all aspects of resident care and clinical services.
• Manage facility staff, budget, and building operations.
• Ensure strict compliance with all state and federal regulations (this is a primary focus).
• Manage admissions and community relations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Regulatory compliance, leadership, budgeting, knowledge of gerontology, operations.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree; must complete a specialized training program and pass a national exam to earn a state Administrator license (NHA).

Job Description: An administrator who ensures a healthcare organization follows all external laws and regulations (e.g., HIPAA, Medicare/Medicaid billing rules, Stark Law).
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and implement internal compliance policies and procedures.
• Conduct internal audits to identify and mitigate risks.
• Act as the organization's HIPAA Privacy Officer.
• Investigate and respond to all compliance complaints or violations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of healthcare law, risk assessment, auditing, ethics, attention to detail.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree; certification (e.g., CHC - Certified in Healthcare Compliance) is the standard.

Job Description: A specialist who identifies and mitigates any risk that could lead to patient harm or financial loss (e.g., lawsuits, injuries) for the hospital.
Key Responsibilities:
• Investigate patient safety incidents, medication errors, and patient falls.
• Analyze incident data to identify trends and implement preventative strategies.
• Manage malpractice claims and liaise with legal counsel.
• Oversee patient safety programs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Risk assessment, investigative skills, knowledge of healthcare law, data analysis.
• Qualifications: Clinical background (RN or JD) preferred. Certification in healthcare risk management (CPHRM).

Job Description: Organizes and manages all operational aspects of a clinical trial (a research study for a new drug or device) at a hospital or research site.
Key Responsibilities:
• Recruit, screen, and enroll patients for clinical trial participation.
• Schedule all patient visits, tests, and procedures according to the study protocol.
• Collect and enter all clinical data accurately ("clean data").
• Ensure the trial is conducted according to FDA regulations and ethical guidelines (IRB).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Extreme organization, attention to detail, knowledge of medical terminology and research protocols (GCP).
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in a life science is common. CCRC certification is the professional standard.

Job Description: Manages the patient experience and service recovery. This role handles all formal patient complaints, grievances, and compliments to improve satisfaction.
Key Responsibilities:
• Act as the primary point of contact for all patient grievances and formal complaints.
• Investigate patient complaints, interviewing staff and reviewing charts.
• Mediate conflicts between patients/families and the clinical team.
• Analyze complaint data to recommend systemic service improvements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Conflict resolution, de-escalation, empathy, investigative skills, communication.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree; clinical (e.g., RN) or customer service background is highly beneficial.

Job Description: An administrative specialist who translates patient diagnoses, procedures, and medical services into universal alphanumeric codes (ICD-10 for diagnoses, CPT for procedures) for billing and insurance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Review medical charts and assign the correct ICD-10 and CPT codes to every patient encounter.
• Create and submit accurate insurance claims to payers (Medicare, private insurance).
• Handle claim denials, rejections, and appeals.
• Ensure all coding is compliant to prevent fraud.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: High attention to detail, knowledge of medical terminology and coding systems (ICD-10, CPT), data entry, analytical skills.
• Qualifications: Post-secondary certificate. Professional certification (e.g., CPC or CCS) is the industry standard.

Job Description: Manages the systems and staff responsible for all patient data and medical records. They ensure data is accurate, secure, and compliant with privacy laws like HIPAA.
Key Responsibilities:
• Oversee the hospital/clinic's electronic health record (EHR) system.
• Develop and enforce policies for data security and patient privacy (HIPAA compliance).
• Manage the Medical Coding and patient records departments.
• Ensure the integrity and accuracy of all clinical data.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Data governance, knowledge of HIPAA, EHR systems management, leadership, compliance.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Health Information Management. Must pass exam to earn RHIA (Registered Health Information Administrator) certification.

Job Description: A technician (often with an associate's degree) who organizes and maintains patient health information data in EHR systems, ensuring all records are complete, accurate, and secure.
Key Responsibilities:
• Review patient charts for completeness and accuracy.
• Scan and index paper documents into the Electronic Health Record (EHR).
• Process requests for medical records from patients, insurance companies, and lawyers, ensuring HIPAA compliance.
• Maintain the integrity of the master patient index.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Attention to detail, data organization, computer proficiency, knowledge of HIPAA.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree in Health Information Technology. Must pass exam to earn RHIT (Registered Health Information Technician) certification.

Job Description: A role (often held by an RN or physician) that serves as the bridge between clinical staff (doctors/nurses) and the IT department to optimize the Electronic Health Record (EHR).
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze clinical workflows and help redesign them for efficiency within the EHR.
• Train and support physicians and nurses on how to use the EHR effectively.
• Help build and customize order sets, alerts, and documentation templates in the EHR.
• Troubleshoot system problems from a clinical user perspective.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Clinical workflow knowledge (must understand how a hospital runs), EHR systems expertise, communication, training, problem-solving.
• Qualifications: Clinical background (e.g., RN, Pharm.D.) is required. A master's degree in Health Informatics is common.

Job Description: Analyzes large sets of clinical, financial, and operational data to identify trends, measure quality outcomes, and find areas for improvement.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write complex queries (using SQL) to extract data from clinical and financial databases.
• Analyze data to track key performance indicators (KPIs) like patient readmission rates, wait times, or costs.
• Build dashboards (in Tableau, Power BI) for hospital leadership.
• Provide data to support quality improvement projects.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: SQL, data visualization (Tableau/Power BI), statistical analysis, Excel, understanding of healthcare operations.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Analytics, Health Informatics, or Public Health.

Job Description: A technical support role focused on maintaining the specific computer hardware, software, and networks within a healthcare environment.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide help desk support to clinical staff for computers, printers, and mobile devices.
• Troubleshoot issues with the Electronic Health Record (EHR) and other clinical software.
• Maintain network connectivity and data security (following HIPAA rules).
• Install and configure new computer workstations and medical devices.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: IT troubleshooting, customer service, knowledge of networking and hardware, understanding of HIPAA security protocols.
• Qualifications: Associate's or Bachelor's in IT. CompTIA A+ and Network+ certifications are common.

Job Description: The highest level of pre-hospital care provider in an emergency medical services (EMS) system. They provide advanced life support (ALS) to critically ill and injured patients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform advanced patient assessments and triage.
• Administer a wide range of medications via IV, IO, or injection.
• Perform advanced, invasive procedures (e.g., intubation, chest decompression, cricothyrotomy).
• Interpret EKGs and provide advanced cardiac life support (ACLS).
• Lead the ambulance crew and communicate with the receiving hospital.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced life support, critical thinking under pressure, pharmacology, invasive procedures.
• Qualifications: Completion of a 2-year Paramedic program (Associate's degree); state and national (NREMT-P) licensure.

Job Description: An entry-level emergency care provider. EMTs are trained in basic life support (BLS) and are responsible for stabilizing and safely transporting patients to the hospital.
Key Responsibilities:
• Respond to 911 calls and assess the patient's condition.
• Perform basic life support: CPR, bleeding control, administering oxygen, and immobilization (splinting).
• Operate an automated external defibrillator (AED).
• Safely transport the patient to the hospital via ambulance, assisting the Paramedic.
• Provide a verbal and written report to the hospital staff.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Basic life support, rapid assessment, calmness in emergencies, driving skills.
• Qualifications: Completion of a semester-long EMT-Basic course; state and national (NREMT) certification.

Job Description: A public health scientist who investigates the patterns, causes, and effects of diseases and injuries in human populations. They are "disease detectives."
Key Responsibilities:
• Plan and direct studies to investigate disease outbreaks (infectious) or chronic conditions (e.g., cancer, obesity).
• Collect and analyze public health data using statistical methods.
• Identify risk factors for diseases and methods of prevention.
• Present findings to policymakers and the public.
• Manage public health surveillance programs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Statistical analysis, data analysis software (SAS, R), research methods, communication.
• Qualifications: Master of Public Health (MPH) or a PhD in Epidemiology.

Job Description: A public health professional who designs and implements programs and strategies to teach people about health and wellness and promote healthy behaviors within a community.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess the health needs of a specific community or population.
• Develop educational programs and materials (brochures, websites, workshops).
• Teach community classes on topics like nutrition, smoking cessation, or managing diabetes.
• Advocate for public policies that improve community health.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Public speaking, curriculum design, program planning, communication, community outreach.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Health Education. CHES (Certified Health Education Specialist) certification is the standard.

Job Description: A licensed food and nutrition expert. They assess nutritional needs, promote healthy eating, and develop therapeutic diets for patients to manage medical conditions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess patients' nutritional needs and health conditions.
• Develop personalized meal plans and nutrition therapies (e.g., for diabetes, kidney disease, weight loss).
• Provide nutrition education and counseling to patients and families.
• Manage food service operations in hospitals or schools.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Knowledge of nutrition science and biochemistry, counseling, meal planning.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree (transitioning to Master's requirement) in Dietetics; completion of a supervised internship; must pass the CDR exam to become a Registered Dietitian (RD).

Job Description: A frontline public health worker who is a trusted member of a community. They serve as a bridge, connecting community members to health and social services.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct outreach in the community to find individuals needing services.
• Help people enroll in health insurance (Medicaid) or social programs (SNAP).
• Provide basic health education and translation services.
• Advocate for individuals and help them navigate the complex healthcare system.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Empathy, communication (often bilingual), knowledge of community resources, advocacy.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; certificate program. Primary qualification is being a trusted member of the target community.

Job Description: A healthcare professional focused on the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of neuromuscular disorders, with an emphasis on manual adjustment and/or manipulation of the spine.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess patient posture, spine, and reflexes to diagnose neuromuscular issues.
• Perform spinal adjustments (manipulations) to restore joint mobility.
• Advise patients on corrective exercises, ergonomics, and nutrition.
• Use other therapies like heat, ice, or electrical stimulation.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep anatomical knowledge, manual adjustment techniques, diagnostic skills.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Chiropractic (D.C.) degree (a 4-year post-graduate program); state licensure.

Job Description: A practitioner of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) who treats patients by inserting extremely thin, sterile needles into specific points on the body to stimulate healing and promote wellness.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose patient conditions based on TCM principles (e.g., tongue and pulse diagnosis).
• Select specific acupuncture points based on the diagnosis.
• Insert sterile, single-use needles and manipulate them as needed.
• May use other therapies like cupping, moxibustion, or herbal medicine.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Knowledge of TCM and acupuncture points, needle insertion technique, holistic assessment.
• Qualifications: Master's degree from an accredited acupuncture school; must pass NCCAOM board exams and be state-licensed (L.Ac.).

Job Description: A practitioner who blends modern scientific medical knowledge with traditional and natural forms of medicine, focusing on holistic, preventative care and the self-healing process.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose and treat conditions using a holistic approach.
• Prescribe natural therapies, including clinical nutrition, botanical medicine (herbs), and lifestyle counseling.
• (In some states) May prescribe conventional medications and perform minor surgery.
• Focus on identifying the root cause of illness rather than just symptoms.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Holistic diagnostics, nutritional science, botanical medicine, patient counseling.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Naturopathic Medicine (ND) degree from an accredited 4-year program; licensure varies widely by state.

Job Description: A primary eye care doctor (not an M.D.) who provides comprehensive eye exams, diagnoses vision problems and eye diseases, and prescribes corrective lenses (glasses/contacts).
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform comprehensive vision and eye health examinations.
• Diagnose refractive errors and prescribe eyeglasses and contact lenses.
• Diagnose, treat, and manage common eye diseases (e.g., glaucoma, macular degeneration, dry eye).
• Identify systemic diseases that manifest in the eye (like diabetes).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Diagnostic testing, refraction, knowledge of ocular disease, patient care.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Optometry (O.D.) degree (4-year post-graduate program); state licensure.

Job Description: A support service role responsible for the safe and timely movement of patients from one area of the hospital to another (e.g., from their room to radiology for an X-ray).
Key Responsibilities:
• Safely move patients via wheelchair, stretcher, or hospital bed.
• Ensure the patient is secure and comfortable during transport.
• Transport medical equipment (e.g., IV poles, oxygen tanks) with the patient.
• Clean and maintain transport equipment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Physical stamina, patient safety techniques, communication, reliability.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; on-the-job training and BLS/CPR certification.

Job Description: A frontline administrative role (also called Patient Registrar or Scheduler) responsible for handling all patient intake, scheduling, and registration for hospital or clinic services.
Key Responsibilities:
• Schedule patient appointments for clinic visits, tests, and procedures.
• Register patients upon arrival, collecting all demographic and insurance information.
• Verify patient insurance eligibility and benefits.
• Collect patient co-pays and deposits.
• Ensure all registration paperwork (e.g., consent forms, HIPAA notices) is complete.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Data entry accuracy, customer service, multitasking, knowledge of insurance terminology.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; certificate in medical office admin is helpful.

Job Description: A professional who works for a hospital or independently to help patients navigate the complex healthcare system. They act as a liaison, helping to resolve issues with care, billing, or insurance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Help patients and families understand diagnoses and treatment options.
• Investigate and resolve patient complaints or concerns about their care.
• Assist patients with appealing insurance denials.
• Help patients navigate scheduling and find community resources.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advocacy, communication, problem-solving, knowledge of the healthcare system, empathy.
• Qualifications: Varies widely. Many are social workers, nurses, or experienced administrators. BCPA (Board Certified Patient Advocate) is a cert.

Job Description: A certified professional who facilitates communication between healthcare providers and patients who speak a different language, ensuring accurate and clear understanding.
Key Responsibilities:
• Provide precise, consecutive interpretation of medical information between patient and provider (doctor, nurse).
• Must interpret complex medical terminology accurately, without adding or omitting information.
• Adhere to a strict code of ethics, including patient confidentiality.
• May also translate written documents like consent forms.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Fluency in two+ languages, deep knowledge of medical terminology in both languages, impartiality, short-term memory.
• Qualifications: Must be professionally certified (e.g., CMI or CHI), which requires passing rigorous exams.

Job Description: A professional (often an RN or Social Worker) who manages a patient's care progression, typically in a hospital. They coordinate care, plan the discharge, and arrange for post-hospital services.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess patient needs for post-discharge care.
• Coordinate with the medical team, patient, and family to create a safe discharge plan.
• Arrange services like home health nursing, physical therapy, or placement in a rehab facility.
• Liaise with insurance companies to authorize services.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Care coordination, knowledge of insurance and community resources, communication, critical thinking.
• Qualifications: Active RN license or LCSW license. Certification in Case Management (CCM) is common.

Job Description: A medical professional (doctor) who diagnoses, treats, and prevents diseases and injuries in animals, from household pets to farm animals and zoo animals.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform wellness exams, administer vaccinations, and provide preventative care.
• Diagnose illnesses and injuries using exams, X-rays, and lab tests.
• Perform surgery (e.g., spays/neuters, tumor removal, injury repair).
• Prescribe medication and counsel owners on animal care.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Broad medical and surgical knowledge (across multiple species), diagnostics, communication with pet owners.
• Qualifications: Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree (4-year post-graduate program); must pass the NAVLE and state licensing exams.

Job Description: The animal equivalent of a Registered Nurse. A licensed professional who assists the veterinarian with advanced clinical and technical procedures.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist the DVM in surgery (monitoring anesthesia, passing instruments).
• Perform dental cleanings (scaling and polishing) under supervision.
• Take X-rays, draw blood, and run laboratory tests.
• Administer medications and provide nursing care to hospitalized animals.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Animal handling/restraint, clinical skills (phlebotomy, catheter placement), anesthesia monitoring, anatomy.
• Qualifications: Associate's degree in Veterinary Technology; must pass the VTNE national exam and be state-licensed (LVT/RVT/CVT).

Job Description: An entry-level support role (similar to a CNA) who assists veterinarians and vet techs with daily tasks, animal handling, and clinic cleanliness.
Key Responsibilities:
• Safely restrain animals for exams, blood draws, and procedures.
• Feed, walk, and monitor hospitalized animals.
• Clean and sanitize exam rooms, surgical suites, and animal kennels.
• Prepare surgical packs and assist with inventory.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Animal handling, physical stamina, compassion, cleanliness, reliability.
• Qualifications: High school diploma; certificate programs are available but often trained on the job.

Job Description: The administrator responsible for all business operations of a veterinary clinic, allowing the veterinarians to focus on medicine.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage all non-clinical staff (receptionists, vet assistants).
• Manage clinic finances, budgeting, billing, and inventory purchasing.
• Handle marketing and client communication/complaints.
• Ensure clinic compliance with safety (OSHA) and veterinary regulations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Leadership, financial management, operations, customer service, inventory control.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's in Business preferred. CVPM (Certified Veterinary Practice Manager) is the specialty cert.

Job Description: A specialist DVM who diagnoses diseases in animals by examining tissue samples, bodily fluids, and performing necropsies (animal autopsies).
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagnose diseases by examining biopsies and tissue samples under a microscope.
• Oversee clinical laboratories (hematology, chemistry).
• Perform necropsies to determine the cause of death in animals.
• Work in research, diagnostics labs, or pharmaceutical safety.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expert diagnostic skills, pathology, microscopy, knowledge of animal diseases.
• Qualifications: DVM degree followed by a multi-year Pathology Residency and board certification (ACVP).

Job Description: Creates original works of art using paint as a primary medium. May work on commission, for galleries, or build a body of work for exhibition.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conceptualize and create original paintings.
• Prepare surfaces (stretch canvas, prime).
• Manage art supplies and studio space.
• Market work to galleries, dealers, and collectors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of a medium (oil, acrylic, watercolor).
• Strong understanding of color theory, composition, and art history.
• A professional portfolio is essential.
• BFA or MFA in Fine Arts.

Job Description: Creates three-dimensional forms of art using materials like stone, wood, metal, clay, or found objects.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and conceptualize 3D artworks.
• Manipulate materials using carving, modeling, casting, or construction.
• Manage studio safety, especially with tools and chemicals.
• Install large-scale pieces in galleries or public spaces.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in sculptural techniques (carving, welding, casting).
• Knowledge of materials and structural integrity.
• Physical stamina.
• BFA or MFA in Fine Arts.

Job Description: Creates original art by transferring images from a matrix (like a woodblock, metal plate, or stone) onto another surface, such as paper, allowing for multiple originals.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design images for print processes (e.g., etching, lithography, screenprinting).
• Prepare plates or screens using chemicals or carving tools.
• Operate a printing press to pull impressions.
• Manage editions of prints and maintain studio equipment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of one or more printmaking techniques.
• Precision and attention to detail.
• Knowledge of chemical safety.
• BFA or MFA in Printmaking.

Job Description: A specialized artist who creates representations of a person or animal. Works directly with clients (sitters) to capture their likeness and personality.
Key Responsibilities:
• Consult with clients to determine the desired style and medium.
• Capture a subject's likeness through live sittings or reference photos.
• Utilize drawing or painting skills to render features and expression.
• Manage commissions and client expectations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional drawing and painting skills, especially in realism.
• Strong understanding of human anatomy.
• Interpersonal skills to work with sitters.
• Portfolio of portrait work.

Job Description: An artist who designs and paints large-scale artworks directly onto walls, ceilings, or other large permanent surfaces, often for public art or private commissions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design large-scale compositions that fit the architecture.
• Pitch designs to clients or public art committees.
• Operate lifts, projectors, and scaffolding.
• Paint in various conditions using durable materials (e.g., aerosol, silicate paint).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Ability to work at a large scale.
• Physical stamina and comfort with heights.
• Strong design and drawing skills.
• Experience with large-scale projects (portfolio required).

Job Description: Provides technical and administrative support to an established professional artist (painter, sculptor, etc.), helping with the production of art and management of the studio.
Key Responsibilities:
• Prepare art materials (mix paint, stretch canvas, prep clay).
• Assist with art fabrication, sanding, or finishing.
• Manage studio organization, inventory, and supply ordering.
• Handle crating, shipping, and administrative tasks.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Practical knowledge of art materials.
• Strong organizational skills.
• Reliability and ability to follow directions.
• BFA degree often preferred.

Job Description: Creates visual concepts using computer software or by hand, to communicate ideas that inspire, inform, or captivate consumers. They create logos, websites, ads, and brochures.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design logos, brand identities, and marketing layouts.
• Use typography and illustration to meet a client's brief.
• Adapt designs for different formats (print, digital, social media).
• Prepare final design files for print or web production.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign).
• Strong knowledge of typography, color theory, and layout.
• A professional portfolio is mandatory.

Job Description: Responsible for the overall visual style and images in magazines, newspapers, product packaging, and movie/television productions. They unify the vision and lead other designers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop the overarching visual concept for a campaign.
• Lead and direct a team of graphic designers, photographers, and illustrators.
• Pitch visual concepts to clients or executive stakeholders.
• Ensure all visual assets adhere to a unified brand style.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Senior-level design expertise.
• Strong leadership, communication, and presentation skills.
• A portfolio demonstrating conceptual leadership.
• 5+ years experience as a senior designer.

Job Description: The highest-level creative role. They manage the entire creative department, develop the high-level strategic vision, and are responsible for the final quality of all creative work.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead the overarching creative strategy for a brand or agency.
• Manage and mentor Art Directors and senior creatives.
• Drive the creative vision for major multi-channel campaigns.
• Act as the primary creative voice to clients and C-suite executives.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• 10+ years of experience in design/advertising.
• Exceptional leadership and strategic thinking.
• Proven track record of successful, high-impact campaigns.
• Masterful presentation and persuasion skills.

Job Description: Designs the concepts and specifications for manufactured products (e.g., cars, electronics, furniture), focusing on their function, usability, ergonomics, and appearance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Research how consumers use a product.
• Sketch, model, and create 3D prototypes of new product concepts.
• Balance aesthetics, engineering requirements, and manufacturing cost.
• Use CAD software to create precise technical specifications.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• CAD software (Rhino, SolidWorks), 3D modeling.
• Understanding of ergonomics and manufacturing processes.
• Creativity and problem-solving.
• Portfolio of product designs.

Job Description: Plans and designs functional and aesthetically pleasing interior spaces for residential, commercial, or public settings. This is a technical role involving codes and space planning.
Key Responsibilities:
• Consult with clients to determine project scope and style.
• Create detailed floor plans, mood boards, and 3D renderings.
• Select all finishes, furniture, lighting, and materials.
• Ensure designs comply with building codes and accessibility standards (ADA).
• Manage project execution, coordinating with contractors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Space planning, CAD software (AutoCAD, Revit).
• Knowledge of building codes, materials, and furniture.
• Project management skills.
• Degree in Interior Design; professional license (NCIDQ) often required.

Job Description: A specialized graphic designer who focuses on creating the visual identity, branding, and physical container for a product, focusing on marketing appeal and functionality.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the graphics, typography, and layout for product labels and boxes.
• Consider the 3D form and "shelf appeal" of a product.
• Create mockups and prototypes for client approval.
• Ensure designs comply with all legal labeling requirements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in Adobe Illustrator/Photoshop.
• Understanding of 3D form, branding, and marketing.
• Knowledge of print production and materials.
• Portfolio of packaging projects.

Job Description: Designs and creates the layout, displays, and narrative flow for temporary or permanent exhibitions in museums, galleries, trade shows, or visitor centers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Translate curatorial concepts into a 3D spatial experience.
• Design the exhibit layout, traffic flow, and interactive displays.
• Create 3D models and renderings of the exhibit space.
• Collaborate with curators, fabricators, and lighting designers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Spatial awareness and interior design skills.
• Proficiency in 3D modeling (SketchUp, Vectorworks).
• Knowledge of materials, lighting, and fabrication.
• Degree in Exhibition Design or a related field.

Job Description: Focuses on the overall feel of a digital product. They are responsible for making a website or app functional, intuitive, and enjoyable for the user to interact with.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct user research and create user personas.
• Create user flow diagrams, journey maps, and wireframes.
• Build low-fidelity prototypes for usability testing.
• Analyze user feedback to identify and fix pain points.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Wireframing (Figma, Sketch), prototyping.
• User research methods.
• Empathy and analytical thinking.
• Portfolio of UX case studies.

Job Description: A specialized graphic designer for digital products. They focus on the visual look and feel—the "skin"—of a website or app, including buttons, icons, typography, and color schemes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design high-fidelity, pixel-perfect mockups of all app/web screens.
• Create comprehensive design systems and component libraries.
• Ensure brand identity is translated consistently into the digital product.
• Hand off precise visual specs to the development team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in UI tools (Figma, Sketch).
• Strong grasp of typography, color, and layout.
• Attention to pixel-perfect detail.
• Portfolio of visual design projects.

Job Description: A specialist who uses qualitative and quantitative methods to understand user behaviors, needs, and motivations, providing actionable insights to the design and product teams.
Key Responsibilities:
• Plan and conduct usability tests, user interviews, and surveys.
• Analyze quantitative data and qualitative feedback to find patterns.
• Create personas and journey maps based on data.
• Present research findings and recommendations to stakeholders.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Qualitative and quantitative research methods.
• Data analysis and synthesis.
• Empathy and strong communication skills.
• Degree in Psychology, HCI, or related field.

Job Description: Creates animated graphics, text, and visual effects for videos, commercials, websites, and presentations. They bring static designs to life.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and animate graphic elements, logos, and titles.
• Create data visualizations and explainer video animations.
• Composite visual effects into live-action footage.
• Work closely with video editors and art directors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in Adobe After Effects and Illustrator.
• Strong understanding of animation principles, timing, and typography.
• A professional demo reel is mandatory.

Job Description: The creative visionary who designs new collections of clothing, footwear, and accessories. They research trends, sketch designs, and select fabrics.
Key Responsibilities:
• Research and forecast fashion trends, themes, and color palettes.
• Sketch new designs (by hand or with software).
• Select fabrics, trims, and materials for a collection.
• Oversee the creation of prototype garments (samples).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Creativity and strong drawing skills.
• Knowledge of garment construction, textiles, and fashion history.
• A professional portfolio is essential.
• Bachelor's in Fashion Design.

Job Description: The "engineer" of fashion. They translate a designer's creative sketch into a technical blueprint ("tech pack") that a factory can use for precise manufacturing.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create detailed technical specification packets ("tech packs").
• Lead fit sessions on models to assess garment fit and function.
• Communicate precise fit corrections (e.g., "raise armhole 1/4 inch") to factories.
• Ensure sizing and fit consistency (grading) across all products.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of garment construction and patternmaking.
• Extreme attention to detail.
• Adobe Illustrator.
• Strong communication and problem-solving skills.

Job Description: A highly skilled artisan who drafts the 2D "pattern pieces" of a garment based on a designer's sketch. This pattern is the template used to cut the fabric.
Key Responsibilities:
• Draft original patterns (by hand, draping on a mannequin, or using CAD software).
• Create all pattern pieces needed to construct the garment.
• Adjust patterns based on feedback from fit sessions.
• "Grade" the pattern (scale it) to create all sizes (S, M, L).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of pattern drafting techniques (flat pattern, draping).
• Expert knowledge of geometry and human anatomy.
• CAD software (Gerber, Lectra) or expert hand-drafting skills.

Job Description: An artist who designs the prints, weaves, and knit patterns that appear on fabric. They work in fashion, home goods, and other industries.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design original 2D repeating patterns for fabrics.
• Create print designs using digital software (Photoshop, Illustrator) or traditional art.
• Develop new fabric textures through weave or knit structures.
• Work with textile mills to ensure proper color and production.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• High creativity and artistic skill (drawing, painting).
• Mastery of pattern repeats.
• Strong understanding of color theory.
• BFA or Textile Design degree.

Job Description: Works for a retail company and is responsible for selecting, planning, and purchasing the mix of apparel products that will be sold in stores.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze sales data and fashion trends to forecast customer demand.
• Select specific products and brands to sell for the upcoming season.
• Negotiate pricing, delivery, and quantities with apparel vendors.
• Manage the inventory budget (Open-to-Buy) to ensure profitability.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong analytical and quantitative skills (advanced Excel).
• Trend forecasting ability.
• Negotiation and financial acumen.
• Bachelor's in Fashion Merchandising or Business.

Job Description: Designs and implements the visual displays within a retail store, including window displays, in-store layouts, and mannequins, to create an engaging environment that drives sales.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and execute compelling window displays based on seasonal themes.
• Plan the store layout and product adjacencies.
• Style mannequins and create in-store displays per brand guidelines.
• Analyze sales data to determine the effectiveness of displays.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Creativity and strong styling skills.
• Spatial awareness and interior design knowledge.
• Understanding of retail analytics and consumer behavior.
• Degree in Visual Merchandising or Fashion.

Job Description: Selects the clothing and accessories for published editorial features, print or television advertising campaigns, music videos, concert performances, and public appearances made by celebrities.
Key Responsibilities:
• Consult with clients (celebrities, brands, magazines) on their image.
• Source clothing and accessories from brands and PR showrooms.
• Assemble and style complete outfits for photo shoots or events.
• Manage all garments and build relationships with designers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong knowledge of fashion history and current trends.
• Exceptional networking and relationship-building skills.
• Strong personal sense of style.
• Portfolio of work (e.g., editorial spreads).

Job Description: A person who works with a fashion brand's technical design team to test the fit, drape, and movement of prototype garments, acting as a live mannequin with the brand's exact sample-size measurements.
Key Responsibilities:
• Try on prototype garments during "fit sessions."
• Stand, walk, and move as directed by the technical designer.
• Provide verbal feedback on the garment's comfort and feel.
• Must precisely maintain specific body measurements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Specific, consistent body measurements that match the brand's sample size.
• Professionalism and patience.
• Ability to provide clear, objective feedback on garment feel.

Job Description: A specialized artist who creates stylized, elegant drawings of fashion designs or products, often used in editorial spreads, advertising, or as concept art by designers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Draw, sketch, or paint fashion figures and garments.
• Work with designers to visually communicate their concepts.
• Create artwork for magazine editorials, ad campaigns, or websites.
• Adapt their artistic style to fit the brand's image.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional drawing and artistic skills.
• A unique, recognizable artistic style.
• Knowledge of human anatomy and garment drape.
• Portfolio of illustrations.

Job Description: A research professional who analyzes cultural shifts, market data, and runway shows to predict future trends in color, fabric, silhouette, and style for the fashion industry.
Key Responsibilities:
• Research and analyze social, economic, and cultural data.
• Attend fashion shows, trade shows, and art exhibitions.
• Synthesize research into comprehensive trend reports and presentations.
• Advise design and buying teams on future product development.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong analytical and research skills.
• Intuitive and forward-thinking mindset.
• Excellent presentation and communication skills.
• Degree in Fashion, Marketing, or Sociology.

Job Description: Uses speech, movement, and expression to portray a character in a theatrical production, film, or television show.
Key Responsibilities:
• Audition for roles by performing monologues or reading scripts.
• Memorize lines, blocking (movements), and choreography.
• Research and interpret a character to develop an authentic portrayal.
• Rehearse and perform consistently under the guidance of a director.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Performance skills (vocal, physical).
• Memorization ability.
• Resilience and ability to handle rejection.
• BFA or MFA in Acting or extensive training; a "headshot" and resume are required.

Job Description: The primary creative leader of a theatrical production. They are responsible for unifying all elements (acting, design, text) to create a cohesive artistic vision.
Key Responsibilities:
• Select or be assigned a script (play or musical).
• Develop the central artistic concept or "vision" for the production.
• Cast the actors and lead all rehearsals.
• Collaborate with designers (set, light, sound, costume) to ensure a unified production.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong leadership and communication skills.
• Deep knowledge of dramatic text and theatre history.
• Ability to collaborate and synthesize ideas.
• Directing portfolio; MFA in Directing is common.

Job Description: The primary organizational and communications hub of a production. They manage scheduling, run rehearsals, and are responsible for "calling" all technical cues (lights, sound) during the live performance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create and manage the rehearsal schedule.
• Document all actor blocking and technical cues in a prompt book.
• Act as the primary liaison between the director, designers, and actors.
• "Call" the show by giving cues to light and sound board operators.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Extreme organization and attention to detail.
• Ability to stay calm under pressure.
• Strong communication and leadership skills.
• Certification or degree in Stage Management.

Job Description: Designs the lighting for a production. This involves selecting instruments, positioning, and color, and creating a "light plot" to create atmosphere, mood, and focus.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and collaborate with the director on the lighting concept.
• Design a detailed light plot specifying instrument type and location.
• Attend rehearsals and write lighting cues (dimming, timing, movement).
• Supervise the master electrician during programming and focus.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Deep knowledge of lighting technology and instruments.
• Understanding of color theory and mood.
• Software for drafting (Vectorworks) and console operation.
• Portfolio of design work.

Job Description: Responsible for all audio aspects of a production, including ambient soundscapes, pre-show music, sound effects (e.g., a doorbell, thunder), and microphone systems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and collaborate with the director on the audio concept.
• Source or create all required sound effects and background music.
• Design the speaker plot and microphone system for the theatre.
• Program sound cues into software (like QLab) for the operator.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in audio editing (Pro Tools, Logic) and playback software (QLab).
• Knowledge of acoustics, microphones, and speakers.
• A "good ear" and sense of timing.

Job Description: Designs all clothing and accessories (costumes) worn by the actors in a production, helping to define the character, period, and style of the show.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze the script to determine costume requirements (period, style, character).
• Sketch costume designs ("renderings") for each character.
• Source fabrics and materials; collaborate with the costume shop to build garments.
• Oversee costume fittings with actors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong knowledge of fashion history and garment construction.
• Excellent drawing/rendering skills.
• Creativity and research skills.
• Portfolio of designs; MFA in Costume Design common.

Job Description: Responsible for acquiring, creating, and managing all "props"—any object handled by an actor on stage, as well as set dressing (furniture).
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and create a detailed "props list."
• Source props by buying, renting, borrowing, or building them.
• Manage the props budget and workshop.
• Create a props "run sheet" for the stage crew to track props during the show.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Wide range of craft skills (woodworking, upholstery, painting, sewing).
• Extreme resourcefulness and organization.
• Budget management skills.

Job Description: Manages the technical operations of a theatre, primarily focusing on the build and installation of the scenery. They translate the Set Designer's drawings into a structural reality.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze the Set Designer's plans for feasibility and budget.
• Create technical draftings (construction plans) for the scene shop.
• Manage the scene shop budget, materials purchasing, and carpentry staff.
• Oversee the safe installation ("load-in") of the set into the theatre.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of structural engineering and construction.
• Advanced carpentry and welding skills.
• Budget management and leadership skills.
• BFA or MFA in Technical Direction.

Job Description: The artist who writes the script for a play, including the dialogue, stage directions, and dramatic structure.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conceive and write original dramatic texts.
• Create compelling characters, plot, and dialogue.
• Workshop new plays with directors and actors.
• Submit scripts to theatres, agents, and competitions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional writing ability, specifically in dialogue and dramatic structure.
• Creativity and persistence.
• Ability to accept and integrate feedback.
• MFA in Playwriting is common.

Job Description: The in-house literary and research expert for a theatre or specific production. They provide context, research, and critical analysis to the director, actors, and audience.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct in-depth historical and literary research for a play.
• Assist the director in interpreting the script's themes and structure.
• Write program notes and lobby display materials for the audience.
• Help the theatre select its season of plays.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Deep academic knowledge of theatre history and dramatic literature.
• Exceptional research and writing skills.
• Analytical and critical thinking skills.
• Master's or PhD in Theatre Studies or Dramaturgy.

Job Description: Designs and directs the dance movements and routines in a production, whether for concert dance (ballet, modern), a musical, a music video, or a film.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conceive and create original dance sequences and movement vocabulary.
• Audition and cast dancers.
• Teach choreography to performers and lead dance rehearsals.
• Collaborate with the director and designers on the overall artistic vision.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert-level training in one or more dance techniques.
• Strong creative vision and teaching skills.
• Understanding of music, rhythm, and spatial relations.
• Professional dance and choreography experience.

Job Description: A performer who uses their body and movement to express ideas and stories, typically as part of a company (ballet, contemporary) or in a commercial production (musical, music video).
Key Responsibilities:
• Attend daily technique classes to maintain peak physical condition.
• Learn and rehearse choreography from a choreographer.
• Perform routines precisely and expressively.
• Audition regularly for new roles or productions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Elite-level technical training in a specific dance style.
• Physical stamina, flexibility, and strength.
• Musicality, discipline, and resilience.

Job Description: Manages the audition process for a film, TV, or theatre production. They work with the director to find the best actors for each role.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and create "character breakdowns" (role descriptions).
• Schedule and run auditions, reviewing hundreds of actors.
• Recommend a shortlist of actors to the director and producers.
• Negotiate initial contracts and availability with agents.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Deep knowledge of the acting talent pool.
• Strong intuition for talent and character.
• Organization and negotiation skills.
• Member of the Casting Society of America (CSA).

Job Description: A highly trained instrumentalist who performs as part of a symphony orchestra. They must play their specific part perfectly in sync with a large ensemble.
Key Responsibilities:
• Practice their instrument (e.g., violin, flute, trumpet) daily.
• Rehearse complex musical scores with the full orchestra.
• Follow the conductor's direction precisely regarding tempo, dynamics, and articulation.
• Perform in public concerts.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Virtuosic mastery of a specific musical instrument.
• Exceptional sight-reading skills.
• Ability to play collaboratively within an ensemble.
• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Music Performance.

Job Description: The artistic leader of an orchestra or choir. They use physical gestures (conducting) to unify the ensemble, set the tempo, and shape the artistic interpretation of a musical work.
Key Responsibilities:
• Study musical scores in-depth to develop an artistic interpretation.
• Lead orchestra rehearsals, correcting timing, pitch, and dynamics.
• Conduct the ensemble during live performances.
• Often acts as the orchestra's Music Director, choosing the season's programming.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of music theory, history, and instrumentation.
• Expressive physical gesture and strong leadership skills.
• A "good ear" for pitch and harmony.
• Master's or Doctorate in Conducting.

Job Description: An artist who creates original music. This can include writing scores for orchestras, film/TV, video games, or writing songs for bands or theatrical musicals.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conceive and write original musical compositions (melody, harmony, rhythm).
• Notate music using software (like Sibelius or Finale) or by hand.
• Orchestrate music for specific instruments.
• Collaborate with directors, lyricists, or producers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of music theory, harmony, and orchestration.
• Proficiency with a primary instrument (usually piano).
• Creativity and discipline.
• Degree in Music Composition.

Job Description: Oversees the entire process of creating a song or album, acting as the creative project manager. They guide the artist's vision and manage the technical recording process.
Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with artists to shape the song's arrangement and instrumentation.
• Manage the studio recording budget and schedule.
• Coach the artist and musicians during recording sessions.
• Supervise the audio engineer during mixing and mastering.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong musical "ear" and understanding of song structure.
• Technical knowledge of studio recording.
• Project management and interpersonal skills.
• Expertise in a specific genre (pop, rock, hip-hop).

Job Description: The technical specialist responsible for recording, mixing, and mastering audio. They operate the studio equipment (microphones, mixing boards, software) to capture the highest quality sound.
Key Responsibilities:
• Select and place microphones to record instruments and vocals.
• Operate a digital audio workstation (DAW) like Pro Tools.
• "Mix" the track: balancing levels, adding effects (reverb, EQ), and polishing the sound.
• "Master" the final track for distribution.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Technical mastery of DAWs (Pro Tools, Logic Pro).
• Deep understanding of acoustics, signal flow, and audio effects.
• Patience and extreme attention to sonic detail.

Job Description: "Artist & Repertoire." This person works for a record label and is responsible for scouting, discovering, and signing new musical talent to the label.
Key Responsibilities:
• Scout new talent by attending live shows, listening to demos, and monitoring online buzz.
• Sign promising artists to recording contracts.
• Act as the artist's primary liaison within the record label.
• Guide the artist's creative and career development.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• A strong "ear" for commercially viable music.
• Networking and relationship-building skills.
• Negotiation skills.
• Deep knowledge of the music industry.

Job Description: Manages all the logistics for a musical act while they are on tour. They are the "road boss" responsible for travel, finances, and scheduling.
Key Responsibilities:
• Book all travel, accommodation, and ground transport for the band and crew.
• Manage the tour budget and distribute "per diems" (daily cash) to the band.
• Coordinate with venue managers to ensure all technical and hospitality needs ("riders") are met.
• Ensure the band gets to every show on time.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Extreme logistical organization and crisis-management skills.
• Budgeting and basic accounting skills.
• Stamina and ability to work long hours with difficult personalities.

Job Description: Manages the copyrights for musical compositions (lyrics and melody). They work to get an artist's songs placed in movies, ads, or recorded by other artists, and then collect the royalties.
Key Responsibilities:
• Administer the copyright catalog for songwriters.
• "Pitch" songs to film supervisors, ad agencies, and other artists.
• Negotiate synchronization (sync) licenses for media use.
• Track, collect, and pay out royalties to the songwriter.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong understanding of music copyright law.
• Sales and networking skills.
• Meticulous administrative skills.

Job Description: Creates the illusion of motion by sequencing images or digital models. 3D animators work for film/games (Pixar, EA), while 2D animators work on traditional or motion graphics.
Key Responsibilities:
• Use software (like Maya, Blender, or Toon Boom Harmony) to animate characters.
• Follow storyboards and direction to create character performance and movement.
• Manipulate digital models frame-by-frame to convey weight, physics, and emotion.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of animation software.
• Strong understanding of the 12 principles of animation, physics, and acting.
• Patience and attention to detail.
• A professional demo reel is mandatory.

Job Description: A visual artist who translates a script into a series of comic-book-like panels (a storyboard). This sequence visually plans the film, animation, or commercial, shot by shot.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and visualize the action.
• Draw quick sketches of key scenes, showing character position, camera angles, and motion.
• Collaborate with the director to refine the visual narrative flow.
• Ensure the story is clear and the pacing works visually.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional and fast drawing skills.
• Deep understanding of cinematic language (camera angles, composition, editing).
• Storytelling skills.
• Portfolio/Reel of storyboard work.

Job Description: A highly skilled designer who creates the initial "look and feel" for films, video games, or animations. They design the characters, creatures, vehicles, and environments before they are built in 3D.
Key Responsibilities:
• Brainstorm and iterate on dozens of visual ideas (sketches, speed paintings).
• Design characters, settings, and props based on the script or game design doc.
• Create highly polished "keyframe" paintings of important scenes.
• Visually solve design problems for the production team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Elite drawing and painting skills (digital or traditional).
• High level of creativity and imagination.
• Expertise in Photoshop.
• A stunning professional portfolio is required.

Job Description: The primary creative force behind a film. They are responsible for making all artistic decisions, guiding the actors' performances, and managing the overall vision of the movie.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop or select a screenplay.
• Define the artistic vision and style of the film.
• Collaborate with the cinematographer, production designer, and editor.
• Direct actors on set to achieve the desired performance.
• Oversee the editing process (post-production).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong leadership and communication skills.
• A clear artistic vision.
• Deep knowledge of storytelling, cinematography, and acting.
• A portfolio of directed short films or features (a reel).

Job Description: The Director of Photography. This artist is responsible for the "look" of the film, managing the camera, lighting, and composition of every shot.
Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with the director to translate the script's mood into a visual style.
• Select all cameras, lenses, and filters for the production.
• Design the lighting plan for every scene.
• Manage the camera and lighting crews during filming.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of camera technology and lighting techniques.
• A strong "eye" for composition and color.
• Leadership skills.
• A professional demo reel is required.

Job Description: Assembles raw video footage, audio, and graphics to create a polished, final product. They are storytellers who use technical skill to shape the pacing, tone, and narrative.
Key Responsibilities:
• Import and organize raw footage and media assets.
• Cut and sequence footage to create a compelling narrative.
• Perform color correction and audio mixing.
• Add graphics, titles, and effects.
• Export the final video in the required formats.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in editing software (Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, DaVinci Resolve).
• Strong sense of storytelling, pacing, and rhythm.
• Attention to detail.
• A professional demo reel is required.

Job Description: A digital artist who creates or composites photorealistic imagery that would be impossible or too dangerous to film in real life (e.g., explosions, monsters, fantasy environments).
Key Responsibilities:
• Integrate computer-generated imagery (CGI) seamlessly into live-action footage.
• Specialize in areas like modeling, texturing, lighting, or compositing.
• Use software (like Nuke, Maya, Houdini) to create visual effects.
• Remove wires, rigs, or unwanted objects from a shot.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of specialized VFX software.
• A keen eye for photorealism, light, and physics.
• Extreme attention to detail.
• A professional demo reel showcasing specific skills.

Job Description: The primary creative architect of a video game. They design the "fun," creating the rules, systems, story, and player experience of the game.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the core gameplay mechanics, levels, and narrative.
• Write the Game Design Document (GDD) to outline the vision.
• Script gameplay events and balance game difficulty.
• Collaborate with artists and programmers to build the game.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Creativity and strong analytical skills.
• Deep knowledge of game systems and player psychology.
• Technical scripting skills.
• Portfolio of game projects or mods.

Job Description: The author of the screenplay (script) for a film or television show, creating the characters, dialogue, and plot structure.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conceive and outline a story (plot, structure, character arcs).
• Write the complete screenplay in industry-standard format.
• "Pitch" ideas and completed scripts to producers and studios.
• Re-write and edit scripts based on producer or director feedback.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional writing ability, especially in dialogue and story structure.
• Creativity and persistence.
• Knowledge of screenplay format.
• Portfolio of completed scripts.

Job Description: Responsible for the entire auditory world of a film or game, except the music. This includes creating sound effects, recording dialogue (ADR), and mixing the final soundtrack.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create custom sound effects (e.g., footsteps, creature sounds, lasers).
• Edit and "clean" dialogue recorded on set.
• Oversee the recording of Foley (re-created sounds).
• Mix all audio elements (dialogue, effects, music) into the final "soundtrack."
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of DAWs (Pro Tools).
• Field recording skills.
• A creative and technical "ear" for sound.
• Audio engineering degree or equivalent experience.

Job Description: Creates original works of narrative fiction, such as novels or short stories, for publication and entertainment.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conceive, outline, and write long-form original narratives.
• Create compelling characters, plot, and worlds.
• Edit and revise manuscripts multiple times.
• Work with literary agents and editors to publish their work.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional creative writing and storytelling skills.
• Self-discipline and persistence.
• Strong grasp of language and narrative structure.
• A portfolio of completed manuscripts.

Job Description: An artist who uses language, rhythm, and metaphor to create works of poetry. They may publish in journals, perform live (slam poetry), or publish collections (chapbooks).
Key Responsibilities:
• Write and revise original poetry.
• Curate poems into collections (manuscripts or chapbooks).
• Submit work to literary magazines and presses.
• Perform poetry at live readings.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of language, metaphor, and rhythm.
• A unique creative voice.
• Deep knowledge of poetic forms and literary history.
• MFA in Creative Writing (Poetry) is common.

Job Description: Works for a publishing house and is responsible for finding, acquiring ("buying"), and developing books for publication. They are the author's primary advocate at the publisher.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read and evaluate manuscripts submitted by agents.
• Champion promising books and bid to acquire publishing rights.
• Perform developmental edits (big picture story/structure) with the author.
• Manage the book project from manuscript to final publication.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong editorial judgment and commercial instincts.
• Negotiation skills.
• Deep knowledge of the book market and a specific genre.

Job Description: A technical editor who reads manuscripts to ensure they are free of errors in grammar, spelling, punctuation, and syntax, and that they adhere to a publisher's style guide.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read text line-by-line to find and correct grammatical errors.
• Fact-check names, dates, and statements.
• Ensure consistent style (e.g., AP, Chicago Manual of Style).
• Prepare the manuscript for the proofreader and typesetter.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert-level mastery of grammar and style guides.
• Extreme attention to detail.
• Ability to focus for long periods.
• Editing certificate or relevant experience.

Job Description: The final quality-control checker. A proofreader reviews the "final proof" (the typeset version) of a book or article right before it goes to print, looking only for typos or formatting errors.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the typeset proof and compare it to the copy-edited manuscript.
• Catch any remaining typos, spelling errors, or formatting mistakes.
• Check for bad line breaks, pagination errors, or font issues.
• This is the final check before printing.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Absolute, meticulous attention to detail.
• Exceptional spelling and grammar skills.
• Patience and focus.
• Experience or certification in proofreading.

Job Description: An author's business representative. An agent scouts talented writers, signs them as clients, and then "pitches" their manuscripts to editors at publishing houses, negotiating the best contract.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read and evaluate query letters and manuscripts from aspiring authors.
• Sign authors and provide editorial guidance to polish their manuscript.
• Curate a list of appropriate editors and "submit" the manuscript.
• Negotiate contracts (advances, royalties, rights) on behalf of the author.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong editorial eye and commercial instincts.
• Salesmanship and strong industry network.
• Negotiation skills and knowledge of publishing contracts.

Job Description: The head of a publishing house (or a small press). This is the senior executive responsible for the overall business and editorial direction of the company.
Key Responsibilities:
• Set the overall editorial strategy and financial goals for the press.
• Manage senior staff (editors, marketing, sales directors).
• Oversee the company's budget, P&L, and major acquisitions.
• Act as the final decision-maker on which books to publish.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong business and financial acumen.
• Deep knowledge of the publishing industry and book market.
• Executive leadership and strategic planning skills.

Job Description: A specialized graphic designer who designs the entire physical book. This includes the cover design (artistic) and the interior layout/typography (technical).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the book cover to be visually striking and marketable.
• Design the interior layout: selecting fonts, setting margins, and creating the chapter styles.
• Typeset the manuscript into the final layout using InDesign.
• Ensure the final product is beautiful and readable.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in graphic design (InDesign, Photoshop).
• Mastery of typography and layout.
• Understanding of book binding and print production.

Job Description: An artist who rewrites a literary work (novel, poem) from one language into another, attempting to preserve the original author's tone, style, meaning, and cultural nuance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read and deeply understand a text in its original language.
• Re-write the text in a new language, balancing literal meaning with artistic intent.
• Research cultural contexts to ensure accurate translation of idioms.
• Collaborate with the original author or editor.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Fluency (native or near-native) in at least two languages.
• Strong writing and creative skills in the target language.
• Deep cultural knowledge of both languages.

Job Description: A professional writer and analyst who reviews and evaluates works of art (books, films, plays, paintings) for a publication (newspaper, magazine, website).
Key Responsibilities:
• Consume and analyze new works of art.
• Write articulate, insightful reviews that place the work in a broader cultural context.
• Develop a unique critical voice and build an audience.
• Provide informed opinions based on deep subject-matter expertise.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional analytical and persuasive writing skills.
• Deep, expert-level knowledge of a specific art form (e.g., art history, dramatic lit).
• Ability to meet tight deadlines.

Job Description: An academic specialist and content manager for a museum. They are responsible for acquiring, managing, researching, interpreting, and displaying the museum's collection.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct scholarly research on the museum's collection.
• Conceptualize, plan, and execute new exhibitions.
• Write text for exhibition labels, catalogs, and publications.
• Recommend new acquisitions (purchases/donations) for the collection.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Subject matter expertise (PhD or Master's in Art History, History, etc.).
• Academic research and writing skills.
• Project management.
• Strong professional network.

Job Description: An entry-level role supporting the curatorial department. Assists with research, administration, and execution of exhibitions.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct research for upcoming exhibitions and publications.
• Manage departmental correspondence, files, and budgets.
• Assist with creating exhibition labels and catalog entries.
• Coordinate loan paperwork and image rights.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• MA in Art History or Museum Studies (or in progress).
• Strong research, writing, and organizational skills.
• Foreign language proficiency is often required.

Job Description: The primary data and logistics manager for a museum's collection. They are responsible for all paperwork and logistics related to artworks, including loans, shipping, and storage.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the museum's collection database (tracking location, condition, provenance).
• Handle all legal paperwork for new acquisitions and outgoing/incoming loans.
• Arrange all packing, crating, shipping, and insurance for artwork transportation.
• Oversee the physical storage and safety of the collection.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Extreme organization and meticulous record-keeping.
• Knowledge of museum database software and art handling procedures.
• Understanding of legal contracts and insurance.

Job Description: The senior executive (CEO) of a museum. They are responsible for the overall strategic vision, financial health, and management of the entire institution, reporting to the Board of Trustees.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead the museum's long-term strategic planning.
• Act as the chief fundraiser, cultivating major donors and foundations.
• Manage the senior leadership team (Chief Curator, CFO, Development Director).
• Serve as the public face of the museum to the community and press.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Executive leadership and financial management skills.
• Proven fundraising and networking ability.
• Advanced degree (MA, PhD, or MBA) and extensive museum experience.

Job Description: A highly trained scientist/artist who preserves and restores damaged or deteriorating artworks. They use chemistry and art history to stabilize artifacts for the future.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze the physical and chemical condition of an artwork.
