• Perform highly delicate restoration treatments (e.g., clean a painting, repair torn canvas, re-assemble broken pottery).
• Develop preventative care plans for the collection (climate control).
• Document all treatments and conditions with written reports.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Master's degree in Art Conservation.
• Expert knowledge of chemistry, studio art, and art history.
• Extreme patience and manual dexterity.

Job Description: Manages and preserves collections of historical records, documents, photographs, and other media, making them accessible to researchers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Appraise, collect, and organize historical materials.
• Create detailed finding aids (guides) to help researchers navigate the collection.
• Implement preservation and digitization strategies for fragile materials.
• Assist researchers, scholars, and the public with their inquiries.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Master's degree in Library Science (MLS) or Archival Studies.
• Meticulous organization and research skills.
• Knowledge of database systems and preservation techniques.

Job Description: Manages the operations of a commercial art gallery. This role combines art expertise with sales and business management.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage relationships with the gallery's roster of artists.
• Plan and install new exhibitions (sales-focused).
• Conduct sales with collectors, clients, and museums.
• Oversee gallery marketing, staff, and daily operations.
• Represent the gallery at international art fairs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong sales and networking skills.
• Business management and operations skills.
• Deep knowledge of the contemporary art market.

Job Description: A highly skilled technician responsible for the physical packing, shipping, installation, and de-installation of artworks in a museum or gallery.
Key Responsibilities:
• Safely handle, pack, and crate valuable and fragile works of art.
• Install artwork for exhibitions (hang paintings, place sculpture).
• Build custom mounts, pedestals, and temporary walls.
• Load/unload art trucks and manage the art storage facility.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of art handling techniques.
• Skills in carpentry, rigging, and problem-solving.
• Physical strength and attention to detail.
• Experience working in a museum or fine art shipping company.

Job Description: The chief fundraiser for a non-profit arts organization (museum, theatre, orchestra). They are responsible for raising all contributed income.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop and execute the organization's annual fundraising strategy.
• Cultivate and solicit major gifts from individual donors and board members.
• Oversee grant writing, membership programs, and fundraising events (galas).
• Manage the development department staff and budget.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Proven fundraising track record and sales skills.
• Exceptional communication and networking skills.
• Ability to build relationships with wealthy donors.
• CFRE certification is a plus.

Job Description: A specialized writer who researches and writes grant proposals to foundations, corporations, and government agencies (like the NEA) to secure funding for an arts organization.
Key Responsibilities:
• Research and identify potential grant funding opportunities.
• Write clear, persuasive grant applications and proposals that match the funder's goals.
• Gather all supporting documentation (budgets, project plans).
• Manage grant deadlines and write final reports on how funds were used.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional persuasive writing skills.
• Strong research and project management skills.
• Ability to write detailed budgets.
• Experience in non-profit fundraising.

Job Description: Promotes an arts organization's programming (exhibits, performances) to the public with the goal of driving ticket sales and attendance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop the marketing strategy for the season or specific shows.
• Manage advertising campaigns (digital, print, social media).
• Oversee content creation (social media, email newsletters).
• Analyze ticket sales data and audience demographics.
• Work closely with the press/PR team to promote shows.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Digital marketing skills (social media, PPC, email).
• Data analysis and budgeting skills.
• Strong writing and communication skills.
• Passion for the arts.

Job Description: Manages all operations of a theatre's box office, overseeing ticket sales, customer service, and reporting.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the ticketing system (e.g., Tessitura, Ticketmaster).
• Hire, train, and schedule all box office staff.
• Provide high-level customer service and resolve ticket disputes.
• Generate daily, weekly, and final sales reports for the finance department.
• Coordinate with the marketing team on pricing and promotions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in ticketing software.
• Strong customer service and conflict resolution skills.
• Cash handling and financial reporting accuracy.
• Leadership.

Job Description: Responsible for the "Front of House" (FOH) experience during a live performance. They manage the audience, the ushers, and the lobby, ensuring the show starts safely and on time.
Key Responsibilities:
• Train and supervise all FOH staff (ushers, ticket takers).
• Manage audience seating, handle customer complaints, and find lost items.
• Ensure the lobby and theatre are clean and safe for the public.
• Coordinate with the Stage Manager to know when to "open the house" and start the show.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong customer service and crisis management skills.
• Leadership and communication.
• Ability to be calm and authoritative under pressure.

Job Description: An artist who creates functional (pottery) or sculptural objects out of clay, using techniques like wheel-throwing, hand-building, and glazing.
Key Responsibilities:
• Prepare clay and create forms (on a potter's wheel or by hand).
• Fire pieces in a kiln (bisque firing and glaze firing).
• Develop and test unique glaze formulas.
• Sell work at craft fairs, galleries, or online.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of ceramic techniques (throwing, glazing).
• Knowledge of kiln operation and firing chemistry.
• Artistic design skills.
• BFA or apprenticeship experience.

Job Description: An artisan who shapes molten glass into art or functional objects (like vases, bowls, sculptures) using a blowpipe and other specialized tools in a hot shop.
Key Responsibilities:
• Gather molten glass from a furnace (glory hole).
• Shape, blow, and color the glass using pipes, jacks, and other tools.
• Work collaboratively as part of a glassblowing team.
• Place finished pieces in an annealer to cool slowly.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Advanced glassblowing skills (learned via apprenticeship).
• Physical stamina and high tolerance for heat.
• Teamwork and precise communication.
• Strong artistic sensibility.

Job Description: A skilled artisan who designs, fabricates, and repairs jewelry. They work with precious metals (gold, silver, platinum) and gemstones.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design original pieces of jewelry.
• Fabricate jewelry using techniques like soldering, casting, stone setting, and polishing.
• Perform delicate repairs on existing jewelry.
• Appraise gemstones and metal.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert manual dexterity and precision.
• Knowledge of metallurgy and gemology.
• Skills in soldering, casting, and stone setting.
• GIA certification or apprenticeship.

Job Description: A craftsperson who designs and builds high-quality, often custom, furniture (chairs, tables, cabinets) from wood, focusing on joinery and fine finishing.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design original furniture pieces and create blueprints.
• Select, cut, and shape fine woods.
• Construct furniture using precision joinery (e.g., dovetails, mortise and tenon).
• Apply fine finishes (stains, oils, varnishes).
• Work on commission for clients.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert carpentry and woodworking skills.
• Mastery of joinery.
• Attention to detail and precision.
• Knowledge of wood properties and finishes.

Job Description: An artist who creates textiles (like tapestries, rugs, or cloth) by interlacing threads on a loom, or creates sculptural objects using fiber.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design textile patterns or artistic compositions.
• Set up ("dress") a complex loom with warp threads.
• Weave weft threads to create the final fabric or artwork.
• May also dye their own yarn.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of loom operation and weaving structures.
• Strong design and color theory skills.
• Patience and attention to detail.
• BFA or MFA in Fiber Arts.

Job Description: A theatrical artist who paints the scenery (flats, backdrops) for a play, opera, or film set, often creating large-scale illusions of texture, place, and perspective.
Key Responsibilities:
• Work from the Set Designer's renderings to paint scenery.
• Use large-scale techniques to create textures (wood grain, brick, marble).
• Paint large backdrops, sometimes requiring cartooning and perspective painting.
• Mix large batches of custom paint colors.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Advanced painting skills (realistic and illusory).
• Ability to work at a very large scale.
• Knowledge of scenic materials and techniques.
• Scenic artist union membership (USA 829) or apprenticeship.

Job Description: An artist who specializes in the visual art of decorative handwriting or lettering, creating elegant scripts for invitations, documents, or branding.
Key Responsibilities:
• Master various calligraphic hands (e.aws, italics, copperplate) using specialized pens and nibs.
• Create custom lettering for wedding invitations, event signage, and certificates.
• Design original logos or tattoo lettering.
• Digitize lettering for commercial use.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional manual dexterity and control.
• Deep knowledge of letterforms, tools, and inks.
• Patience and precision.
• A portfolio of lettering work.

Job Description: A state-licensed educator who develops curriculum and teaches visual arts (drawing, painting, ceramics, art history) to elementary, middle, or high school students.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create and teach lesson plans based on art fundamentals and art history.
• Manage a classroom environment and student behavior.
• Order and manage all art supplies and the art room budget.
• Assess student artwork and development.
• Curate student art shows.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Broad knowledge of multiple art mediums (drawing, painting, ceramics).
• Strong classroom management skills.
• Passion for art and working with children.
• Bachelor's in Art Education and a state teaching license.

Job Description: A high-level educator and practicing artist who teaches art at the college level, mentors students, and is expected to maintain their own professional art career.
Key Responsibilities:
• Teach advanced courses in a specific discipline (e.g., Painting, Sculpture).
• Mentor undergraduate and graduate (MFA) students.
• Maintain an active and prestigious career as a professional artist (exhibitions, grants).
• Serve on university committees and advise students.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery (expert-level) in a specific art discipline.
• A Master of Fine Arts (MFA) degree is the terminal degree required.
• A significant and active exhibition record (CV).
• Teaching experience.

Job Description: A mental health professional who uses the creative process of art-making, combined with psychological theory, to help clients explore feelings, reduce anxiety, and improve mental health.
Key Responsibilities:
• Use art-making as a form of psychotherapy with diverse populations (hospitals, schools, private practice).
• Guide clients through creative exercises to express non-verbal feelings.
• Analyze the art-making process and artwork to provide psychological insight.
• Maintain confidential patient records and treatment plans.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Knowledge of psychology, psychotherapy, and clinical practice.
• Empathy and strong interpersonal skills.
• Proficiency in various art mediums.
• Master's degree in Art Therapy and professional licensure (ATR-BC, LPC).

Job Description: Designs and leads educational programming (tours, workshops, classes) for a museum, making the collection accessible and engaging for diverse audiences (families, schools, adults).
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and lead guided tours and workshops for school groups and adults.
• Develop educational materials (activity guides, lesson plans) related to exhibits.
• Manage outreach programs to local schools.
• Train volunteer tour guides (docents).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong public speaking and teaching skills.
• Knowledge of art history and museum collections.
• Curriculum development skills.
• MA in Museum Education or Art History.

Job Description: A specialized writer who researches and writes grant proposals to foundations, corporations, and government agencies (like the NEA) to secure funding for an arts organization.
Key Responsibilities:
• Research and identify potential grant funding opportunities.
• Write clear, persuasive grant applications and proposals that match the funder's goals.
• Gather all supporting documentation (budgets, project plans).
• Manage grant deadlines and write final reports on how funds were used.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional persuasive writing skills.
• Strong research and project management skills.
• Ability to write detailed budgets.
• Experience in non-profit fundraising.

Job Description: An artist who creates and records custom sound effects in sync with the picture in post-production. They physically recreate sounds like footsteps, rustling cloth, or punches in a studio.
Key Responsibilities:
• Watch a film sequence and identify all human-made sounds needed.
• Use a vast collection of objects ("props") on various surfaces to recreate sounds in real-time.
• Record sounds (like footsteps, door creaks, cloth movement) in perfect synchronization with the actor's movements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Extreme creativity and timing.
• A keen ear for sound and acoustics.
• Knowledge of microphones and recording techniques.
• This is a highly specialized craft learned via apprenticeship.

Job Description: The analytical and creative thinker who defines a company's brand identity. They determine the "why"—the target audience, competitive positioning, and core message of the brand.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct market research, competitive analysis, and stakeholder interviews.
• Define the brand's core identity (mission, vision, values, positioning).
• Develop the brand architecture and naming conventions.
• Create the creative brief that guides all design and marketing work.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills.
• Market research and data analysis.
• Excellent communication and presentation skills.
• Background in marketing, design, or business.

Job Description: A sales professional who buys and sells artworks, acting as an intermediary between artists, collectors, and galleries. They are experts in the art market.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build a network of collectors, museum curators, and artists.
• Acquire artwork (from artists or auctions) and manage inventory.
• "Place" artwork with collectors by making sales presentations.
• Stay current on art market trends and auction results.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert sales and negotiation skills.
• Deep knowledge of art history and the contemporary art market.
• Networking and relationship management.
• Discretion.

Job Description: Oversees the operations of the costume shop. They manage the budget, staff (stitchers, drapers), and schedule to ensure all costumes designed by the Costume Designer are built on time.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze costume designs for budget and labor feasibility.
• Hire and supervise the shop staff (cutters, drapers, stitchers).
• Source and purchase all fabrics, notions, and supplies.
• Manage the costume budget and build schedule for the production.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of garment construction and patternmaking.
• Strong leadership and budget management skills.
• Project management and problem-solving.

Job Description: A master artisan in a costume shop who translates the designer's 2D sketch into a 3D garment by draping fabric on a mannequin and then creating the master pattern.
Key Responsibilities:
• Interpret the costume designer's rendering to create the garment's form.
• Drape fabric on a dress form to create the initial design.
• Draft the master pattern that will be used to cut the fabric.
• Supervise stitchers in the construction of the garment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of patternmaking, draping, and garment construction.
• Knowledge of period costume shapes.
• Precision and translation skills.

Job Description: An artisan who builds and repairs stringed instruments, such as guitars, violins, or cellos.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build new instruments by hand-selecting wood, shaping, and assembling.
• Perform precision repairs, restorations, and "setups" (adjustments) on instruments.
• Apply fine varnishes and finishes.
• Work with musicians to customize their instruments.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert woodworking and craft skills.
• Deep knowledge of acoustics and music.
• Patience and extreme precision.
• Apprenticeship or specialized schooling.

Job Description: A specialized concept artist who designs the worlds, locations, and settings for video games and films. They create the mood, architecture, and lighting of a place.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the look and feel of fictional locations (cities, forests, interiors).
• Create "world maps" and detailed architectural designs.
• Paint "keyframe" illustrations that establish the mood and lighting of a scene.
• Provide visual research and reference for 3D modelers.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Elite digital painting skills (Photoshop).
• Strong understanding of perspective, architecture, and lighting.
• High-level imagination.
• Portfolio demonstrating world-building skills.

Job Description: A highly specialized designer who creates new fonts (typefaces). This involves designing the entire character set, including letters, numbers, and punctuation, and programming the font file.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design a cohesive set of characters for a new typeface.
• Ensure consistency in weight, style, and proportion.
• "Kern" the font (adjust the spacing between every letter pair).
• Program the final font file (OTF, TTF) using specialized software.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Absolute mastery of typography and letterforms.
• Extreme attention to detail.
• Proficiency in font design software (e.g., Glyphs, FontLab).
• A portfolio of typefaces.

Job Description: A hybrid role combining Registrar and Conservator duties, typically at a smaller institution. Responsible for the long-term care, documentation, and storage of all objects in the collection.
Key Responsibilities:
• Oversee the physical storage environment (climate control, pest management).
• Monitor the condition of objects and perform basic preventative conservation.
• Manage the collections database and all records.
• Coordinate object logistics for exhibitions and loans.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Knowledge of preventative conservation and museum standards.
• Meticulous database and administrative skills.
• MA in Museum Studies or Conservation.

Job Description: Manages the public image of a fashion brand. Their primary goal is to secure media coverage (press) and get clothing "placed" on celebrities and influencers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Write press releases for new collections.
• Maintain relationships with fashion editors, bloggers, and stylists.
• Manage the "sample closet," sending garments out for photo shoots.
• Pitch stories to media and coordinate celebrity/influencer dressing.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong network of media contacts in the fashion industry.
• Excellent communication skills.
• Organization (for sample tracking).
• Degree in PR or Fashion Communications.

Job Description: Captures high-quality images for businesses and brands for use in advertising, marketing, and e-commerce.
Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with Art Directors and clients on a creative brief.
• Set up and manage complex studio lighting.
• Photograph products, models, or food to look appealing.
• Perform high-end digital retouching and color correction.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of camera operation and studio lighting.
• Expertise in Photoshop and Capture One.
• Client management skills.
• A strong commercial portfolio.

Job Description: A journalist who tells news stories through compelling images. They capture real-world events as they happen, adhering to journalistic ethics (no staging).
Key Responsibilities:
• Research and pitch story ideas to publications.
• Travel to locations (sometimes dangerous) to document events.
• Capture images that tell a complete and accurate story.
• Write captions and submit photos to editors on deadline.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong photography skills (working fast in unpredictable conditions).
• Journalistic ethics and integrity.
• Resilience and bravery.
• Degree in Journalism or Photojournalism.

Job Description: The head of the art department for a film. They are responsible for designing and creating the entire physical world of the film, including all sets, locations, and props.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and collaborate with the Director to define the film's overall look.
• Design all sets and manage their construction.
• Oversee the Set Decorator, Props Master, and construction crew.
• Manage the entire art department budget.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong creative vision.
• Knowledge of architecture, art history, and construction.
• Leadership and budget management skills.
• Portfolio of design work; union membership.

Job Description: Works under the Production Designer to select and acquire all the "dressing" (furniture, rugs, curtains, artwork, small details) that make a film set look like a real, lived-in space.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the script and break down all set dressing requirements.
• Source all items by buying or renting from prop houses.
• Place all dressing on the set to bring the environment to life.
• Manage the set dressing budget and crew.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong interior design and storytelling skills.
• Extreme resourcefulness and organization.
• Knowledge of historical periods and design styles.

Job Description: Manages the human resources and logistics for a theatre company or a specific production, focusing on the well-being of the cast and crew.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage artist contracts, payroll, and union paperwork.
• Arrange all travel and housing for out-of-town actors and creatives.
• Serve as the primary liaison for all artist needs and problems.
• Manage the company budget for a specific show.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong organization and problem-solving skills.
• Empathy and interpersonal skills.
• Knowledge of union contracts (e.g., AEA).

Job Description: A general term for a professional who manages the business side of a non-profit arts organization, handling tasks in finance, marketing, development, and operations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist with budgeting and financial tracking.
• Help coordinate marketing campaigns and ticket sales.
• Assist with fundraising events and grant applications.
• Provide administrative support to the leadership team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Organization and multitasking.
• Passion for the arts.
• Strong communication and administrative skills.
• Degree in Arts Administration.

Job Description: A strategic role, often in tech, that combines UX design, UI design, and user research. They are responsible for the entire experience of a digital product, from initial research to final visual design.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct user research to identify product opportunities.
• Define the user flow, wireframes, and prototypes (UX).
• Design the high-fidelity visual interface (UI).
• Continuously test and iterate on the product with users.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of the entire design process (UX/UI).
• Strategic thinking and problem-solving.
• Proficiency in Figma/Sketch.
• Portfolio of product case studies.

Job Description: A highly skilled visual artist who designs and applies permanent tattoos to clients' bodies using specialized needles and ink.
Key Responsibilities:
• Consult with clients to create original, custom tattoo designs.
• Operate tattoo machines and apply ink precisely to the skin.
• Adhere to all health and bloodborne pathogen safety standards strictly.
• Clean and sterilize all equipment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional drawing and artistic skills.
• Knowledge of tattoo equipment and techniques.
• Strict adherence to health/safety protocols.
• Requires a formal apprenticeship and state license.

Job Description: A specialized illustrator who creates the artwork for children's books, interpreting the text to create engaging visual narratives for young readers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the manuscript and create character designs.
• Storyboard the entire book to plan the layout and flow.
• Create final, full-color illustrations for every page.
• Collaborate with the author, editor, and art director.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• A unique, marketable, and family-friendly art style.
• Strong storytelling and character design skills.
• Mastery of a medium (digital or traditional).
• Professional illustration portfolio.

Job Description: A writer who reports on the arts and culture beat, reviewing performances, interviewing artists, and covering cultural events for a publication.
Key Responsibilities:
• Attend and review theatre, gallery openings, concerts, and films.
• Interview artists, directors, curators, and authors.
• Pitch and write long-form feature articles on cultural trends.
• Adhere to journalistic ethics and deadlines.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong critical writing and reporting skills.
• Deep knowledge of the local and national arts scene.
• Interviewing and research skills.
• Degree in Journalism or a related arts field.

Job Description: Applies makeup to actors for theatre, film, and television. This can range from "corrective" makeup for cameras to elaborate fantasy or prosthetic special effects.
Key Responsibilities:
• Apply makeup to actors to create the look of a specific character.
• Read scripts to determine makeup requirements.
• May specialize in special effects (SFX) makeup, applying prosthetics, wounds, or aging effects.
• Maintain continuity of makeup during shooting.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Knowledge of makeup techniques (beauty and corrective).
• (For SFX) Skills in sculpting, mold-making, and prosthetic application.
• State cosmetology or esthetician license often required.

Job Description: A licensed healthcare professional who uses music-based interventions (listening, singing, playing instruments) to accomplish individualized therapeutic goals for patients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess the physical, emotional, and cognitive needs of a client.
• Design music-based interventions to meet therapeutic goals.
• Conduct individual or group therapy sessions.
• Work with diverse populations (hospitals, nursing homes, schools).
• Maintain clinical documentation.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Proficiency in guitar, piano, and voice.
• Knowledge of psychology and clinical therapy practices.
• Empathy.
• Master's degree and board certification (MT-BC).

Job Description: An artist who specializes in the visual art of decorative handwriting or lettering, creating elegant scripts for invitations, documents, or branding.
Key Responsibilities:
• Master various calligraphic hands (e.aws, italics, copperplate) using specialized pens and nibs.
• Create custom lettering for wedding invitations, event signage, and certificates.
• Design original logos or tattoo lettering.
• Digitize lettering for commercial use.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional manual dexterity and control.
• Deep knowledge of letterforms, tools, and inks.
• Patience and precision.
• A portfolio of lettering work.

• Extreme resourcefulness and organization.
• Knowledge of historical periods and design styles.

Job Description: Manages the human resources and logistics for a theatre company or a specific production, focusing on the well-being of the cast and crew.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage artist contracts, payroll, and union paperwork.
• Arrange all travel and housing for out-of-town actors and creatives.
• Serve as the primary liaison for all artist needs and problems.
• Manage the company budget for a specific show.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong organization and problem-solving skills.
• Empathy and interpersonal skills.
• Knowledge of union contracts (e.g., AEA).

Job Description: A general term for a professional who manages the business side of a non-profit arts organization, handling tasks in finance, marketing, development, and operations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist with budgeting and financial tracking.
• Help coordinate marketing campaigns and ticket sales.
• Assist with fundraising events and grant applications.
• Provide administrative support to the leadership team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Organization and multitasking.
• Passion for the arts.
• Strong communication and administrative skills.
• Degree in Arts Administration.

Job Description: A strategic role, often in tech, that combines UX design, UI design, and user research. They are responsible for the entire experience of a digital product, from initial research to final visual design.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct user research to identify product opportunities.
• Define the user flow, wireframes, and prototypes (UX).
• Design the high-fidelity visual interface (UI).
• Continuously test and iterate on the product with users.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of the entire design process (UX/UI).
• Strategic thinking and problem-solving.
• Proficiency in Figma/Sketch.
• Portfolio of product case studies.

Job Description: A highly skilled visual artist who designs and applies permanent tattoos to clients' bodies using specialized needles and ink.
Key Responsibilities:
• Consult with clients to create original, custom tattoo designs.
• Operate tattoo machines and apply ink precisely to the skin.
• Adhere to all health and bloodborne pathogen safety standards strictly.
• Clean and sterilize all equipment.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional drawing and artistic skills.
• Knowledge of tattoo equipment and techniques.
• Strict adherence to health/safety protocols.
• Requires a formal apprenticeship and state license.

Job Description: A specialized illustrator who creates the artwork for children's books, interpreting the text to create engaging visual narratives for young readers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the manuscript and create character designs.
• Storyboard the entire book to plan the layout and flow.
• Create final, full-color illustrations for every page.
• Collaborate with the author, editor, and art director.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• A unique, marketable, and family-friendly art style.
• Strong storytelling and character design skills.
• Mastery of a medium (digital or traditional).
• Professional illustration portfolio.

Job Description: A writer who reports on the arts and culture beat, reviewing performances, interviewing artists, and covering cultural events for a publication.
Key Responsibilities:
• Attend and review theatre, gallery openings, concerts, and films.
• Interview artists, directors, curators, and authors.
• Pitch and write long-form feature articles on cultural trends.
• Adhere to journalistic ethics and deadlines.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Strong critical writing and reporting skills.
• Deep knowledge of the local and national arts scene.
• Interviewing and research skills.
• Degree in Journalism or a related arts field.

Job Description: Applies makeup to actors for theatre, film, and television. This can range from "corrective" makeup for cameras to elaborate fantasy or prosthetic special effects.
Key Responsibilities:
• Apply makeup to actors to create the look of a specific character.
• Read scripts to determine makeup requirements.
• May specialize in special effects (SFX) makeup, applying prosthetics, wounds, or aging effects.
• Maintain continuity of makeup during shooting.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Knowledge of makeup techniques (beauty and corrective).
• (For SFX) Skills in sculpting, mold-making, and prosthetic application.
• State cosmetology or esthetician license often required.

Job Description: A licensed healthcare professional who uses music-based interventions (listening, singing, playing instruments) to accomplish individualized therapeutic goals for patients.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess the physical, emotional, and cognitive needs of a client.
• Design music-based interventions to meet therapeutic goals.
• Conduct individual or group therapy sessions.
• Work with diverse populations (hospitals, nursing homes, schools).
• Maintain clinical documentation.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Proficiency in guitar, piano, and voice.
• Knowledge of psychology and clinical therapy practices.
• Empathy.
• Master's degree and board certification (MT-BC).

Job Description: A highly specialized artist with training in both medicine and art. They create scientifically accurate visuals (drawings, models) for textbooks, journals, and patient education.
Key Responsibilities:
• Collaborate with doctors and scientists to understand complex medical information.
• Create accurate and clear illustrations of human anatomy, surgical procedures, and biological processes.
• Use digital tools (Photoshop, Illustrator) or 3D modeling software.
• Ensure all visuals are scientifically precise.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Master's degree from an accredited Medical Illustration program.
• Expert knowledge of human anatomy.
• Exceptional drawing and digital art skills.
• CMI (Certified Medical Illustrator) certification.

Job Description: A creative professional responsible for writing the text (or "copy") used in advertising and marketing. Their goal is persuasive writing that drives action (e.g., makes a sale, generates a click).
Key Responsibilities:
• Write clear, concise, and persuasive copy for ads (digital, print, TV, radio).
• Create content for websites, email marketing campaigns, and social media.
• Develop and adhere to a specific brand voice and tone.
• Collaborate with art directors and designers to create finished ads.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional persuasive writing, creativity.
• Understanding of marketing and psychology.
• Ability to accept criticism and meet deadlines.
• A strong portfolio of work.

Job Description: Manages the end-to-end creation of a podcast. This role combines creative direction, project management, and technical audio skills.
Key Responsibilities:
• Coordinate with hosts, book guests, and manage production schedules.
• Research topics and assist with scripting or outlining episodes.
• Record, edit, and mix audio to a high professional standard.
• Write show notes, upload episodes to hosting platforms, and assist with promotion.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Audio editing software (Audacity, Pro Tools, Logic Pro).
• Project management, storytelling.
• Communication skills.
• Experience in audio production.

Job Description: A technical artist in 3D animation. They build the digital "skeleton," joints, and control system for a 3D model, allowing animators to move and pose the character.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build the internal armature (skeleton) for a 3D character model.
• Create the user interface (controls) that animators will use.
• Test the "rig" for functionality, weight, and deformation.
• Troubleshoot and fix any issues for the animation team.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Highly technical skill in 3D software (Maya).
• Deep knowledge of scripting (Python, MEL).
• Understanding of anatomy and biomechanics.
• Technical demo reel required.

Job Description: A specialized designer who focuses on creating and managing the visual elements of a brand identity, including the logo, color palette, typography, and image style, ensuring consistency.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design the core brand identity (logo, color, fonts).
• Create a comprehensive brand style guide for the company to follow.
• Apply the brand identity across all assets (website, packaging, marketing).
• Ensure all visuals are cohesive and strategically aligned.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expertise in graphic design and branding strategy.
• Adobe Creative Suite.
• Ability to think systematically and strategically about design.
• Strong branding portfolio.

Job Description: Manages the music library for a major performing ensemble, like a symphony orchestra or opera. They are responsible for acquiring, preparing, and distributing sheet music.
Key Responsibilities:
• Acquire sheet music (purchase or rent) for the season's programming.
• Prepare scores for the conductor (marking) and parts for each musician (bowings).
• Catalog and maintain the physical music library.
• Distribute and collect all sheet music for rehearsals and performances.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Ability to read music fluently.
• Exceptional organization and attention to detail.
• Knowledge of music history and publishing.
• Master's in Library Science (MLS) or Musicology.

Job Description: A highly technical specialist who takes the master pattern (sample size) created by the Pattern Maker and scales it up and down to create the patterns for the full range of sizes (XS, S, M, L, XL, etc.).
Key Responsibilities:
• Use specific grading rules to proportionally increase or decrease pattern measurements.
• Work using CAD software (Gerber, Lectra) or by hand.
• Ensure the fit and proportions remain consistent across all sizes.
• Create the final "nested" set of patterns for the factory.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of pattern grading techniques and rules.
• Advanced math skills and attention to detail.
• Proficiency in grading software (CAD).
• Experience in patternmaking.

Job Description: A certified expert who evaluates artworks to determine their monetary value for insurance, donation, estate tax, or sale purposes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Research the artist, provenance (history of ownership), and condition of an artwork.
• Analyze auction records and private sales data for comparable works.
• Write a formal, legally binding appraisal report stating the artwork's value.
• Adhere to professional ethics standards (USPAP).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of art history and the art market.
• Strong research skills.
• Must be certified by an appraisal organization (ASA, AAA, ISA).

Job Description: An artisan who forges and shapes metal (typically iron and steel) using a forge, hammer, and anvil to create functional items (tools, hardware, gates) or sculpture.
Key Responsibilities:
• Heat metal in a forge to make it malleable.
• Shape hot metal using hammers, anvils, and other metalworking tools.
• Use techniques like welding, bending, and finishing.
• Fabricate custom commissions (e.g., architectural railings).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of metallurgy and forging techniques.
• Physical strength and stamina.
• High tolerance for heat and dangerous conditions.
• Apprenticeship or specialized craft schooling.

Job Description: (See "Art Handler") A technician who performs the skilled manual labor of building, installing, and maintaining museum exhibits.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build temporary walls, pedestals, and custom mounts for art.
• Hang and install artwork according to the curator's layout.
• Set up and maintain lighting and A/V equipment for exhibits.
• Paint gallery walls and perform repairs.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Advanced carpentry skills.
• Knowledge of art handling techniques.
• Ability to read blueprints and designs.
• Experience in construction or fabrication.

Job Description: A specialized artisan who designs and paints high-quality signs by hand, using traditional lettering and gilding (gold leaf) techniques.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design lettering and layouts for commercial signage.
• Hand-paint lettering with brushes with extreme precision.
• Apply gold leaf (gilding) to surfaces like glass windows.
• Work on-site to paint large-scale signs on buildings.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Mastery of brush lettering (sign painting).
• Knowledge of layout, design, and typography.
• Extreme patience and a steady hand.
• Apprenticeship-based skill.

Job Description: A specialized MUA who focuses on "practical effects"—creating non-human characters, wounds, aging, and monsters using prosthetics, sculpting, and mold-making.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design characters (e.g., aliens, monsters) with the director.
• Sculpt features in clay and create molds.
• Run foam latex or silicone prosthetic appliances from the molds.
• Apply and paint prosthetics onto the actor, blending them seamlessly with the skin.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert skills in sculpting, painting, and mold-making.
• Deep knowledge of materials (silicone, foam latex).
• Understanding of anatomy.
• Specialized SFX makeup school portfolio.

Job Description: Works with the Lighting Designer to implement the light plot. They are responsible for acquiring, hanging, focusing, and wiring all lighting instruments in the theatre.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read the Lighting Designer's plot and acquire all needed instruments and cables.
• Supervise a crew of electricians to hang all lights in the grid.
• Focus each light according to the designer's specifications.
• Ensure all electrical systems are safe and functional.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Expert knowledge of theatrical lighting instruments.
• Understanding of electrical theory and safety.
• Leadership and ability to read complex plots.
• Certification or BFA/MFA.

Job Description: A specialized illustrator who attends legal trials and quickly draws the proceedings (judge, jury, defendant) for media outlets, particularly when cameras are banned from the courtroom.
Key Responsibilities:
• Attend high-profile court cases.
• Quickly sketch key figures and dramatic moments using pastel or pencil.
• Capture the likeness and emotion of the participants under a tight deadline.
• Deliver sketches to news media for broadcast.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional, fast drawing and sketching skills.
• Ability to capture likeness and gesture quickly.
• Ability to work under high pressure.
• Journalism or illustration background.

Job Description: Creates precise visual illustrations to explain complex technical information. They create the diagrams in instruction manuals, engineering journals, and scientific textbooks.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read and interpret complex technical data or engineering blueprints.
• Create clean, accurate diagrams, cutaways, and "exploded views" of machinery or products.
• Use digital tools (Adobe Illustrator) to create precise vector-based illustrations.
• Work with engineers and writers to ensure accuracy.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Exceptional precision drawing skills (digital).
• Ability to understand and visualize complex technical concepts.
• Mastery of Adobe Illustrator.
• Portfolio of technical diagrams.

Job Description: A highly specialized consultant, typically hired by international publishers or film studios, to be their "eyes and ears" and find promising books in other markets before they become bestsellers.
Key Responsibilities:
• Read manuscripts and new books across a specific market (e.g., the US publishing market).
• Network extensively with agents and editors to get early tips.
• Write reports recommending the most promising books to their international clients.
• Identify trends and breakout intellectual property (IP).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Voracious reader with strong editorial instincts.
• Exceptional networking and social skills.
• Ability to synthesize information and write reports quickly.
• Deep industry connections.

Job Description: Works for a government agency or non-profit to manage the "Percent for Art" program. They facilitate the entire process of commissioning and installing public art.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage the "call for artists" process and RFQ submissions.
• Coordinate the selection committee (jury) process.
• Act as the liaison between the artist, the city/architects, and the community.
• Manage the project budget, timeline, and installation logistics.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Project management skills.
• Knowledge of public art, fabrication, and construction.
• Diplomacy and communication skills.
• MA in Arts Administration or Public Policy.

Job Description: An undergraduate or MBA student in a temporary role. Supports a project team by conducting research, performing data analysis, and learning the fundamentals of the consulting methodology.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct market research and data collection.
• Perform quantitative analysis and financial modeling in Excel.
• Assist in the creation of client presentation slides (PowerPoint).
• Participate in team brainstorming and problem-solving sessions.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Strong analytical thinking, Excel/PowerPoint, communication, teamwork.
• Qualifications: Currently enrolled in a top-tier BA or MBA program.

Job Description: The entry-level, post-undergraduate role. Responsible for a specific "workstream" within a project, focusing heavily on data gathering, quantitative analysis, and slide creation.
Key Responsibilities:
• Own a specific piece of the analysis (e.g., competitor benchmarking, financial model).
• Synthesize research findings into clear insights.
• Prepare and polish client-ready presentation decks.
• Work directly with client data to identify trends.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Data analysis, financial modeling, research, advanced PowerPoint, problem-solving, professionalism.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's degree from a top-tier university (any major).

Job Description: The standard post-MBA role. Expected to manage their own complex workstream, lead analysts, and begin to structure the project's overall narrative and recommendations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Structure and manage complex analytical workstreams.
• Supervise and mentor 1-2 analysts or associates.
• Formulate strategic recommendations based on data.
• Begin to co-manage parts of the client relationship.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Project management, advanced structured thinking, leadership, client management, business acumen.
• Qualifications: MBA from a top-tier business school or direct promotion from an Analyst role.

Job Description: Responsible for the day-to-day management of an entire consulting project. Manages the full team, the client relationship, the project plan, and the final deliverable.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead the project team, delegate work, and manage timelines.
• Act as the primary point of contact for the client project team.
• Structure the team's problem-solving approach and ensure hypothesis-driven analysis.
• Build and present the final recommendation deck to client leadership.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Team leadership, project management, client relationship skills, storytelling, conflict resolution.
• Qualifications: 3-5+ years of post-MBA experience; proven project delivery.

Job Description: A senior leader who manages multiple project teams simultaneously. Begins to shift focus from project delivery to project sales and business development.
Key Responsibilities:
• Manage a portfolio of 2-3 projects and their respective managers.
• Build and maintain relationships with senior client executives.
• Lead business development proposals (RFPs) and sales pitches.
• Develop intellectual capital (white papers) for the firm.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Business development (sales), client relationship management, practice leadership, expert-level knowledge.
• Qualifications: Extensive (8-12+ years) consulting experience; proven track record of selling projects.

Job Description: A co-owner of the firm responsible for setting strategy, driving revenue, and managing the firm's most senior client relationships (C-suite). The ultimate P&L owner.
Key Responsibilities:
• Own and grow a multi-million dollar book of business (client portfolio).
• Sell and sign large-scale transformation projects.
• Serve as the lead relationship manager for C-suite clients.
• Mentor junior partners and principals; lead the practice area.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Executive leadership, rainmaking/sales, C-suite relationship building, strategic vision.
• Qualifications: Elected to the partnership; proven track record of generating significant revenue and firm impact.

Job Description: A specialist consultant who advises clients on how to expand their business, focusing on market entry, product diversification, and new revenue streams.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze market size, competitive landscape, and customer segments.
• Model financial projections for new business ventures.
• Develop strategic roadmaps for entering new markets or launching new products.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Market analysis, financial modeling, strategic forecasting, competitive intelligence.
• Qualifications: Standard consulting background (BA/MBA) with a focus on marketing or corporate strategy projects.

Job Description: Advises clients on improving operational efficiency. Focuses on supply chain, manufacturing, procurement, and cost-reduction initiatives.
Key Responsibilities:
• Diagram and analyze client's core operational processes.
• Identify bottlenecks, waste, and areas for cost reduction.
• Design new operational models (e.g., Lean manufacturing, strategic sourcing).
• Quantify potential savings from efficiency improvements.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Process mapping, Lean/Six Sigma, cost-benefit analysis, supply chain management, procurement.
• Qualifications: BA/MBA; often an engineering or operations background is preferred.

Job Description: A niche consultant who uses economic and data models to help clients determine the optimal price for their products and services to maximize revenue and profitability.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct conjoint analysis and other market research to test price sensitivity.
• Analyze competitor pricing models and market positioning.
• Develop new pricing frameworks (e.g., subscription, tiered, value-based).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Economics, econometrics, statistical analysis (R/Python), strong quantitative modeling.
• Qualifications: Degree in Economics, Statistics, or related field. Often holds a PhD.

Job Description: Advises clients on the feasibility and strategy for entering a new geographic market (e.g., a US company entering Asia).
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze foreign market regulations, cultural barriers, and local competition.
• Evaluate build vs. buy vs. partner entry models.
• Develop a phased roadmap for market entry.
• Assess geopolitical and economic risks.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: International business, risk analysis, regulatory research, competitive strategy.
• Qualifications: BA/MBA, often with international experience or fluency in other languages.

Job Description: Works on the commercial (not financial) side of M&A. Answers the question: "Is this a good company and market to buy?" (Part of Transaction Services).
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct rapid market research on a target company's industry.
• Interview key customers, suppliers, and experts.
• Assess the target's competitive advantage and market position.
• Identify key commercial risks and opportunities for the acquiring client.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Rapid research, expert interviewing, financial analysis, hypothesis generation, deadline-driven.
• Qualifications: Standard consulting background; experience in fast-paced M&A projects.

Job Description: Advises CIOs and CTOs on high-level IT strategy, such as digital transformation roadmaps, technology operating models, and IT cost optimization.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess a client's current technology stack and capabilities.
• Align IT strategy with the overall business strategy.
• Create multi-year roadmaps for digital transformation.
• Design new IT governance and operating models.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Business acumen, IT knowledge, strategic planning, digital trends, communication.
• Qualifications: BA/MBA, often mixed with a technical (CompSci) degree.

Job Description: A broad role focused on helping legacy companies adopt digital technologies. Manages the human and technical side of moving to cloud, mobile, and data-centric operations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Create roadmaps for adopting digital-first customer experiences.
• Manage projects related to cloud migration, data analytics, and mobile app dev.
• Integrate change management to ensure employee adoption.
• Bridge the gap between business leaders and IT implementation teams.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Project management, change management, broad knowledge of cloud/data/Agile, stakeholder alignment.
• Qualifications: BA/MBA with strong project management experience.

Job Description: Advises clients on designing and managing their core hardware, networks, data centers, and infrastructure operations.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess existing data center and network architecture.
• Design scalable infrastructure solutions (on-prem, cloud, or hybrid).
• Plan large-scale hardware refresh or migration projects.
• Advise on network security and disaster recovery planning.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Network architecture, data center operations, cloud infrastructure, ITIL, virtualization (VMware).
• Qualifications: Technical degree or certifications (e.g., CompTIA, Cisco).

Job Description: Advises clients on how to strategically procure technology hardware, software, and services, focusing on cost optimization and vendor management.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze client's annual IT spend and vendor contracts.
• Run the RFP (Request for Proposal) process for new tech vendors.
• Negotiate pricing, licensing terms (SLA), and contracts.
• Advise on outsourcing (BPO/ITO) strategies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Negotiation, procurement, contract analysis, financial analysis, IT asset management (ITAM).
• Qualifications: Background in finance, procurement, or IT operations.

Job Description: A strategist who helps clients develop their "cloud-first" strategy. Determines which applications to move to the cloud, which cloud provider to use (AWS, Azure, GCP), and the financial business case.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build the business case (TCO analysis) for cloud migration.
• Assess application portfolios for "cloud readiness."
• Recommend specific providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) and service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS).
• Develop the migration roadmap and governance model.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Cloud fundamentals (AWS, Azure, GCP), financial modeling (TCO), IT strategy, communication.
• Qualifications: Cloud certifications (e.g., AWS Cloud Practitioner) + consulting background.

Job Description: A hands-on technical expert who actually builds, configures, and deploys solutions on cloud platforms for a client.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and deploy client solutions using AWS, Azure, or GCP services.
• Write Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform or CloudFormation.
• Configure virtual networks, IAM policies, and compute resources.
• Migrate on-premise applications and data to the cloud.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expert-level AWS/Azure/GCP, Terraform/Ansible, scripting (Python), networking, security.
• Qualifications: Professional-level cloud certifications (e.g., AWS Solutions Architect Pro).

Job Description: Advises CISOs and boards on their overall cybersecurity posture, risk management framework, and security program maturity.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct security maturity assessments (e.g., against NIST framework).
• Develop a security program roadmap and governance structure.
• Advise on security budgets and technology investments.
• Create incident response plans and data privacy policies (GDPR, CCPA).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Cybersecurity frameworks (NIST, ISO 27001), risk assessment, data privacy laws, communication.
• Qualifications: Security certs (CISSP, CISM) + consulting background.

Job Description: A tactical consultant who performs technical risk assessments and audits to ensure a client is compliant with security standards.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform vulnerability assessments and penetration tests (ethical hacking).
• Conduct GRC (Governance, Risk, Compliance) audits against standards (PCI, HIPAA, SOX).
• Test and validate internal security controls.
• Write detailed reports on vulnerabilities and remediation steps.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Vulnerability scanning, penetration testing tools, compliance auditing, technical writing.
• Qualifications: Technical certs (CEH, Security+) or auditing certs (CISA).

Job Description: Helps clients leverage their data. Focuses on building business intelligence (BI) dashboards, setting up data visualization tools, and pulling insights from data.
Key Responsibilities:
• Interview business stakeholders to define Key Performance Indicators (KPIs).
• Build and maintain data models and BI dashboards (in Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
• Write complex SQL queries to extract and transform data.
• Train end-users on how to use BI tools for self-service analytics.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Tableau/Power BI (expert-level), SQL, data warehousing, communication, data storytelling.
• Qualifications: Degree in analytics, business, or CS. Strong portfolio of dashboards.

Job Description: A specialist consultant who helps large organizations manage their data as an asset. Focuses on data quality, data catalogs, and policies.
Key Responsibilities:
• Implement data governance frameworks and establish a Data Governance Office.
• Create data catalogs, data dictionaries, and business glossaries.
• Define data quality rules and data stewardship policies.
• Advise on master data management (MDM) strategies.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Data governance principles, data quality tools, MDM concepts, policy writing.
• Qualifications: Experience in data management. Certifications in specific governance tools (e.g., Collibra).

Job Description: Advises clients on how to leverage Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning. Identifies high-value use cases and builds the strategic roadmap for implementation.
Key Responsibilities:
• Run "art of the possible" workshops to identify AI use cases.
• Build the business case and ROI models for AI projects.
• Develop an AI/ML implementation roadmap, including data and talent strategy.
• Advise on AI ethics, governance, and model risk management.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Strong understanding of AI/ML concepts (not necessarily a builder), strategic planning, business acumen.
• Qualifications: MBA or data science background with strong communication skills.

Job Description: A hands-on builder (Data Scientist) who works on client projects to build, test, and deploy machine learning models to solve specific business problems.
Key Responsibilities:
• Clean and prepare large, messy datasets for modeling.
• Train, test, and validate predictive models (regression, classification, etc.).
• Use Python (scikit-learn, pandas) or R for statistical analysis.
• Present model findings and limitations to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Python/R, SQL, machine learning, statistics, data visualization, communication.
• Qualifications: Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (CompSci, Statistics, Econ).

Job Description: Advises clients on how to adopt DevOps culture and practices (like CI/CD and automation) to release software faster and more reliably.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess a client's current software development lifecycle (SDLC).
• Design and implement CI/CD pipelines (e.g., using Jenkins, GitLab CI).
• Implement automation tools for infrastructure provisioning (IaC).
• Coach development and operations teams on Agile/DevOps collaboration.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: CI/CD tools, containerization (Docker, Kubernetes), IaC (Terraform), Agile/Scrum.
• Qualifications: Background in software development or systems administration.

Job Description: An expert in Agile methodologies hired by organizations to train teams, manage the transition from Waterfall to Agile, and act as a Scrum Master for critical projects.
Key Responsibilities:
• Train client teams on Agile principles and the Scrum framework.
• Act as a Scrum Master or Agile Coach for multiple development teams.
• Help organizations scale Agile (e.g., using SAFe, LeSS).
• Facilitate Agile ceremonies (sprints, retrospectives, stand-ups).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expert knowledge of Agile/Scrum/Kanban, coaching, facilitation, conflict resolution.
• Qualifications: Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or SAFe certification; extensive project management experience.

Job Description: An expert in a specific SAP "module" (like Finance, Supply Chain, or HR) who helps clients configure the system to match their business processes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct workshops to gather business requirements from client stakeholders.
• Configure a specific SAP module (e.g., FI/CO, SD, MM) to meet requirements.
• Write functional specification documents for developers.
• Test the system and train end-users.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep expertise in a specific SAP module, business process mapping, communication.
• Qualifications: SAP certification in the relevant module; multiple project lifecycle experience.

Job Description: A software developer who specializes in customizing the SAP environment by writing custom code in SAP's proprietary language, ABAP.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop custom reports, interfaces, conversions, and enhancements (RICEFW).
• Write custom ABAP code to meet functional specifications.
• Perform performance tuning and debugging of SAP programs.
• Manage data migration and system integrations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: ABAP programming (expert), SAP architecture, data integration (IDocs).
• Qualifications: CompSci degree and extensive ABAP development experience.

Job Description: Helps clients implement and customize the Salesforce.com platform (Sales Cloud, Service Cloud) to meet their sales and customer service needs.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead workshops to understand and map the client's sales/service process.
• Configure the Salesforce platform using clicks (not code): objects, fields, workflows, and validation rules.
• Build reports and dashboards for the sales team.
• Train administrators and end-users on the new system.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep Salesforce platform knowledge, business analysis, sales process, communication.
• Qualifications: Salesforce Administrator certification is required. Sales Cloud or Service Cloud Consultant certs preferred.

Job Description: A technical consultant who builds custom functionality on the Salesforce platform using proprietary code (Apex) and frameworks (Lightning Web Components).
Key Responsibilities:
• Build custom Apex code (triggers, controllers) and Lightning Components.
• Integrate Salesforce with external systems using APIs (REST, SOAP).
• Handle complex data migration and system customizations.
• Write technical documentation and perform unit testing.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Apex, SOQL, JavaScript (LWC), API integration.
• Qualifications: Salesforce Platform Developer I and II certifications.

Job Description: A specialist in the Workday platform who helps clients implement and manage their HRIS (HR) and Financial systems, specializing in modules like HCM, Payroll, or Finance.
Key Responsibilities:
• Gather client requirements for HR or Finance processes.
• Configure the Workday tenant (business processes, security, compensation).
• Build custom reports and integrations.
• Manage the testing cycles and train the client's HR/Finance teams.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep Workday module expertise (e.g., HCM Core), reporting, business process logic.
• Qualifications: Workday Pro Certification in a specific module (must be sponsored by a partner firm).

Job Description: An implementation expert who helps clients migrate from on-premise systems (like E-Business Suite) or competitors to Oracle's cloud ERP or HCM applications.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead implementation of Oracle Cloud ERP (Financials) or HCM modules.
• Configure modules to client specifications.
• Manage data migration from legacy systems.
• Build reports (OTBI, BI Publisher) and integrations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Oracle Cloud expertise, knowledge of finance or HR processes, data migration.
• Qualifications: Oracle Cloud certification in relevant modules.

Job Description: Helps clients implement the ServiceNow platform to manage their IT Service Management (ITSM), IT Operations (ITOM), or other business workflows.
Key Responsibilities:
• Gather requirements and configure ServiceNow ITSM modules (Incident, Problem, Change).
• Design and build custom workflows, forms, and service catalogs.
• Integrate ServiceNow with other client IT tools.
• Train the client's IT support staff on using the platform.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: ServiceNow platform knowledge, ITSM/ITIL processes, JavaScript, system administration.
• Qualifications: ServiceNow Certified System Administrator (CSA); implementation specialist certs.

Job Description: An expert in Microsoft's suite of business applications (Finance, Supply Chain, or Customer Engagement/CRM) who manages implementation projects.
Key Responsibilities:
• Implement and configure Dynamics 365 modules.
• Customize the platform and build workflows using Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate).
• Integrate D365 with other Microsoft ecosystem products (Office 365, Azure).
• Manage data migration and user training.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Dynamics 365, Power Platform, business analysis (Finance or CRM).
• Qualifications: Relevant Microsoft certifications (e.g., PL-200, MB-300).

Job Description: An entry-level role in the M&A consulting practice. Supports the team performing financial due diligence on companies being bought or sold.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assist with Quality of Earnings (QofE) analysis by analyzing general ledgers.
• Identify non-recurring or unusual financial items.
• Analyze company cash flow, debt, and net working capital.
• Help prepare the final due diligence report for the client (PE firm or corporation).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced accounting (GAAP/IFRS), strong Excel/data analysis, financial modeling.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's in Accounting or Finance; CPA is highly preferred.

Job Description: A specialist who determines the economic value of a business or asset. This is used for M&A, financial reporting (goodwill testing), or litigation.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform complex financial modeling (Discounted Cash Flow, comparable company analysis).
• Determine the value of intangible assets (patents, trademarks).
• Write detailed valuation reports defending the analysis and conclusion.
• Value complex securities like derivatives or stock options.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Expert financial modeling, advanced accounting, valuation theory, technical writing.
• Qualifications: Finance/Acct. degree; progress towards CFA, CPA, or ABV (Accredited in Business Valuation) credential.

Job Description: An investigative accountant hired to find evidence of financial misconduct. Works on fraud investigations, litigation disputes, and regulatory probes.
Key Responsibilities:
• Investigate alleged fraud, embezzlement, or bribery within a company.
• Conduct data analysis to trace illicit funds and identify patterns.
• Prepare forensic reports and calculate economic damages for litigation.
• May serve as an expert witness in court.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Auditing, investigative mindset, data analysis tools, discretion, knowledge of legal procedures.
• Qualifications: CPA + Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) certification.

Job Description: A specialist who assists lawyers with the financial aspects of a legal case, such as calculating damages, analyzing financial evidence, or serving as an expert witness.
Key Responsibilities:
• Calculate economic damages in breach of contract or intellectual property disputes.
• Analyze financial data and present findings to the legal team.
• Prepare expert reports and testify in depositions or at trial.
• Rebut the analysis of the opposing side's financial expert.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Financial analysis, communication, ability to simplify complex topics, poise under pressure.
• Qualifications: CPA, CFA, or CFE. Experience in legal settings.

Job Description: Advises companies that are in severe financial distress (near or in bankruptcy). Helps them restructure debt, sell assets, and create a viable plan to emerge from bankruptcy.
Key Responsibilities:
• Advise distressed companies on liquidity, cash flow forecasts, and bankruptcy filings.
• Negotiate with creditors and lenders on behalf of the client.
• Develop the "plan of reorganization" to exit Chapter 11 bankruptcy.
• Manage the process of selling off distressed assets.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep corporate finance, bankruptcy law knowledge, cash flow modeling, negotiation.
• Qualifications: Finance/Accounting degree; often a CIRA (Certified Insolvency & Restructuring Advisor).

Job Description: Advises banks and financial institutions on how to measure, model, and manage their financial risks, such as credit risk, market risk, and liquidity risk.
Key Responsibilities:
• Help banks develop models to quantify market and credit risk (e.g., VaR models).
• Advise clients on financial regulations (e.g., Basel III, Dodd-Frank).
• Design and validate risk models used for capital calculations.
• Conduct stress testing on investment portfolios.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Quantitative analysis, financial modeling, statistics, knowledge of financial instruments and regulations.
• Qualifications: Master's in Finance or Quant. field; FRM or CFA certification highly valued.

Job Description: A quantitative specialist who builds statistical models for banks to predict the probability of a borrower defaulting on a loan.
Key Responsibilities:
• Build, test, and validate credit risk models (PD, LGD, EAD).
• Work with large datasets of loan performance.
• Ensure models are compliant with banking regulations (CECL, Basel).
• Use Python, R, or SAS for statistical modeling.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Statistics, Python/R/SAS, econometrics, machine learning, banking regulations.
• Qualifications: Master's or PhD in Statistics, Economics, or a quantitative field.

Job Description: Hired by public companies to help them design, document, and test their internal controls over financial reporting to comply with the Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX).
Key Responsibilities:
• Document key financial processes and identify financial risks.
• Design internal controls (e.g., reviews, approvals, reconciliations) to mitigate risks.
• Perform annual testing of controls to ensure they are operating effectively.
• Work with external auditors during their SOX audit.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Auditing, accounting, process mapping (Visio), risk assessment, SOX regulations.
• Qualifications: CPA or CIA (Certified Internal Auditor). Often ex-auditors.

Job Description: A consultant who helps banks and financial institutions set up and improve their Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Know Your Customer (KYC) programs to prevent financial crime.
Key Responsibilities:
• Review and enhance client KYC policies and customer onboarding procedures.
• Tune and optimize transaction monitoring software to detect suspicious activity.
• Conduct investigations into complex money laundering alerts.
• Help banks remediate regulatory findings (e.g., from the OCC or FinCEN).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Knowledge of AML laws (Bank Secrecy Act), investigative skills, financial analysis.
• Qualifications: CAMS (Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialist) certification is the industry standard.

Job Description: An actuary working at a consulting firm (vs. an insurance co.) who advises clients on pricing insurance products, valuing reserves, or managing risk.
Key Responsibilities:
• Advise insurance clients on pricing strategies for new policies (auto, home, life).
• Perform actuarial valuations of insurance liabilities (reserves).
• Model catastrophic risk (hurricanes, earthquakes).
• Assist with insurance-related M&A or new regulations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced math/statistics, actuarial modeling, risk analysis, strong communication.
• Qualifications: Bachelor's in Actuarial Science. Must be passing exams toward ASA/FSA designation.

Job Description: An actuarial consultant who advises corporations or governments on their defined benefit pension plans, calculating long-term liabilities and required annual funding.
Key Responsibilities:
• Perform annual actuarial valuations of pension plan assets and liabilities.
• Calculate required employer contributions based on funding models.
• Model the impact of plan design changes or market fluctuations.
• Ensure plan compliance with ERISA and other regulations.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Advanced math, financial modeling, knowledge of pension regulations (ERISA).
• Qualifications: Passing exams toward ASA (Associate) or FSA (Fellow) of the Society of Actuaries.

Job Description: A strategic consultant who helps CHROs redesign the entire HR function, including its operating model, technology (HRIS), and processes, to better align with business goals.
Key Responsibilities:
• Assess the maturity and effectiveness of a client's HR function.
• Design a new HR operating model (e.g., implementing HR Business Partners, Centers of Excellence).
• Develop the strategy and business case for a new HRIS (like Workday).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: HR strategy, operating model design, business process reengineering, change management.
• Qualifications: Consulting background with a focus on HR; experience with HRIS platforms.

Job Description: Advises clients on how to design and manage their employee compensation programs. This can be for broad-based employees or specialized executive compensation.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct salary market-benchmarking studies.
• Design base salary structures, bonus plans, and sales commission plans.
• (If Executive Comp): Structure long-term incentive (stock option) plans for senior leaders.
• Ensure pay practices are fair, competitive, and legally compliant.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Data analysis, statistics, financial modeling, knowledge of compensation trends and laws.
• Qualifications: Degree in HR, Finance, or Econ. CCP (Certified Compensation Professional) is a plus.

Job Description: Works at a large brokerage/consulting firm (like Mercer or Aon) advising companies on their employee health and welfare (medical, dental, life) benefits strategy.
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze client's healthcare spending and claim data.
• Advise on benefits plan design to control costs (e.g., PPO vs. HDHP).
• Manage the RFP process to bid out insurance carrier contracts.
• Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations (ACA, ERISA).
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Deep knowledge of healthcare/insurance industry, financial analysis, negotiation, regulations.
• Qualifications: Health & Life insurance license. Often has a finance or data background.

Job Description: Advises companies on their employee retirement plans, primarily 401(k) or 403(b) defined contribution plans. (Different from the actuarial pension role).
Key Responsibilities:
• Help clients design their 401(k) plan (e.g., matching formula, auto-enrollment).
• Conduct vendor searches for 401(k) recordkeepers (e.g., Fidelity, Vanguard).
• Serve as a 3(21) or 3(38) fiduciary, helping select and monitor the plan's investment fund lineup.
• Educate employee investment committees on their fiduciary duties.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Investment knowledge, retirement plan regulations (ERISA), fiduciary process, sales.
• Qualifications: Finance degree; Series 7 and 65/66 licenses; CFA or CFP designations.

Job Description: Helps companies restructure their organization (the "org chart") to improve efficiency, reduce costs, or align with a new strategy (common after a merger).
Key Responsibilities:
• Analyze the current organizational structure, roles, and responsibilities.
• Design new structures (e.g., functional vs. divisional vs. matrix).
• Define roles, levels, and reporting lines for the new organization.
• Plan the implementation and communication of the new structure.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: Operating model design, systems thinking, HR strategy, change management.
• Qualifications: MBA or Master's in Organizational Development/Psychology.

Job Description: Advises clients on how to manage their "human capital," focusing on performance management, leadership development, and succession planning.
Key Responsibilities:
• Design and implement new performance management processes (e.g., moving away from annual reviews).
• Create career pathing frameworks and competency models.
• Develop leadership development programs for high-potential employees.
• Design the company's formal succession planning process for key roles.
Required Skills & Qualifications:
• Skills: HR strategy, organizational psychology, talent assessment, instructional design.
• Qualifications: Master's in I/O Psychology or Organizational Development; HR experience.

Job Description: Focuses on the "people side" of a major business change (like a new technology, merger, or re-org). Their goal is to get employees to buy into and adopt the change successfully.
Key Responsibilities:
• Conduct stakeholder analysis to identify groups impacted by a change.
• Develop a comprehensive communication plan.
• Design training programs for new processes or technologies.
• Identify and manage "resistance" to the change; measure adoption rates.
